# Sardis Canvases — Full Text Dump

This file is the concatenated plain-text content of every canvas at canvas.sardis.sh.
Safe to paste into any large-context AI (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) for summarization,
Q&A, or architecture review.

Canvases are ordered as they appear in the sidebar.

=====================================
# MANIFESTO
https://canvas.sardis.sh/manifesto
=====================================

Canvas title="The missing substrate for trustworthy agents" currentId="manifesto"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment
div class="manifesto-mobile-progress" data-manifesto-mobile-progress
div class="manifesto-mobile-progress-fill" data-manifesto-progress-fill

div class="manifesto-layout"
article class="manifesto" data-manifesto-article

header
span class="eyebrow"Manifesto · April 2026/span

## 
The missing substrate for trustworthy agents.

div class="byline"
spanEfe Baran Durmaz/span
span aria-hidden="true"·/span
span20, writing from Ankara/span

p class="lede"
The simplest way I can say it: I think AI agents are being held back,
not by models, but by the fact that they cannot be trusted with money.
The honest version of that sentence is longer. Here is the longer one.

p
AI agents do not just have a capability problem. They have a trust
problem.

p
A year ago I started thinking about agent autonomy the way an infra
person thinks about it. What is the actual, structural thing stopping
this from working in production. Models kept getting better every
month. Tool calling kept getting better. Browser use kept getting
better. And yet every agent demo I watched ended at the same wall: the
moment it needed to move money, a human had to step in. The agent could
reason about a purchase, negotiate a rate, draft a contract, book a
table. It could not actually pay for any of it without a credential
somebody had handed it by hand.

p
That bottleneck looked small from far away. The closer I looked, the
more I thought it was the thing holding back a huge chunk of AI's
economic value. An agent that reasons but cannot transact is a very
expensive draft. The decision matters only if it lands.

p
So I started where most people start. A payment rail wrapper. Something
thin. Give an agent a way to move stablecoins, plug in an on-ramp and
an off-ramp, call it a day. I was going to be done in a weekend.

## What changed my mind

p
I never used my own card for any of this. I knew from day one that was
the wrong thing to do. But a lot of people did, and are still doing it.
Around the same time I started, OpenAI pushed
a href="https://openai.com/index/buy-it-in-chatgpt/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"Buy it in ChatGPT/a
and the whole agentic commerce pitch started getting louder everywhere.
That was the signal that the wall was about to get hit. A lot.

p
I did not have to wait long to see what that looked like in practice.
In February, an OpenAI engineer's
a href="https://www.kucoin.com/news/flash/openclaw-ai-agent-incident-causes-millions-in-user-losses" target="_blank" rel="noopener"OpenClaw-based trading agent/a
got a simple request ("send 4 SOL for medical expenses") and, through
a quantity-parsing slip, sent 52.43 million tokens worth something
like strong$600K/strong. Nothing was wrong with the model. Nothing
was wrong with the request. The failure was at the layer below: the
agent had the authority to move everything, and a small misread was
enough to trigger it. Mastercard eventually put out a
a href="https://www.mastercard.com/us/en/news-and-trends/stories/2026/openclaw-ai-security-standards.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener"post about OpenClaw and the urgent need for AI security standards/a.
This is a category now, not an incident.

p
So I moved up a level. Forget wrapping card rails. Build something
agent-shaped: prepaid wallets per agent, on-ramp in, off-ramp out,
spend caps, kill switches. Better. Much better than a card. The agent
could no longer drain the principal's account because the principal's
account was not connected. I spent a good chunk of autumn on that.

p
And it was still not enough.

p
A prepaid wallet solves the blast-radius problem and not the
authorization problem. An agent with a funded wallet can still loop.
Can still get prompt-injected. Can still misread a quantity and spend
everything in the wallet on one transaction. The wallet shrinks the
damage; it does not prevent the class of bug. And the second you cap
the wallet, you have given up the very autonomy you were trying to
enable. The agent is no longer making real economic decisions, it is
just drawing from a small pot you pre-approved.

p class="pull"
What I wanted was an agent that could act autonomously without me
having to pre-decide every decision. What I had built was a leash
with a slightly longer rope.

## Why payments were the sharp edge

p
It is worth being specific, because the shape is not obvious and the
existing infra assumes something very different.

p
A human makes maybe a dozen deliberate purchases a day. An agent
running a single task can make hundreds. A procurement agent polling
five suppliers, a research agent hitting paid APIs per query, an infra
agent provisioning cloud resources on demand, a trading agent reacting
to ticks. All of these look like payment floods compared to human
usage. Current estimates put agent-driven transactions at
strong10x to 1000x human frequency/strong, with per-transaction
amounts often in the cent-to-dollar range rather than tens-to-thousands.
Card rails at 2.9% + $0.30 per charge are mathematically dead at this
density. ACH at 1 to 3 business days is actually dead, the decision
and the settlement live in different economic eras.

p
What the rails have to carry is a different shape entirely:
sub-second authorization, marginal fees close to zero on
high-frequency flows, deterministic finality for small amounts, and
escrow semantics when the stakes rise. Nobody is shipping all of that
cleanly today. That is why Sardis treats the
a href="/dual-rail"internal ledger as the primary rail/a
and external rails as on/off-ramps. PayPal's shape, applied to
machine commerce.

## The git shape I could not stop thinking about

p
Around November I started noticing something that felt important. AI
adoption inside software is enormous. Engineers give agents write
access to their whole codebase and walk away. Trillion-dollar-adjacent
industries, finance, healthcare, legal, procurement, are much more
cautious. Orders of magnitude more cautious.

p
The thing that makes software different is git. Every change is a
commit. Every commit is reversible. Every merge is reviewable. Blame
goes down to the line. If an agent ships something bad, you revert it.
You know exactly what happened and exactly who did it, even when the
"who" is a program.

p
Outside of software, there is no git. An agent that pays a vendor
does not leave a diff. An agent that signs a contract does not leave
a rollback path. An agent that moves inventory does not leave a
three-way merge. The reason AI has not flooded into these industries
is not model capability. It is that the substrate does not let you
undo.

p
This was the point where I stopped thinking of Sardis as a payments
company. Payments were just the sharpest place to see the real
problem. The real problem was never how agents move money. It was how
non-human actors do irreversible things without requiring blind trust.
The general shape is: how does a non-human actor do emany/em
consequential thing and leave evidence behind that makes the action
safe to allow in the first place.

## What the thesis actually demands

p
Once I stopped thinking about this as "agent payments," the same set
of missing primitives kept reappearing everywhere.

p
Trustworthy agents need reviewable state transitions instead of opaque
side effects. They need attributable identity instead of "some process
somewhere did this." They need approvals as first-class transitions,
not ad hoc modal dialogs or settings-page toggles. They need evidence
that survives the action. They need delegation and authority to be
explicit rather than inferred after the fact.

p
And they need all of those things to compose. That is the part people
underestimate. It is not enough to have one protocol for tool use,
another for communication, another for payments, another for identity,
and another for approvals if the shared semantics between them stay
implicit. That is where trustworthy systems break back into product
glue.

p
Agent systems do not just need more APIs. They need a shared trust
layer.

p class="aside"
The names of some of the systems I have built around these primitives
are in the references. The point here is not the portfolio. The point
is the shape of the missing substrate.

## Where Sardis sits in all this

p
Sardis is what you build when you realize money is just the most
high-stakes version of an irreversible agent action, and the primitive
underneath has to be better than "give the agent a credential and
hope."

p
Sardis is not a card wrapper. It is not a prettier dashboard for
agent spend. It is not just a prepaid wallet with stricter limits.

p
A payment, in the model I ended up with, is a emone-time object/em.
It exists for a single transaction. It is minted when needed, expires
in minutes, signed by three parties (principal, issuer, agent), bound
to a specific merchant session, drawn from a specific pool of funds.
You cannot steal it and use it elsewhere. You cannot replay it. You
cannot hold it and spend it next week.

p
Underneath the object is a emmandate/em: a signed statement of
exactly what the agent is allowed to do. Merchants, categories,
amounts per transaction, amounts per period, approval thresholds,
currency, rails. Between the mandate and the payment is a policy
engine that lives on the execution path, not in a settings page.
Every payment that crosses the system, from any SDK, any framework,
any protocol, runs through the same twelve checks in the same order.
There is no fast lane that bypasses enforcement, because every fast
lane I ever let myself build became the bug that mattered.

p
The rest of this site is the spec. If you want the details, start at
a href="/protocol-objects"Protocol Objects/a.

## Why the layer underneath cannot be owned

p
AI agents are starting to get some of their own primitives:
identity, communication, tool use, payments, verification, versioned
state. That is good. What is still missing is the layer where these
primitives meet. Right now the shared semantics between them are still
mostly implicit, hidden inside product-specific assumptions and glue
code.

p class="pull"
The missing layer is not another app. It is shared trust semantics.

p
That is part of why I started writing
a href="https://github.com/EfeDurmaz16/oaps" target="_blank" rel="noopener"OAPS/a,
an open standard for agentic primitives: intent, authority, approval,
delegation, and evidence, expressed in a form other systems can
verify, enforce, and build against. That missing layer is not a
detail. It is the control surface. Without it, every company ends up
with its own private dialect for trust. The words sound similar. The
guarantees are not.

p
Without open semantics for authority, agent ecosystems drift into
private dialects.

p
In parallel, I have been shipping
a href="https://github.com/EfeDurmaz16/osp" target="_blank" rel="noopener"OSP/a,
an open protocol for agent-to-service discovery and provisioning.
OAPS and OSP are not where the money is. They are where
interoperability either survives or dies.

p class="pull"
OAPS is the standard. Sardis is the first production implementation
of the payment half of it.

p
Products should compete on distribution, rails, compliance, developer
experience, and regulated posture. But the validation primitive
underneath consequential agent action should be open, inspectable,
and portable. If the layer that decides what an agent is allowed to
do is proprietary, then trust is not infrastructure. It is rented
software. A payment authorization layer under a single company's
control is a layer that can be deprecated, repriced, bought, or
quietly changed. Plumbing does not benefit from brand loyalty.

p
This is the one thing I am not willing to trade. Everything else is
negotiable.

## What I think happens next

p
Agent payments is going to consolidate into a small number of serious
product companies with real distribution, real bank partnerships, and
real regulatory posture. They will do things I am not good at and
should not pretend to be good at.

p
What I want is for the primitive underneath all of them to be the
right shape. One-time payment objects. Signed mandates. Policy on the
path. Verifiable by anyone. Owned by no one. If that turns out to be
the substrate the ecosystem settles on, I do not care whose logo is
in the dashboard. I care that three years from now, when an agent I
wrote is spending real money on my behalf while I am asleep, the
primitive it is using is one I would have agreed to if I had been
awake.

p
That is why I am still building. If you are anywhere near this
problem, tell me about it.
a href="mailto:efe@sardis.sh"efe@sardis.sh/a. I would rather
compare notes than build parallel versions of the same thing.

div class="sources"
p style="font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size:11px; letter-spacing:0.1em; text-transform:uppercase; color:var(--text-muted); margin-bottom:10px;"References

p style="font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-muted); margin-top: 40px;"
Efe Baran Durmaz · April 2026

· a href="/"canvases/a

aside class="manifesto-rail" aria-label="Manifesto sections" data-manifesto-rail
div class="manifesto-rail-track" aria-hidden="true"
div class="manifesto-rail-fill" data-manifesto-progress-fill

ol class="manifesto-rail-list" data-manifesto-sections/ol

script is:inline
(() = {
function slugify(text) {
return text.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "-").replace(/^-+|-+$/g, "");
}

function initManifestoRail() {
const article = document.querySelector("[data-manifesto-article]");
const rail = document.querySelector("[data-manifesto-rail]");
const list = document.querySelector("[data-manifesto-sections]");
const fills = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("[data-manifesto-progress-fill]"));
if (!article || !rail || !list || !fills.length) return;

const headings = Array.from(article.querySelectorAll("h2"));
if (!headings.length) return;

list.innerHTML = "";
const links = headings.map((heading, index) = {
if (!heading.id) heading.id = slugify(heading.textContent || `section-${index + 1}`);
const item = document.createElement("li");
const link = document.createElement("a");
link.className = "manifesto-rail-link";
link.href = `#${heading.id}`;
link.textContent = heading.textContent || "";
item.appendChild(link);
list.appendChild(item);
return link;
});

const setProgress = () = {
const articleRect = article.getBoundingClientRect();
const start = window.scrollY + articleRect.top - 96;
const end = start + article.offsetHeight - window.innerHeight * 0.6;
const raw = (window.scrollY - start) / Math.max(end - start, 1);
const progress = Math.max(0, Math.min(raw, 1));
const percent = `${progress * 100}%`;
fills.forEach((fill) = {
fill.style.width = percent;
fill.style.height = percent;
});

const current = headings.findLast((heading) = heading.getBoundingClientRect().top = 140) || headings[0];
links.forEach((link) = {
link.classList.toggle("is-active", link.getAttribute("href") === `#${current.id}`);
});

const mobileBar = document.querySelector("[data-manifesto-mobile-progress]");
if (mobileBar) {
mobileBar.classList.toggle("is-visible", window.innerWidth = 1080);
}
};

setProgress();
window.addEventListener("scroll", setProgress, { passive: true });
window.addEventListener("resize", setProgress);
}

document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", initManifestoRail);
document.addEventListener("astro:page-load", initManifestoRail);
})();
/script
/Canvas


=====================================
# ARCHITECTURE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/architecture
=====================================

Canvas title="01 · Architecture Overview — Sardis Canvas" currentId="architecture"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 01 · System/span

## Architecture Overview

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis is a seven-layer stack. An AI agent enters at the top; real money moves at the bottom.
Every request traverses the same spine: identity, mandate, policy, orchestration, execution,
settlement, audit. This canvas shows that spine and why each layer exists.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
AI agents can reason, but they cannot be trusted with money. Sardis inserts a verifiable control
plane between an agent's decision and a bank rail — non-custodial MPC wallets below, natural-language
spending policies above, an append-only audit trail on the side. Live on Base and Tempo mainnet.

!-- Core principles --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Principles

## Five commitments the architecture is shaped around.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
These aren't slogans; each one forces a specific structural choice. Change a principle and the stack
reshapes around it.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Non-custodial first

div class="card-description"
Private keys never live in Sardis memory. All signing goes through an MPC provider
(Turnkey default, Fireblocks/Circle/Lit pluggable). strongWhy it matters:/strong an agent
compromised through prompt injection cannot exfiltrate funds because there is no key to steal.
A platform breach limits blast radius to metadata, not movement.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Policy before execution

div class="card-description"
Every transaction passes through codePaymentOrchestrator/code →
codePreExecutionPipeline/code (policy + KYC/AML + mandate + rate limits + anomaly).
strongWhy it matters:/strong there is no "private fast path" — wallets, mandates, ap2
routers all funnel through the same gate, so one hardening improves safety everywhere.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Fail-closed by default

div class="card-description"
Compliance failure, policy failure, unreachable KYA/FIDES service — all default to deny.
strongWhy it matters:/strong agents run unattended; "when in doubt, allow" is how you
wake up to a regulator letter. The override flag (codeSARDIS_AGIT_FAIL_OPEN=true/code)
exists for local dev only.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Audit everything, anchor it on-chain

div class="card-description"
Every decision writes a ledger entry; batches are Merkle-rooted and anchored via
codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code. strongWhy it matters:/strong agent disputes resolve to
cryptographic evidence, not logs that could have been edited.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Provider abstraction

div class="card-description"
Every external service — MPC, KYC, AML, card issuer, fiat rail, PSP — sits behind an
interface in codepackages/sardis-core/code. strongWhy it matters:/strong Didit
replaced Persona without touching business logic; Lithic can replace Stripe Issuing when
funding requirements allow. Swap cost stays low, lock-in stays zero.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Open-core moat

div class="card-description"
Framework SDKs, protocol verifiers, reference contracts — open source.
Policy engine, trust graph, enterprise compliance — proprietary.
strongWhy it matters:/strong distribution goes viral through SDKs; revenue defends
through the parts regulated entities actually pay for.

!-- Layered view --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The Spine

## Seven layers — top to bottom.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Read top-to-bottom for a request's journey; bottom-to-top for a money movement's journey.
Canvas a href="/payment-flow"07 · Payment Flow/a walks one request through every box.

div class="layer-stack"

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 1 · Agent Surface

div class="layer-title"AI framework SDKs + MCP + CLI

div class="layer-desc"
Where the agent code actually lives. Thin adapters that expose Sardis primitives (pay,
policy check, wallet balance) as native tools in each framework. Zero crypto concepts leak up.

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"sardis-langchain/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-crewai/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-openai-agents/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-agent-sdk/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-adk/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-agentkit/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-mcp-server/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-cli/span
span class="layer-chip"+ 10 more/span

div class="arrow-down"↓ HTTPS / JSON

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 2 · API Gateway

div class="layer-title"FastAPI · 123 routers · auth + rate limit + CSP

div class="layer-desc"
Composition root: codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/main.py/code. Every router is grouped
by domain (auth, wallets, payments, policy, checkout, compliance, ops). CSP/HSTS/CORS enforced
at middleware; principal extraction in coderequire_principal/code; rate limits Redis-backed.

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"auth / api_keys / me/span
span class="layer-chip"wallets / agents / groups/span
span class="layer-chip"payments / pay / ap2 / mandates/span
span class="layer-chip"checkout / merchant_checkout/span
span class="layer-chip"compliance / kyc_onboarding/span
span class="layer-chip"admin / emergency / kill_switch/span

div class="arrow-down"↓ codePaymentOrchestrator.execute()/code

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 3 · Pre-Execution Pipeline

div class="layer-title"Policy + Compliance + Mandate + Limits + Anomaly

div class="layer-desc"
The single gate. Implements AGIT (Agent Identity and Trust), KYA (Know Your Agent), FIDES
(trust graph), and the Spending Policy engine. Fail-closed; every deny is audited with
reason code.

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"spending_policy.py/span
span class="layer-chip"agit_policy_engine.py/span
span class="layer-chip"anomaly_detection.py/span
span class="layer-chip"approval_service.py/span
span class="layer-chip"rate_limiter/span
span class="layer-chip"erc8183_policy_guard.py/span

div class="arrow-down"↓ approved intent

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 4 · Protocol Verification

div class="layer-title"AP2 · TAP · x402 · A2A mandate chains

div class="layer-desc"
If the request arrived via a standard protocol (Google/Visa/Mastercard AP2, Visa TAP, x402
402-Payment-Required, Google A2A), the full mandate chain is verified — Ed25519/ECDSA-P256
sigs, replay cache, TTL — before the orchestrator will even schedule a transaction.

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"sardis-protocol/span
span class="layer-chip"MandateVerifier/span
span class="layer-chip"ReplayCache (Postgres + Redis)/span
span class="layer-chip"MandateArchive/span

div class="arrow-down"↓ signed mandate

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 5 · Orchestration + Wallet

div class="layer-title"ChainExecutor · WalletManager · MPC signing

div class="layer-desc"
Chooses the rail (Base, Tempo, Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism), selects the token
(USDC, EURC, USDT, PYUSD), requests an MPC signature from Turnkey (or Fireblocks/Circle),
and submits to the appropriate RPC. Dedicated executors for Tempo
(codetempo/executor.py/code) and EVM chains.

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"sardis-chain/executor.py/span
span class="layer-chip"sardis-wallet/manager.py/span
span class="layer-chip"tempo_keychain.py/span
span class="layer-chip"Circle Paymaster (USDC gas)/span
span class="layer-chip"Pimlico (backup)/span

div class="arrow-down"↓ broadcast tx

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 6 · Settlement

div class="layer-title"CCTP v2 · Fiat rails · Stripe Connect

div class="layer-desc"
Cross-chain USDC via Circle CCTP v2 (unified addresses). Fiat off-ramp via Stripe Connect
(US), Striga (EU vIBANs + SEPA), Bridge (multi-rail). Settlement is explicit and reconciled —
not "best effort."

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"CCTP v2 burn/mint/span
span class="layer-chip"merchant_payout/span
span class="layer-chip"striga_vibans/span
span class="layer-chip"bridge (ACH/SEPA/PIX)/span
span class="layer-chip"reconciliation_queue/span

div class="arrow-down"↓ state hashes

div class="layer"
div class="layer-label"Layer 7 · Audit + Ledger

div class="layer-title"Append-only ledger · Merkle anchor · Evidence export

div class="layer-desc"
Every decision — approved, denied, errored — writes a ledger row. Batches are Merkle-rooted
and anchored on-chain via codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code. Operators and regulators get
cryptographic evidence, not "trust our logs."

div class="layer-chips"
span class="layer-chip"sardis-ledger/span
span class="layer-chip"SardisLedgerAnchor.sol/span
span class="layer-chip"policy_decisions/span
span class="layer-chip"evidence_export/span
span class="layer-chip"data_export/span

!-- Mermaid flow --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Interaction

## One request, end to end.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A simplified sequence — what happens when an agent calls codesardis.pay(to, amount)/code.
Canvas a href="/payment-flow"07 · Payment Flow/a expands every box here with real code references.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent (LangChain)
participant S as Sardis SDK
participant G as API Gateway
participant P as Pre-Execution Pipeline
participant V as Protocol Verifier
participant O as Orchestrator
participant W as Wallet/MPC
participant C as Chain (Base/Tempo)
participant L as Ledger + Anchor

A-S: pay(to, amount, reason)
S-G: POST /v1/payments (API key auth)
G-P: require_principal + orchestrator.execute()
P-P: Spending policy ✓
P-P: KYC/AML ✓
P-P: Mandate delegation ✓
P-P: Rate limit ✓
P-P: Anomaly score ≥ threshold ✓
P-V: verify AP2/TAP mandate chain
V--P: signed ok
P-O: approved intent
O-W: request MPC signature (Turnkey)
W--O: signed tx
O-C: broadcast (Alchemy RPC)
C--O: tx hash + receipt
O-L: append ledger_entry + policy_decision
L-L: Merkle batch + on-chain anchor
O--G: PaymentResult
G--S: 200 OK
S--A: { tx_hash, status }

[/diagram]

!-- Two planes --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Planes

## Data plane vs. control plane.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A common source of confusion: Sardis has two planes. The strongdata plane/strong moves money.
The strongcontrol plane/strong moves opinions emabout/em money — policies, approvals,
trust graph updates, anomaly thresholds. They share a database but not a code path.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Data plane — "do the thing"

div class="card-description"
API gateway → orchestrator → wallet → chain. Synchronous, latency-sensitive (agents are
watching a response), fail-closed. Owned by codesardis-api/code, codesardis-chain/code,
codesardis-wallet/code.

dl class="defs mt-16"
dtLatency budget
dd~2s p99 end-to-end (excluding chain confirmation)

dtBlast radius on bug
ddmoney moves wrong → fail-closed design contains

dtOwner test suite
ddcodetests/integration/code + on-chain replay

/dl

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Control plane — "decide the policy"

div class="card-description"
Policy manager + approvals + kill switch + trust registry + webhooks +
live events. Async, eventually consistent, human-in-the-loop friendly.
The hosted dashboard is private product code; the public repo keeps the API,
policy modules, event streaming routers, and integration contracts.

dl class="defs mt-16"
dtLatency budget
dd~5s to UI from event bus

dtBlast radius on bug
ddbad policy → caught at next execution (fail-closed)

dtOwner test suite
ddPlaywright (dashboard) + policy unit tests

/dl

!-- Key files --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Key files if you want to dig in.

table class="data"
thead
trthFile
thLayer
thWhy it matters

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py/code
tdEverything
tdThe canonical configuration. Every env var, every provider toggle, every chain config. If you want to know what's live, start here.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/orchestrator.py/code
td3 · Gate
tdcodePaymentOrchestrator/code — the single chokepoint through which wallets, mandates, ap2, pay all flow.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/spending_policy.py/code
td3 · Gate
tdThe policy engine. Natural-language input → structured policy → enforcement at runtime.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/main.py/code
td2 · Gateway
tdFastAPI composition root. Shows all 123 routers wired up with middleware.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_v2_chain/executor.py/code
td5 · Execution
tdChain selection, RPC submission, receipt parsing. Multi-chain orchestration lives here.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/manager.py/code
td5 · Execution
tdWallet lifecycle + MPC signing. Where Turnkey/Fireblocks/Circle is abstracted.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-protocol/src/sardis_v2_protocol/ap2.py/code
td4 · Verification
tdAP2 mandate-chain verifier — Intent → Cart → Payment signatures.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-ledger/code
td7 · Audit
tdAppend-only ledger + Merkle batching. Feeds on-chain anchor.

trtdcodecontracts/src/SardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code
td7 · Audit
tdOn-chain anchor for Merkle-rooted evidence batches.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/code
tdControl plane API
tdPolicy, approval, kill-switch, live-event, and merchant/session routes.

/tbody
/table

!-- Where it runs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Deployment Topology

## Where each piece actually runs.

table class="data"
thead
trthSurface
thRuntime
thRegion
thNotes

/thead
tbody
trtdAPI (sardis-api)
tdCloud Run
tdus-central1
tdGunicorn + uvicorn inside Docker; 300s timeout; Fluid-compute-equivalent concurrency.

trtdLanding (apps/landing)
tdVercel
tdEdge
tdNext.js; codesardis.sh/code; static + incremental for blog/compare pages.

trtdDatabase
tdNeon Postgres
tdus-east
tdServerless; 107 migrations applied in order; branching for preview envs.

trtdCache / rate-limit / dedup
tdUpstash Redis
tdglobal
tdRequired in prod (codeSARDIS_REDIS_URL/code); rate limiter fail-closed if unreachable.

trtdMPC signing
tdTurnkey API
tdProvider
tdOrganization + sub-org per tenant; keys never leave Turnkey.

trtdChain RPC
tdAlchemy
tdProvider
tdEnv-driven (codeSARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL/code, etc.); public RPC as fallback.

trtdEvent streaming
tdSSE + webhook outbox
tdCloud Run
tdPer-merchant stream; outbox pattern for webhook at-least-once.

trtdSmart contracts
tdBase + Tempo mainnet
tdL2
tdSee canvas 06 for addresses.

/tbody
/table

=====================================
# REPO STRUCTURE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/repo-structure
=====================================

Canvas title="02 · Repo Structure — Sardis Canvas" currentId="repo-structure"

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 02 · System/span

## Repo Structure

p class="hero-lead"
A map of the Sardis monorepo, every top-level folder, what lives inside, and where to look
when you're lost. The stack is pragmatic, not prescriptive: Python where the money logic
lives, TypeScript where the browser and SDK consumers are, Solidity for the on-chain parts.
Nothing here is load-bearing opinion about what language you should be using.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Three workspaces (codepackages//code, codeapps//code, codecontracts//code) carry
99% of the production code. Everything else is either supporting material (docs, scripts,
demos) or build artefacts. If you're reading code, start in codepackages/sardis-core/code.
If you're running the public site, start in codeapps/landing/code. If you're verifying on-chain,
start in codecontracts/src/code.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"By the numbers

!-- Top-level tree --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Top level

## The whole repo at a glance.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Trimmed of generated artefacts (codenode_modules/code, codedist/code,
code.venv/code, lockfiles, cache directories). What's left is what a new contributor
should actually care about.

div class="tree"sardis/
├── span class="dir"apps//span span class="note"# public apps: landing + canvas source/span
│ ├── landing/ span class="note"# sardis.sh · marketing + blog/span
│ └── canvas-site/ span class="note"# canvas.sardis.sh source/span
├── span class="dir"packages//span span class="note"# public protocol, SDK, integration packages/span
├── span class="dir"contracts//span span class="note"# Foundry · Solidity 0.8.24/span
├── span class="dir"docs//span span class="note"# architecture, governance, OSS contribution docs/span
├── span class="dir"demos//span span class="note"# 18 runnable demos/span
├── span class="dir"examples//span span class="note"# 20+ framework-specific examples/span
├── span class="dir"tests//span span class="note"# cross-package integration tests/span
├── span class="dir"scripts//span span class="note"# ops, deploy, health scripts/span
├── pyproject.toml span class="file"# uv workspace root/span
├── pnpm-workspace.yaml span class="file"# pnpm workspace root/span
├── Dockerfile span class="file"# Cloud Run image/span
└── README.md span class="file"# public-facing overview/span

details style="margin-top: 16px;"
summary style="cursor: pointer; font-family: var(--font-mono); font-size: 12.5px; color: var(--text-muted); padding: 8px 0;"
Show secondary folders (api proxies, monitoring, marketplace, legal, playground...)
/summary
div class="tree" style="margin-top: 8px;"├── span class="dir"api//span span class="note"# Vercel API routes (checkout proxy, docs)/span
├── span class="dir"api-proxy//span span class="note"# Edge proxy for rate-limit-heavy endpoints/span
├── span class="dir"deploy//span span class="note"# GCP Cloud Run configs + env templates/span
├── span class="dir"docs-site//span span class="note"# Docusaurus-style public docs/span
├── span class="dir"canvases//span span class="note"# generated canvas HTML artefacts/span
├── span class="dir"monitoring//span span class="note"# Upptime config + uptime badges/span
├── span class="dir"marketplace//span span class="note"# MCP / Composio / Activepieces submissions/span
└── span class="dir"sardis//span span class="note"# thin public Python import surface/span

/details

details style="margin-top: 12px;"

Show the packages/ expansion (top 12 of 51)
/summary
div class="tree" style="margin-top: 8px;"packages/
├── sardis-core/ span class="note"# domain models, policy, orchestrator/span
├── sardis-api/ span class="note"# FastAPI, 123 routers/span
├── sardis-chain/ span class="note"# multi-chain executor + Tempo/span
├── sardis-wallet/ span class="note"# MPC signing abstraction/span
├── sardis-protocol/ span class="note"# AP2 / TAP / x402 verifiers/span
├── sardis-ledger/ span class="note"# append-only audit + Merkle anchor/span
├── sardis-compliance/ span class="note"# KYC (Didit) + AML (Elliptic)/span
├── sardis-cards/ span class="note"# virtual cards (Stripe Issuing)/span
├── sardis-checkout/ span class="note"# Pay with Sardis merchant flow/span
├── sardis-langchain/ span class="note"# + 17 other framework adapters/span
└── ... span class="note"# see canvas 03 for the full list/span

/details

!-- apps --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"apps/

## Public apps — docs and developer-facing surfaces.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The hosted product dashboard moved to the private product repo. The public repo keeps
the open developer surfaces: the landing site and the technical canvas source.

## apps/landing — codesardis.sh/code

p class="text-muted"Marketing surface. MDX-driven blog + glossary + compare pages. Fully static with ISR for blog.

div class="tree"apps/landing/
├── app/
│ ├── span class="dir"blog//span span class="note"# MDX posts + RSS/span
│ ├── span class="dir"compare//span span class="note"# vs. Stripe, Crossmint, Privy, etc./span
│ ├── span class="dir"faq//span span class="note"# support FAQ/span
│ ├── span class="dir"glossary//span span class="note"# agent-economy term definitions/span
│ ├── span class="dir"landing//span span class="note"# variant landing pages for campaigns/span

│ ├── llms.txt span class="file"# short LLM-friendly summary/span

!-- packages --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"packages/

## Public packages — the protocol and integration surface.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Python packages use codeuv/code, TypeScript packages use codepnpm/code. The hosted
product UI packages moved private; the public package tree focuses on protocol logic,
SDKs, framework adapters, and OSS verification surfaces.

table class="data"

trthGroup
thPackages
thSummary

/thead

trtdCore platform

tdsardis-core, sardis-api, sardis-chain, sardis-wallet, sardis-protocol, sardis-ledger, sardis-compliance, sardis-cards, sardis-guardrails, sardis-zk-policy, sardis-zkp, sardis-ucp

trtdCheckout & merchant
td3
tdsardis-checkout, sardis-connect, sardis-connect-js

trtdPayment providers
td6
tdsardis-coinbase, sardis-lightspark, sardis-striga, sardis-mpp, sardis-mpp-proxy, sardis-ramp

!-- contracts --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"contracts/

## Solidity — the bottom of the stack.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Foundry-based. 9 contracts in codesrc//code, plus audit-surface docs and Forge scripts.
Full deep-dive in a href="/contracts"canvas 06/a.

div class="tree"contracts/
├── src/
│ ├── SardisLedgerAnchor.sol span class="note"# Merkle-root anchor for audit batches/span
│ ├── SardisPolicyModule.sol span class="note"# on-chain policy guard (Zodiac-compatible)/span
│ ├── SardisIdentityRegistry.sol span class="note"# agent identity attestations (ERC-8004)/span
│ ├── SardisReputationRegistry.sol span class="note"# agent reputation scores/span
│ ├── SardisValidationRegistry.sol span class="note"# validator attestations/span
│ ├── SardisJobManager.sol span class="note"# agent-to-agent job lifecycle/span
│ ├── SardisJobRegistry.sol span class="note"# job registry/span
│ ├── SardisVerifyingPaymaster.sol span class="note"# ERC-4337 paymaster/span
│ ├── RefundProtocol.sol span class="note"# Circle RefundProtocol fork/span
│ ├── hooks/ span class="note"# ERC-8183 trust hooks/span
│ ├── interfaces/ span class="note"# shared interfaces/span
│ └── verifiers/ span class="note"# ZK verifier stubs/span
├── script/ span class="note"# Forge deploy scripts/span
├── test/ span class="note"# Foundry tests/span
├── lib/ span class="note"# forge-std, openzeppelin-contracts (submodules)/span
├── out/ span class="note"# build artefacts/span
├── deployments/ span class="note"# per-chain deployment records/span
├── foundry.toml span class="file"# Foundry config/span
├── AUDIT_SURFACE.md span class="file"# explicit audit scope/span
└── DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md span class="file"# mainnet deploy runbook/span

!-- Supporting folders --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"

## Everything else — what each folder is for.

table class="data"
thead
trthFolder
thPurpose
thKey files / subfolders

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeapi//code
tdVercel serverless routes — checkout proxy, docs preview, health checks.
tdcodeindex.py/code, codecheckout//code, codedashboard//code, codelanding//code, codebase-chain-rpc//code

trtdcodeapi-proxy//code
tdEdge proxy in front of rate-limit-heavy endpoints. Offloads Alchemy + Turnkey bursts from Cloud Run.
tdCloudflare Worker source

trtdcodedeploy//code
tdDeployment configs — GCP Cloud Run YAML, env templates, service specs.
tdcodegcp//code, codeenv//code

trtdcodescripts//code
tdPublic contributor tooling: inventory, OSS surface checks, release readiness, protocol conformance, package publication, and safe contract helpers.
tdcoderepo_inventory.py/code, codeoss_surface_check.py/code, codepackage_maturity_check.py/code, codecheck_release_readiness.sh/code, codedeploy-contracts.sh/code, codeaudit//code, codeci//code

trtdcodedocs//code
tdPublic architecture, OSS boundary, contribution, and modernization documentation.
tdcodearchitecture//code, codeoss//code, codemodernization//code, codeplans//code, codequickstart//code

trtdcodedocs-site//code
tdPublic documentation site — codesardis.sh/docs/code.
tdMarkdown + config

trtdcodedemos//code
tdRunnable end-to-end demos. What a new prospect sees.
tdcodemvp_base_sepolia_demo.py/code, codetempo_e2e_demo.py/code, codesardis_connect_e2e_demo.py/code, codesardis-demo-video//code

trtdcodeexamples//code
tdSmall, focused snippets per framework. One concept each.
tdcodesimple_payment.py/code, codelangchain_sardis_agent.py/code, codecrewai_finance_team.py/code, codegoogle_adk_agent.py/code, codevercel_ai_payment.ts/code

trtdcodetests//code
tdCross-package integration tests. Per-package unit tests live in the package.
tdcode__init__.py/code (plus integration suites added via pytest config)

trtdcodecanvases//code
tdGenerated HTML canvases describing the public system.
tdcodeindex.html/code, generated route folders, codecss//code, codejs/nav.js/code

trtdcoderesearch//code
tdPrototypes that may or may not ship. Never imported from production.
tdone-off notebooks + drafts

trtdcodemonitoring//code
tdUpptime-based public status page config.
tdcode.upptimerc.yml/code lives at repo root; dashboards under codemonitoring//code

trtdcodemarketplace//code
tdSubmissions to third-party marketplaces — MCP, Composio, Activepieces, ChatGPT Actions.
tdper-marketplace folders

trtdcodeops//code
tdRunbooks, incident response, on-call handoffs.
tdcodedocs/on-call.md/code, incident templates

trtdcodelegal//code
tdToS, privacy policy, DPA, MSA drafts.
tdlegal markdown

trtdcodeplayground//code
tdScratchpad. Anything here can be deleted.
td—

trtdcodesardis//code
tdThin public Python surface — codepip install sardis/code lands here. Re-exports from codesardis-sdk-python/code.
tdcode__init__.py/code

/tbody
/table

## Repo-level manifests and gates.

table class="data"

trthFile
thPurpose

trtdcodepyproject.toml/code
tduv workspace root. Declares Python members and shared tooling (ruff, pytest, mypy).

trtdcodepnpm-workspace.yaml/code
tdpnpm workspace root. Declares TypeScript members.

trtdcodepackage.json/code
tdRoot scripts — test, build, lint across all JS packages.

trtdcodeDockerfile/code
tdCloud Run image for codesardis-api/code. gunicorn + uvicorn inside code.venv/code.

trtdcodedocker-compose.yml/code
tdLocal stack — API + Postgres + Redis for full-stack dev.

trtdcode.env.example/code
tdCanonical env var list. Source of truth over CLAUDE.md for env surface.

trtdcode.gitleaks.toml/code
tdSecret-scan config enforced in CI.

trtdcode.pre-commit-config.yaml/code
tdpre-commit hooks — ruff, gitleaks, conventional commits.

trtdcodeCLAUDE.md/code
tdAgent instructions. Declared stale-by-design — trust the code.

trtdcodeAGENTS.md/code
tdParallel agent harness spec.

trtdcodeREADME.md/code
tdPublic-facing overview, quickstart.

trtdcodeOPERATIONAL_CHECKLIST.md/code
tdPre-production readiness gate.

trtdcodeLAUNCH_CHECKLIST.md/code
tdLaunch-day go/no-go list.

tdLLM-friendly repo summary.

/table

!-- Dependency mental model --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Mental model

## How the workspaces fit together.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Read top-to-bottom for compile order — codepackages//code builds first, codeapps//code
consume built artefacts, codecontracts//code are deployed separately and their ABIs are
imported back into codepackages/sardis-chain/code.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart TD
subgraph Workspaces
P[packages/ — 51 libs]
A[apps/ — dashboard + landing]
C[contracts/ — 9 Solidity]
end
subgraph Supporting
D[docs/, docs-site/]
S[scripts/, deploy/]
DM[demos/, examples/]
T[tests/]
end

C -- ABIs / addresses -- P
P -- @sardis/* imports -- A
P -- pytest targets -- T
DM -- imports -- P
S -- deploys -- A
S -- deploys -- C
S -- deploys -- P
D -. describes .- P
D -. describes .- A

[/diagram]

!-- Cross-refs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/packages"
div class="canvas-card-num"03 · System

div class="canvas-card-title"Packages (51)

div class="canvas-card-desc"Every package, what's inside, why it exists as a separate package.

/a

div class="canvas-card-num"04 · Backend

div class="canvas-card-title"API Surface

div class="canvas-card-desc"All 123 FastAPI routers grouped by domain.

div class="canvas-card-title"Smart Contracts

div class="canvas-card-desc"Every Solidity source, addresses, deploy pipeline.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/security"
div class="canvas-card-num"19 · Ops

div class="canvas-card-title"Security Model

div class="canvas-card-desc"Trust boundaries, fail-closed defaults, and public security posture.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# PACKAGES
https://canvas.sardis.sh/packages
=====================================

Canvas title="03 · Public Packages — Sardis Canvas" currentId="packages"

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 03 · System/span

## Public Packages

p class="hero-lead"
A unit ships as a separate package when its boundary is real — a provider that can be swapped,
a framework adapter with its own release cadence, or a protocol surface that external auditors
will pin to a version. This canvas maps the OSS protocol, SDK, and integration packages that
remain public after the hosted product UI moved private.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Core platform (12 packages) carries every line of money-moving logic. Framework integrations
(19) are thin adapters that mostly forward to the Python SDK. Payment providers (6) sit behind
uniform interfaces in codesardis-core/code. SDKs + CLIs are the public surface. Hosted
dashboard and checkout UI packages are intentionally not part of this public package catalog.

!-- Dependency graph --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Dependency shape

## How the core packages depend on each other.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Arrows point from consumer to provider. codesardis-core/code is the hub — every other
package either imports from it or registers a provider into it. The API package composes
everything.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
API[sardis-api] -- CORE[sardis-core]
API -- CHAIN[sardis-chain]
API -- WALLET[sardis-wallet]
API -- PROTO[sardis-protocol]
API -- LEDGER[sardis-ledger]
API -- COMP[sardis-compliance]
API -- CHECK[sardis-checkout]
API -- CARDS[sardis-cards]
API -- GUARD[sardis-guardrails]

CHAIN -- CORE
WALLET -- CORE
PROTO -- CORE
LEDGER -- CORE
COMP -- CORE
CHECK -- CORE
CARDS -- CORE
GUARD -- CORE

CHAIN -- WALLET
CHECK -- CHAIN

SDK[sardis-sdk-python] -. HTTP .- API
SDKJS[sardis-sdk-js] -. HTTP .- API
MCP[sardis-mcp-server] -- SDKJS
LC[sardis-langchain] -- SDK

[/diagram]

!-- Core platform --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 1 · Core platform

## The 12 packages that do the actual work.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
If you removed any of these, the product would stop functioning. These carry domain logic,
provider abstractions, and runtime enforcement. All Python, all workspace-local.

table class="data"
thead
trthPackage
thKey files (codesrc/…/code)
thPurpose — why it's separate

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis-core/code
tdcodeconfig.py/code, codespending_policy.py/code, codeorchestrator.py/code, codeagit_policy_engine.py/code, codeanomaly_detection.py/code
tdDomain models, config, policy engine, orchestrator. Every other package imports from here. Split out so downstream can upgrade policy logic without touching transport.

trtdcodesardis-api/code
tdcodesardis/routes/domain/*.py/code, codesardis/routing/*.py/code, codesardis/main.py/code, codealembic//code
tdFastAPI composition root, domain route modules, route registrars, and Alembic migrations. The only package that binds to a port. Separated so contributors can iterate routes without rebuilding policy.

trtdcodesardis-chain/code
tdcodeexecutor.py/code (v2), codesafe_account.py/code, codesmart_router.py/code, codecctp_forwarding.py/code, codetempo/executor.py/code
tdMulti-chain transaction execution. Dedicated Tempo executor lives here. Isolated from wallet so chain-specific RPC quirks don't leak into signing.

trtdcodesardis-wallet/code
tdcodemanager.py/code, codetempo_keychain.py/code, codetempo_accounts.py/code, codemultisig.py/code, codecircle_client.py/code, codesession_manager.py/code, codekey_rotation.py/code
tdMPC signing abstraction (Turnkey / Fireblocks / Circle / Lit). Separated because swapping custody providers must be a zero-day change.

trtdcodesardis-protocol/code
tdcodeap2.py/code, codetap.py/code, codex402_settlement.py/code, codeerc8122.py/code, codeerc8126.py/code, codereplay_cache_redis.py/code, codenonce_registry.py/code
tdAP2, TAP, x402, ERC-8122/8126/8033 mandate-chain verifiers + replay protection. Pinned independently so external auditors can version-lock the verifier.

trtdcodesardis-ledger/code
tdcodeanchor.py/code, codeimmutable.py/code, codemodels.py/code, codereconciliation.py/code, codehybrid.py/code, codedb_engine.py/code
tdAppend-only audit ledger + Merkle anchoring + ImmuDB hybrid mode. Separate so evidence export can be certified without dragging in the rest of the monorepo.

trtdcodesardis-compliance/code
tdcodekyc.py/code, codechecks.py/code, codepep.py/code, codesar.py/code, codecircle_compliance.py/code, codecoinbase_verifications.py/code
tdKYC (Didit) + AML screening (Elliptic) + PEP + SAR filing. Separated because regulated changes have their own approval cycle.

trtdcodesardis-cards/code
tdcodeservice.py/code, codemodels.py/code, codedb_balance.py/code, codedb_subscriptions.py/code, codeofframp.py/code, codeauto_conversion.py/code
tdVirtual cards (Stripe Issuing primary, Lithic pending funding). Separated because issuer onboarding is a distinct revenue line.

trtdcodesardis-checkout/code
tdcodesessions.py/code, codepayment_links.py/code, codeconnectors/sardis_native.py/code, codeconnectors/stripe.py/code
td"Pay with Sardis" merchant flow — sessions, links, settlement connectors. Own release cycle driven by merchant partner ships.

trtdcodesardis-guardrails/code
tdcodezen_engine.py/code, codeml_fraud.py/code, codeanomaly_tuner.py/code, coderate_limiter.py/code, codefingerprint.py/code, codeft3_taxonomy.py/code
tdRuntime guardrails — rule DSL (ZEN), ML anomaly, rate limits, device fingerprint. Loaded into the pre-execution pipeline as pluggable policies.

trtdcodesardis-zk-policy/code
tdcodesrc//code, codepython//code, codeNargo.toml/code, codeProver.toml/code
tdNoir-based ZK proof of policy compliance. Separated because Nargo toolchain is independent of Python.

trtdcodesardis-zkp/code
tdcodecircuits//code, codesrc//code, codeNargo.toml/code
tdLow-level ZK circuit library — shared between zk-policy and research experiments.

trtdcodesardis-ucp/code
tdcodecapabilities/checkout.py/code, codetransports/rest.py/code, codetransports/mcp.py/code
tdUniversal Commerce Protocol — a capability/transport split so the same primitives surface over REST and MCP.

/tbody
/table

!-- Checkout & merchant --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 2 · Checkout & merchant

## Three public packages for merchant/payment primitives.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The public repo keeps the backend checkout engine and Connect SDKs. Hosted browser checkout
UI lives in the private product repo until Sardis deliberately publishes it as a stable OSS widget.

table class="data"
thead
trthPackage
thKey files
thPurpose — why it's separate

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis-checkout/code
td(see group 1)
tdCore backend — sessions, links, connectors.

trtdcodesardis-connect/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_connect/*/code, codepyproject.toml/code, codeuv.lock/code
tdSardis Connect — zero-crypto agent-payment onboarding. Separated because Connect has a distinct onboarding flow and its own sales narrative.

trtdcodesardis-connect-js/code
tdcodesrc//code, codepackage.json/code, codetsconfig.json/code
tdJS client for Sardis Connect. Consumed by partner frontends.

/tbody
/table

!-- AI framework integrations --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 3 · AI framework integrations

## 19 adapters — one per place an agent lives.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Each of these wraps the Python or JS SDK in the native shape of a framework — tools,
toolkits, nodes, actions, pieces. They're intentionally thin. Real work happens in the core
platform; these just speak the local dialect.

table class="data"
thead
trthPackage
thKey files
thPurpose — framework shape

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis-langchain/code
tdcodetoolkit.py/code, codetools.py/code, codecallbacks.py/code
tdLangChain toolkit — codeSardisToolkit/code + per-capability codeBaseTool/codes. v1.0.0 on PyPI.

trtdcodesardis-crewai/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_crewai//code
tdCrewAI agent tools. Exposes Sardis primitives as codeBaseTool/codes for multi-agent crews.

trtdcodesardis-autogpt/code
tdcodesardis_autogpt//code
tdAutoGPT block integration.

trtdcodesardis-openai-agents/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_openai_agents//code
tdOpenAI Agents SDK tool adapter.

trtdcodesardis-openai/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_openai//code
tdOpenAI direct — function-calling tool specs + runtime.

trtdcodesardis-agent-sdk/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_agent_sdk//code
tdClaude Agent SDK integration.

trtdcodesardis-adk/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_adk//code
tdGoogle Agent Development Kit.

trtdcodesardis-a2a/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_a2a//code
tdGoogle Agent-to-Agent protocol adapter — agents transacting with agents.

trtdcodesardis-agentkit/code
tdcodesrc//code
tdCoinbase AgentKit — plug Sardis policies into CDP agents.

trtdcodesardis-browser-use/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_browser_use//code
tdBrowser Use — guardrails for browsing agents that need to pay.

trtdcodesardis-composio/code
tdcodeopenapi.yaml/code, codesrc//code
tdComposio tool marketplace entry — OpenAPI-described.

trtdcodesardis-openclaw/code
tdcodeclawhub.json/code, codesrc//code
tdOpenClaw integration.

trtdcodesardis-stagehand/code
tdcodepackage.json/code, TypeScript
tdStagehand / Browserbase integration — JS-native browsing agents.

trtdcodesardis-ai-sdk/code
tdcodepackage.json/code, Vercel AI SDK tools
tdVercel AI SDK adapter — codetool()/code wrappers for Next.js apps.

trtdcodesardis-activepieces/code
tdcodepackage.json/code, TypeScript
tdActivepieces piece — no-code workflow automation.

trtdcoden8n-nodes-sardis/code
tdcodenodes//code, codecredentials//code
tdn8n community node. Non-money triggers only — see memory: money movement postponed.

trtdcodesardis-mcp-server/code
tdcodesrc/tools/spending.ts/code, codeescrow.ts/code, codefiat.ts/code, codemandates.ts/code, codetrust.ts/code, codeprojects.ts/code
tdMCP server — Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf. Each Sardis capability is an MCP tool.

trtdcodesardis-gpt/code
tdcodeopenapi-actions.yaml/code, codeinstructions.md/code
tdChatGPT Custom GPT Actions spec — no runtime, just declarative.

trtdcodesardis-e2b/code
tdcodee2b.Dockerfile/code, codee2b.toml/code, codeexamples//code
tdE2B sandbox template — preinstalled Sardis SDK for code-execution agents.

/tbody
/table

!-- Payment providers --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 4 · Payment providers

## Six packages wrapping external payment rails.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Each sits behind a provider interface in codesardis-core/code. Installed only when the
downstream deployment needs that rail, so a Base-only deployment doesn't pull in
Lightning dependencies.

table class="data"
thead
trthPackage
thKey files
thPurpose

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis-coinbase/code
tdcodecdp_client.py/code, codex402_client.py/code
tdCoinbase CDP + x402 — onramp, wallets-as-a-service, 402-Payment-Required flows.

trtdcodesardis-lightspark/code
tdcodeclient.py/code, codeuma_registry.py/code, codetransfers.py/code, codepayouts.py/code
tdLightning Network payments + UMA address registry.

trtdcodesardis-striga/code
tdcodeclient.py/code, codekyc.py/code, codeswap.py/code, codestanding_orders.py/code
tdStriga banking/cards — EU vIBANs, SEPA, embedded KYC.

trtdcodesardis-mpp/code
tdcodeclient.py/code, codestripe_method.py/code, codeservices/laso.py/code
tdStripe MPP + Tempo — Merchant Payment Protocol SDK with Sardis policy enforcement.

trtdcodesardis-mpp-proxy/code
tdcodepackage.json/code, TypeScript
tdEdge proxy in front of MPP endpoints — caches capability discovery.

trtdcodesardis-ramp/code
tdcodesrc/sardis_ramp//code
tdMulti-provider on/off ramp — Coinbase Onramp primary, Striga backup.

/tbody
/table

## Six packages — the public surface.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Everything a developer installs. These are emthe/em API contract — backward-compat
guarantees apply only here.

trthPackage
thKey files
thPurpose

/thead

trtdcodesardis-sdk-python/code
tdcodedocs//code, codeexamples//code, codepyproject.toml/code
tdFull Python SDK — codepip install sardis/code ultimately lands here. Async client + mandates + wallets + MPC.

trtdcodesardis-sdk-js/code
tdcode__tests__/code, codedocs//code, codeexamples//code
tdTypeScript SDK — code@sardis/sdk/code. Strict types, Zod validation.

tdNode-based CLI — alternative to Python CLI, bundles alongside code@sardis/sdk/code.

tdDedicated JS client for the ramp flow — embedded in checkout.

/Canvas


=====================================
# API SURFACE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/api-surface
=====================================

Canvas title="04 · API Surface — Sardis Canvas" currentId="api-surface"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 04 · System/span

## API Surface

p class="hero-lead"
One FastAPI application, 123 routers, grouped by domain. Every router sits behind the same
middleware stack (request-id, logging, security headers, rate limit, auth) and — for anything
that moves money — funnels into a single codePaymentOrchestrator.execute()/code chokepoint.
This canvas is the catalogue.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
The surface is large on purpose — every money movement, policy decision, compliance check, and
webhook callback gets its own router so the seams are explicit. The surface is also uniform on
purpose — every request traverses the same middleware, the same principal extractor, and the same
pre-execution pipeline. Wide catalogue, narrow spine.

div class="stat-row"
div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"123
div class="stat-label"Routers

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"/api/v2
div class="stat-label"Canonical prefix

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"12
div class="stat-label"Domain groups

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"10
div class="stat-label"Middleware layers

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"RFC 7807
div class="stat-label"Error model

!-- Middleware stack --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The Spine

## Middleware stack — outermost first.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Registered in codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/main.py/code. FastAPI/Starlette
middleware executes outermost-registered-first on request, innermost-first on response. If you
need to add a cross-cutting concern, add it here, not in a router.

div class="middleware-stack"
div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"01/spanspan class="mw-name"RequestIdMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Assigns codex-request-id/code, propagates through logs and downstream calls./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"02/spanspan class="mw-name"StructuredLoggingMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"JSON log per request: method, path, status, latency_ms, principal, request_id./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"03/spanspan class="mw-name"SecurityHeadersMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"04/spanspan class="mw-name"RequestBodyLimitMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Rejects payloads over configured byte cap — defends against memory-pressure DoS./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"05/spanspan class="mw-name"RateLimitMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Redis-backed token bucket; fail-closed in prod if Redis unreachable (codeSARDIS_REDIS_URL/code)./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"06/spanspan class="mw-name"TapVerificationMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Verifies Visa TAP agent identity headers (Ed25519 / ECDSA-P256) when enabled./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"07/spanspan class="mw-name"X402PaymentMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Implements x402 Payment Required on gated endpoints; verifies on-chain settlement proof./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"08/spanspan class="mw-name"UsageMeteringMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Records billable-event rows for Polar; tags by principal + endpoint class./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"09/spanspan class="mw-name"ActivityLoggerMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Write-ahead of every mutating call into the audit log (feeds Canvas 20)./span

div class="mw-row"
divspan class="mw-order"10/spanspan class="mw-name"CORSMiddleware/spanspan class="mw-desc"Canonical origins: sardis.sh, app.sardis.sh, dashboard.sardis.sh, checkout.sardis.sh + merchant codeembed_origin/code./span

!-- Mermaid: request flow through middleware --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Interaction

## One HTTPS request, traced through the stack.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
From the TLS terminator on Cloud Run, through every middleware, into the router body, and
(if the route is a money-mover) into the orchestrator.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart TD
R[HTTPS request
Cloud Run TLS term] -- MW1[RequestIdMiddleware
x-request-id]
MW1 -- MW2[StructuredLoggingMiddleware]
MW2 -- MW3[SecurityHeadersMiddleware
CSP / HSTS / Frame-Options]
MW3 -- MW4[RequestBodyLimitMiddleware]
MW4 -- MW5[RateLimitMiddleware
Redis token bucket]
MW5 -- MW6[TapVerificationMiddleware
optional — Visa TAP]
MW6 -- MW7[X402PaymentMiddleware
optional — 402 gate]
MW7 -- MW8[UsageMetering + ActivityLogger]
MW8 -- MW9[CORSMiddleware]
MW9 -- DEP[require_principal
API key / JWT / session]
DEP -- ROUTE{Router body}
ROUTE --|money-mover| ORCH[PaymentOrchestrator.execute]
ROUTE --|read-only| DB[(Neon Postgres)]
ORCH -- PIPE[PreExecutionPipeline
policy · KYC · mandate · rate · anomaly]
PIPE -- CHAIN[ChainExecutor → Turnkey MPC → Alchemy RPC]
CHAIN -- LED[Ledger append + Merkle anchor]
LED -- RESP[HTTP 2xx / RFC 7807 problem+json]

[/diagram]

!-- Group 1: Auth & Identity --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 01

## Auth & Identity.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Principal establishment — how a caller proves they are allowed to address the API at all. Three
principal types coexist: human users (JWT / session), tenant service accounts (API keys
codesk_live_…/code), and agents (AGIT identity + signed mandate).

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodeauth.py/code

tdHuman login, signup, MFA, OAuth, password reset, API-key bootstrap.

tdPublic + JWT for protected ops

tdcodePOST /login/code · codePOST /signup/code · codePOST /refresh/code · codeGET /google/callback/code · codePOST /mfa/setup/code · codePOST /api-keys/code

tr
tdcodeapi_keys.py/code

tdTenant API-key CRUD + scoping + hashed storage (SHA-256).

tdJWT (tenant admin)

tdCreate/list/revoke keys; scope by environment (live/test).

tr
tdcodeagent_identity.py/code

tdAGIT (Agent Identity and Trust) registration — Ed25519/ECDSA-P256.

tdAPI key

tdRegister agent identity, rotate keys, attest.

tr
tdcodeagent_auth.py/code

tdSigned-request auth for agents calling on their own behalf.

tdAGIT signature

tdChallenge-response + replay cache.

tr
tdcodeme.py/code

tdCurrent principal introspection — "whoami" for debugging auth.

tdAny

tdcodeGET /me/code returns principal type, tenant, scopes.

tr
tdcodeemail_verification.py/code

tdEmail confirmation flow for new signups.

tdToken link

tdVerify, resend.

tr
tdcodecredentials.py/code

tdProvider credential vault (Turnkey / Stripe / Didit keys per tenant).

tdJWT (tenant admin)

tdEncrypted at rest; never returned cleartext.

tr
tdcodefides_identity.py/code

tdFIDES decentralized trust graph identity lookup.

tdAPI key

tdTrust score + attestation chain for an agent.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 2: Wallets & Agents --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 02

## Wallets & Agents.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Non-custodial MPC wallets plus the agent records that own them. Wallet-facing endpoints never
take a private key — signing is deferred to Turnkey (or Fireblocks / Circle) at execution time.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodewallets.py/code

tdWallet lifecycle + balances + smart-account upgrade + transfer.

tdAPI key / JWT

tdcodePOST /wallets/code · codeGET /{id}/balance/code · codeGET /{id}/balances/code · codePOST /{id}/transfer/code · codePOST /{id}/freeze/code · codePOST /{id}/upgrade-smart-account/code

tr
tdcodeagents.py/code

tdAgent CRUD, metadata, owning-tenant linkage.

tdAPI key

tdCreate agent, attach wallet, assign policy.

tr
tdcodeagent_registry.py/code

tdPublic directory of registered agents for discovery / A2A.

tdMixed

tdSearch by capability, name, owner.

tr
tdcodeagent_heartbeat.py/code

tdLiveness signal — stale agents get KYA-suspended.

tdAGIT signature

tdPeriodic ping; last-seen feeds trust score.

tr
tdcodeagent_activity.py/code

tdPer-agent activity stream (auth events, tx, policy hits).

tdAPI key

tdPaginated timeline for dashboard.

tr
tdcodeagent_events.py/code

tdEmit + query structured agent events (used by KYA & anomaly).

tdAPI key

tdPost event; stream by agent_id.

tr
tdcodegroups.py/code

tdAgent groups — shared budget + policy inheritance.

tdAPI key

tdCreate group, attach agents, group-level spending cap.

tr
tdcodeorganizations.py/code

tdTenant (organization) management — users, roles, env scoping.

tdJWT (org admin)

tdInvite member, switch role, rotate org API keys.

tr
tdcodeholds.py/code

tdReserve funds against a wallet for a pending charge.

tdAPI key

tdCreate / capture / release.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 3: Payments & Mandates --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 03

## Payments & Mandates.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The core money-movers. Every route here ultimately calls
codePaymentOrchestrator.execute()/code — there is no private fast path. A mandate is the
delegated authority from a principal to an agent; a payment is what happens when that authority
is exercised.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodepay.py/code

tdSingle-shot agent payment — the flagship codesardis.pay()/code path.

tdAPI key + AGIT

tdcodePOST /pay/code — amount, to, reason, optional policy override.

tr
tdcodemandates.py/code

tdSpending mandates: create, validate, execute, cancel.

tdAPI key

tdcodePOST /mandates/code · codePOST /{id}/validate/code · codePOST /{id}/execute/code · codePOST /{id}/cancel/code

tr
tdcodemandate_delegation.py/code

tdPrincipal → agent delegation chain, sub-delegation with caps.

tdSigned mandate

tdIssue, revoke, introspect.

tr
tdcodemandate_subscriptions.py/code

tdRecurring mandates (subscriptions with budget windows).

tdAPI key

tdStart / pause / next-renewal.

tr
tdcodespending_mandates.py/code

tdNatural-language spending-mandate creation and enforcement.

tdAPI key

tdParse NL → structured mandate; bind to agent.

tr
tdcodeap2.py/code

tdAP2 (Agent Payment Protocol) mandate-chain verification + execute.

tdAP2 signed chain

tdcodePOST /ap2/payments/execute/code — verifies Intent→Cart→Payment.

tr
tdcodebatch_payments.py/code

tdSubmit many payments in a single request; per-item policy result.

tdAPI key

tdAtomic batch · per-item settle.

tr
tdcodestreaming_payments.py/code

tdMicropayment streams (per-second / per-token rates).

tdAPI key

tdOpen stream, tick, close.

tr
tdcodepayments_refund.py/code

tdInitiate refunds through Circle RefundProtocol + PSP rails.

tdAPI key

tdFull / partial / dispute-linked.

tr
tdcodesubscriptions.py/code

tdMerchant subscription billing (Polar adapter).

tdAPI key

tdPlan CRUD, usage roll-up.

tr
tdcodempp.py/code

tdStripe Merchant Payment Protocol (MPP) integration for Tempo mainnet.

tdAPI key

tdMPP quote → pay → receipt.

tr
tdcodeswap.py/code

tdToken swaps via DEX router (Tempo, Base).

tdAPI key

tdQuote, execute, slippage-protect.

tr
tdcodepayment_objects.py/code

tdCanonical payment object retrieval (ID → full record + state machine).

tdAPI key

tdRead by id, filter by status.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 4: Policy & Compliance --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 04

## Policy & Compliance.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The gate. Natural-language policies in, structured enforcement out. KYC via Didit, sanctions via
Elliptic, KYA (Know Your Agent) implemented internally. Every rejection is audited with a reason
code.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodepolicies.py/code

tdSpending policy CRUD, preview, apply, history, recommendations.

tdAPI key (+ MPP gate on parse/check)

tdcodePOST /parse/code · codePOST /preview/code · codePOST /apply/code · codePOST /check/code · codeGET /templates/code · codeGET /{agent}/history/code

tr
tdcodepolicy_analytics.py/code

tdAggregate policy-decision metrics (allow rate, top denied reason codes).

tdJWT

tdTrend by agent, by reason.

tr
tdcodepolicy_simulation.py/code

tdDry-run a candidate policy against historical traffic.

tdJWT

tdReplay window, impact report.

tr
tdcodefallback_policies.py/code

tdSafety-net policies applied when a primary policy errors.

tdJWT

tdGlobal deny-cap, max-amount backstop.

tr
tdcodeapprovals.py/code · codeapproval_config.py/code

tdHuman-in-the-loop approval queue for over-threshold agent requests.

tdJWT

tdCreate request, approve / reject, notify via webhook.

tr
tdcodecompliance.py/code

tdKYC verify, KYA register/check/suspend, sanctions screen.

tdJWT / API key

tdcodePOST /kyc/verify/code · codeGET /kyc/{agent}/code · codePOST /kya/register/code · codePOST /sanctions/screen/code · codePOST /sanctions/block/code

tr
tdcodekyc_onboarding.py/code

tdDidit session bootstrap → webhook → codesk_live_/code issuance.

tdJWT

tdcodePOST /kyc/initiate/code.

tr
tdcodecompliance_export.py/code

tdRegulated evidence bundles (PDF + JSON + Merkle proof).

tdJWT (admin)

tdExport by date range / agent.

tr
tdcodetrust.py/code

tdFIDES trust-graph mutation (upvote, revoke, peer-attest).

tdAGIT signature

tdGraph ops + local trust score.

tr
tdcodeanomaly.py/code

tdAnomaly detector scoring + rule tuning.

tdJWT

tdScore a request, tune threshold.

tr
tdcodeerc8183.py/code

tdERC-8183 policy-guard hooks for on-chain enforcement.

tdAPI key

tdGuard check, USDC cap, fee cap.

tr
tdcodeguardrails/code (via codesardis-guardrails/code)

tdAgent guardrail presets referenced from codepolicies.py/code.

tdAPI key

tdTemplate by persona (buyer, trader, ops).

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 5: Checkout & Merchant --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 05

## Checkout & Merchant.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
"Pay with Sardis" — the consumer-facing checkout. Public endpoints auth by codeclient_secret/code
(never session id); merchant management endpoints auth by coderequire_principal/code.
Hardened against double-pay via codeSELECT … FOR UPDATE NOWAIT/code and DB-unique
idempotency keys.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodecheckout.py/code

tdLow-level checkout object CRUD + public status read.

tdPublic (client_secret) / API key

tdcodePOST /checkout/code · codeGET /{id}/code · codeGET /payment-methods/code

tr
tdcodemerchant_checkout.py/code

tdMerchant-scoped checkout sessions, SSE stream, links.

tdMerchant API key / public link

tdCreate session, code/stream/code SSE, reusable checkout link, external-wallet confirm.

tr
tdcodecheckout_controls.py/code

tdPer-merchant checkout controls (min/max amount, allowed tokens).

tdMerchant JWT

tdUpsert controls.

tr
tdcodemerchants.py/code

tdMerchant account CRUD, settlement address, embed_origin.

tdJWT

tdCreate merchant, rotate keys.

tr
tdcodesecure_checkout.py/code

tdStep-up auth + SCA-style challenges for high-value sessions.

tdSession token

tdStart challenge, verify.

tr
tdcodeinvoices.py/code

tdInvoice issuance + stable payment link.

tdAPI key

tdCreate, mark paid on settlement.

tr
tdcodereceipts.py/code

tdCanonical receipt retrieval (rendered + JSON).

tdPublic (receipt_id)

tdSigned URL for the PDF.

tr
tdcodeescrow_disputes.py/code

tdDispute flow backed by Circle RefundProtocol.

tdAPI key

tdOpen, respond, resolve.

/tbody

!-- Group 6: Cards --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 06

## Cards.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Virtual-card issuance for agents, via Stripe Issuing (primary, live-ready) and Lithic
(sandbox; blocked on $5M funding requirement). Cards are policy-gated at auth time — every
transaction hits codePreExecutionPipeline/code before Stripe approves.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
trtdcodecards.py/code
tdCard issuance, freeze/unfreeze, limits, PAN reveal.
tdAPI key
tdCreate card, update limits, freeze.

trtdcodevirtual_cards.py/code
tdEphemeral single-use cards for one-off merchant payments.
tdAPI key
tdMint + auto-destroy on settle.

trtdcodestablecoin_cards.py/code
tdUSDC-funded cards — wallet balance backs the card float.
tdAPI key
tdLink wallet to card, auto-top-up.

trtdcodepartner_card_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound auth/clearing hooks from Stripe / Lithic.
tdHMAC signature
tdAuthorize, clear, reverse.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 7: Ramps & Treasury --

## Ramps & Treasury.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
On/off-ramps, FX, and operator-level treasury functions. Coinbase Onramp (hosted, free) is
default; Stripe Connect + Striga + Bridge handle the bank-rail side; Lightspark wires up
Lightning for instant micro-settlement.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

tdAPI key
tdQuote, initiate, status.

trtdcoderamp.py/code
tdUnified ramp router (on + off, provider-agnostic).
tdAPI key
tdProvider select by region.

trtdcodefiat_rails.py/code
tdRaw fiat rail ops (ACH, SEPA, PIX, FPS).
tdAPI key
tdCreate payout, track status.

trtdcodestripe_connect.py/code
tdStripe Connect account onboarding + payouts.
tdJWT
tdCreate account link, check status.

trtdcodestriga.py/code
tdStriga vIBAN issuance + SEPA in/out.
tdAPI key
tdCreate vIBAN, withdraw.

trtdcodebridge.py/code
tdBridge multi-rail router (ACH / SEPA / PIX).
tdAPI key
tdTransfer, track.

tdAPI key
tdPay invoice, create invoice.

trtdcodetreasury.py/code · codetreasury_ops.py/code

tdMove between pools, FX hedge.

tdQuote, lock, execute.

tdAttach source, check capabilities.
