!-- Group 8: Ledger & Audit --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 08

## Ledger & Audit.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Append-only ledger — every decision (allow / deny / error) is a row. Batches are Merkle-rooted
and anchored on-chain via codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code. Regulators get cryptographic
evidence, not trust-our-logs.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodeledger.py/code

tdQuery ledger entries + Merkle-path verification.

tdJWT / API key

tdcodeGET /entries/code · codeGET /entries/{tx_id}/code · codeGET /entries/{tx_id}/verify/code · codeGET /recent/code · codeGET /stats/code

trtdcodeaudit_anchors.py/code
tdOn-chain anchor batch visibility (batch id → tx hash).
tdJWT
tdList batches, verify anchor.

trtdcodeevidence.py/code · codeevidence_export.py/code
tdEvidence bundle builder (decisions + mandate chain + receipts).
tdJWT (admin)
tdBuild by tx, export zip.

trtdcodedata_export.py/code
tdTenant-scope bulk export (GDPR / SOC2).
tdJWT (admin)
tdStart job, download when ready.

trtdcodeattestation.py/code
tdIssue attestation envelope — signed claim about a decision.
tdAPI key
tdCreate, verify.

trtdcodetransactions.py/code
tdCanonical transaction list view for dashboard.
tdJWT
tdFilter by status / agent / date.

trtdcodesettlements.py/code
tdSettlement batch records (fiat + on-chain).
tdJWT
tdBatch detail, reconciliation status.

trtdcodeoutcomes.py/code
tdFinal-state outcome store (succeeded / refunded / disputed).
tdAPI key
tdRead by payment_id.

trtdcodereports.py/code
tdPre-computed financial reports (daily / monthly).
tdJWT
tdFetch, schedule.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 9: Protocol-specific --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 09

## Protocol Gateways — AP2 / A2A / x402 / ACP / SPT / CPN.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Sardis is the universal payment layer for the agent economy — which means speaking every
emerging protocol. Each router here adapts one external standard to the same internal
orchestrator.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotable endpoints

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodex402.py/code

tdHTTP 402 Payment Required — verify + settle + challenge issuance.

tdx402 payload

tdcodePOST /verify/code · codePOST /settle/code · codeGET /settlements/{payment_id}/code · codePOST /dry-run/code · codePOST /challenges/code

trtdcodeap2.py/code
tdGoogle/Visa/Mastercard AP2 — Intent→Cart→Payment chain.
tdAP2 signed chain
tdcodePOST /payments/execute/code.

trtdcodea2a.py/code · codea2a_payments.py/code
tdGoogle Agent-to-Agent protocol — discovery + payment.
tdA2A identity + API key
tdPublic discovery router + authed payment router.

trtdcodeacp.py/code
tdAgent Commerce Protocol adapter.
tdACP signature
tdQuote + purchase.

trtdcodespt.py/code
tdStripe Payment Token protocol.
tdAPI key
tdToken exchange.

trtdcodecpn.py/code · codecpn_webhooks.py/code
tdCommercial Payment Network router + inbound hooks.
tdMixed
tdSend + reconcile.

trtdcodemvp.py/code
tdMinimal demo/path-of-least-resistance endpoints for demos.
tdAPI key
tdQuick pay for pitches.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 10: Webhooks --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 10

## Webhooks — inbound & outbound.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Inbound webhooks (partners → Sardis) are signature-verified with HMAC or provider-specific
schemes and deduped by codeevent_id/code. Outbound webhooks (Sardis → merchant) use an
outbox pattern with at-least-once delivery and per-delivery tracking.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thDirection
thSigner
thNotes

/thead
tbody
trtdcodewebhooks.py/code
tdOutbound
tdSardis HMAC
tdMerchant endpoint configuration, delivery log, replay.

trtdcodestripe_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound
tdStripe signing secret
tdPayment intents, charge events, Issuing authorizations.

trtdcodestripe_spt_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound
tdStripe
tdSPT-specific event types.

trtdcodemastercard_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound
tdMastercard
tdAP2 card-network callbacks.

trtdcodevisa_tap_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound
tdVisa TAP
tdTAP identity and auth events.

trtdcodepartner_card_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound
tdHMAC
tdStripe Issuing / Lithic card auths.

trtdcodecpn_webhooks.py/code
tdInbound
tdPartner-specific
tdCPN network callbacks.

trtdcodepolar_webhook.py/code
tdInbound
tdPolar
tdBilling lifecycle (until Stripe Atlas).

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 11: Admin & Ops --

div class="eyebrow"Group 11

## Admin & Operations.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Platform-operator surface. Separate from tenant APIs — these endpoints require a Sardis-internal
JWT and are scoped to ops / SRE / support roles. The kill switch here is the one Elizabeth Yin
and regulators want to see.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose
thAuth
thNotes

/thead
tbody

tdGeneral admin surface (tenant search, impersonate in support mode).

tdAudit-logged.

trtdcodeadmin_reconciliation.py/code
tdManual reconciliation tools for settlement mismatches.
tdOps JWT
tdCompare ledger vs. provider ledger.

trtdcodeemergency.py/code
tdEmergency stop (kill-switch) for tenant / agent / chain.
tdOps JWT + MFA
tdFail-closed; propagates across regions.

trtdcodeenterprise_support.py/code
tdNamed-contact support routes for enterprise tier.
tdOps JWT
tdTicket create / escalate.

trtdcodealerts.py/code · codews_alerts.py/code
tdAlert rules + WebSocket stream for dashboards.
tdJWT
tdRule CRUD, live stream.

trtdcodereliability.py/code
tdSRE-facing SLO data + feature flags.
tdOps JWT
tdError budget, flag toggles.

trtdcodesandbox.py/code
tdTest-environment management for merchants.
tdJWT
tdProvision, reset.

trtdcodesimulation.py/code
tdTraffic replay + chaos simulation.
tdOps JWT
tdReplay window, fault inject.

trtdcodeexecution_modes.py/code
tdSwitch chain mode (live / simulate / dry-run).
tdOps JWT
tdTenant-scoped.

trtdcodeenvironment_templates.py/code
tdPre-baked env bundles for onboarding.
tdOps JWT
tdClone to new tenant.

trtdcodeworkflow_templates.py/code
tdReusable workflow (policy + wallet + mandate) templates.
tdJWT
tdInstantiate per tenant.

tdBilling state (Polar today, Stripe post-Atlas).
tdJWT
tdRead invoice, usage.

tdRaw usage-metering data.
tdJWT
tdDrill-down by endpoint class.

tdPlugin registry / enable / disable.
tdOps JWT
tdTenant opt-in plugins.

trtdcodeservice_directory.py/code
tdInternal service catalog for A2A discovery.
tdMixed
tdRegister, list.

trtdcodemarketplace.py/code
tdMarketplace listings (agents / policies / templates).
tdMixed
tdList, install.

trtdcodecounterparties.py/code
tdKnown counterparty registry.
tdJWT
tdTag, flag, whitelist.

trtdcodenotifications.py/code
tdMulti-channel notification fanout (email, Slack, webhook).
tdJWT
tdRule CRUD.

trtdcodedev.py/code
tdDev-only helpers (seed data, principal impersonation).
tdDev mode only
tdDisabled in prod.

trtdcodeexceptions.py/code
tdCentral exception handlers → RFC 7807 problem+json.
tdn/a
tdWired via codeapp.add_exception_handler/code.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 12: Analytics & Events --
section class="section"

## Analytics, Metrics & Live Events.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Read-side infrastructure: dashboard metrics, SDK telemetry, event streams. Event-stream
endpoints use Server-Sent Events (SSE) with polling fallback; alerts use a WebSocket.

table class="data"
thead
trthRouter
thPurpose

/thead
tbody

tdTenant analytics (spend by agent, success rate, policy hit rate).

tdPre-aggregated numbers for the dashboard cards.

tdRaw time-series metrics (Prometheus-compatible).

tdSDK-side telemetry ingest (install count, version, error).

trtdcodeevent_stream.py/code
tdSSE event stream per tenant + agent.

trtdcodempp_demo.py/code
tdMPP demo-day endpoints for Tempo launch (2026-03-18).

span class="router-chip"x402.py/span

!-- Error model --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Contract

## Error model — RFC 7807 problem+json.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every non-2xx response conforms to a href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7807" target="_blank" rel="noopener"RFC 7807/a.
Clients key off codetype/code (stable URI) and codecode/code (enum) — not the HTTP
status alone, and definitely not the codetitle/code string.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Shape

div class="card-description"
Every error carries: codetype/code (URI ref), codetitle/code (short human), codestatus/code
(HTTP code), codedetail/code (human detail), codecode/code (machine enum),
coderequest_id/code (for grep'ing logs), and when applicable codereason_code/code
(policy engine) or coderetry_after/code (rate limit).

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Categories

dl class="defs"
dtcode400/code validation_error
ddPydantic schema mismatch — array of field errors in codeerrors[]/code.

dtcode401/code auth_required
ddMissing / malformed principal. codeWWW-Authenticate/code set.

dtcode403/code policy_denied
ddPre-execution pipeline rejection. codereason_code/code names the gate.

dtcode409/code conflict
ddIdempotency collision or state-machine violation.

dtcode422/code mandate_invalid
ddAP2 / TAP / mandate chain verification failed.

dtcode429/code rate_limited
ddRedis token-bucket refusal. coderetry_after/code in seconds.

dtcode500/code internal
ddUnclassified — always carries coderequest_id/code.

dtcode503/code unavailable
ddDependency fail-closed (Redis, KYA, FIDES).

/dl

!-- Cross-refs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper

/Canvas


=====================================
# DATABASE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/database
=====================================

Canvas title="05 · Database Schema — Sardis Canvas" currentId="database"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 05 · Backend/span

## Database Schema

p class="hero-lead"
One Neon Postgres database. 107 sequentially-numbered SQL migrations under
codepackages/api/migrations//code. No ORM is doing the heavy lifting — tables are
defined by hand, in SQL, and applied in strict order. When Postgres is the source of truth for
money, legibility beats abstraction.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Migrations are the canonical record of how the schema evolved. Reading them in order is the
fastest way to understand what Sardis does: identity first, then wallets, then policies, then
ledger, then merchants, then mandates, then protocol-specific tables (Tempo, A2A, x402),
and finally the codeba_/code-prefixed better-auth surface.

div class="stat-row"
div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"107
div class="stat-label"Migrations

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"Neon
div class="stat-label"Postgres, serverless

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"50+
div class="stat-label"Tables

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"NNN_*.sql
div class="stat-label"Naming convention

!-- Conventions --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Conventions

## How this schema is written.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Hand-written SQL, applied in order

div class="card-description"
Migrations live in codepackages/api/migrations//code as
codeNNN_description.sql/code. Applied with
codepsql $DATABASE_URL -f file/code. A codeschema_migrations/code table guards
against double-apply. No Alembic, no Django, no Drizzle — direct SQL keeps the diff honest.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Append-only, additive-only

div class="card-description"
Destructive changes (drop column, tighten constraint) must be proposed as a new migration with
a rollback companion (see code001_initial_schema_rollback.sql/code). codeADD COLUMN IF
NOT EXISTS/code is used liberally so migrations are re-runnable on partially-applied dbs.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Three primary-key styles, chosen on purpose

div class="card-description"
codeuuid/code for records a human might quote (orgs, wallets, merchants). codetext/code
prefixed IDs (codemandate_…/code, codeappr_…/code, codemst_…/code) for records an
agent or operator will handle. codebigserial/code only for high-churn internal queues
(codereconciliation_queue/code).

div class="card"
div class="card-title"codeba_/code prefix for better-auth tables

div class="card-description"
The better-auth library manages its own schema (codeba_user/code, codeba_session/code,
codeba_apikey/code, …) and coexists with legacy Sardis auth tables. Prefix avoids
collisions while the dual-path is live. See migrations 077, 097, 098.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Indexes travel with the columns that need them

div class="card-description"
Every migration that adds a frequently-queried column also adds the index. Migration 087
(codemissing_indexes/code) exists to clean up what slipped through. Query plans stay
predictable.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"JSONB for open-ended, columns for load-bearing

div class="card-description"
codemetadata/code, codesettings/code, codemerchant_scope/code are JSONB because
shape evolves. Money columns (codeamount_per_tx/code, codespent_total/code) are
codeNUMERIC(20,6)/code because floats in finance cost real money.

!-- ER diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Core shape

## ER diagram — the payment spine.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A simplified entity map of the tables every money-moving request touches. Ownership flows left
to right; evidence flows right to left.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

erDiagram
organizations ||--o{ agents : "owns"
organizations ||--o{ ba_user : "has members"
organizations ||--o{ merchants : "operates"
agents ||--o{ wallets : "controls"
agents ||--o{ agent_group_members : "joins"
agent_groups ||--o{ agent_group_members : "groups"
agents ||--o{ spending_policies : "is governed by"
spending_policies ||--o{ policy_versions : "has history"
agents ||--o{ spending_mandates : "is delegated"
spending_mandates ||--o{ mandate_state_transitions : "audit log"
spending_mandates ||--o{ spending_mandates : "parent of"
spending_mandates ||--o{ policy_decisions : "produces"
policy_versions ||--o{ policy_decisions : "evaluates"
wallets ||--o{ transactions : "submits"
transactions ||--o{ receipts : "settles into"
transactions ||--o{ ledger_entries : "records"
ledger_entries ||--o{ merkle_batches : "rolls up into"
merchants ||--o{ merchant_checkout_sessions : "sells via"
merchant_checkout_sessions ||--o{ transactions : "settles through"

[/diagram]

!-- Migration timeline — representative --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Timeline

## Representative migrations — read these to understand the system.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
20 migrations sampled from the full 107. Each one corresponds to a real product moment; reading
them in order is the fastest onboarding you can do as a backend engineer.

table class="data"
thead
trth#
thName
thWhat it added

/thead
tbody
trtdcode001/code
tdcodeinitial_schema/code
tdOrganizations, agents — the tenancy root. Rollback companion ships alongside.

trtdcode002/code
tdcodespending_policy_state/code
tdcodespent_total/code on codespending_policies/code; multi-token codetime_window_limits/code. First enforceable spending limits.

trtdcode003/code
tdcodeledger_compliance_tables/code
tdPromotes ledger codereceipts/code and compliance tables from runtime codeCREATE IF NOT EXISTS/code into durable SQL.

trtdcode004/code
tdcodeapprovals/code
tdHuman-in-the-loop approval queue with codeapproval_status/code enum (pending / approved / denied / expired / cancelled).

trtdcode013/code
tdcodeledger_merkle_receipts/code
tdMerkle batching for ledger evidence — the foundation for on-chain anchors.

trtdcode014/code
tdcodeagent_groups/code
tdMulti-agent governance — group-level budget (per-tx, daily, monthly, total) and codeagent_group_members/code linkage.

trtdcode017/code
tdcodeerc4337_wallet_fields/code
tdAdds smart-account / user-op / entry-point columns on codewallets/code for Safe v1.4.1 upgrade.

trtdcode019/code
tdcodecanonical_ledger_state/code
tdCanonicalizes the state machine — every payment moves through the same FSM regardless of rail.

trtdcode030/code
tdcodemerchant_checkout/code
td"Pay with Sardis" — codemerchants/code, codemerchant_checkout_sessions/code, settlement preferences (USDC / fiat).

trtdcode031/code
tdcodecheckout_hardening/code
tdclient_secret, idempotency_key (unique index), platform_fee_amount, embed_origin, reusable checkout links, webhook delivery dedup.

trtdcode037/code
tdcodedurable_idempotency/code
tdIdempotency-key storage promoted from Redis to Postgres for durability guarantees.

trtdcode040/code
tdcodepolicy_versions/code
tdImmutable version records for every policy change — codepolicy_json/code, codehash/code, codeparent_version_id/code.

trtdcode041/code
tdcodepolicy_decisions/code
tdEvidence log — every codeevaluate()/code produces a signed decision (codeverdict/code, codesteps_json/code, codeevidence_hash/code) for SOC 2.

trtdcode056/code
tdcodeerc8183_jobs/code
tdJob queue for ERC-8183 policy-guard on-chain enforcement.

trtdcode057/code
tdcodereconciliation_queue/code
tdPersistent recon queue — replaces in-memory implementation so pending ledger appends survive restarts.

trtdcode071/code
tdcodespending_mandates/code
tdCore spending-mandate table — principal, issuer, merchant_scope, amount_{per_tx, daily, weekly, monthly, total}, lifecycle enum.

trtdcode072/code
tdcodemandate_transitions/code
tdAudit log of every lifecycle change (activate, suspend, revoke, expire) keyed to codespending_mandates.id/code.

trtdcode074/code
tdcodetempo_support/code
tdTIP-20 gas model — codememo/code, codefee_token/code, codefee_token_amount/code, codetip20_transfer/code on codetransactions/code.

trtdcode077/code
tdcodebetter_auth_tables/code
tdcodeba_user/code, codeba_session/code, codeba_account/code, codeba_verification/code — session-based auth path.

trtdcode080/code
tdcodemandate_trees/code
tdHierarchical mandate delegation — codeparent_mandate_id/code, codedelegation_depth/code, coderoot_mandate_id/code. CFO → dept → agent.

trtdcode085/code
tdcodea2a_tables/code
tdGoogle A2A — codesettlements/code and codeescrows/code tables for agent-to-agent commerce.

trtdcode097/code
tdcodeba_apikey_table/code
tdbetter-auth API-key plugin table — hashed key, rate-limit fields, permissions JSONB, mode (live / test).

trtdcode106/code
tdcodeagent_registry/code
tdPublic agent directory — codecapabilities/code, codesupported_protocols/code, codetrust_score/code, codekya_status/code.

/tbody
/table

## Identity & Tenancy

tdcodeexternal_id/code, codesettings/code jsonb, codeis_active/code
td001

trtdcodeagents/code
tdcodeuuid/code
tdorganization_id FK, external_id, metadata jsonb
td001

trtdcodeagent_groups/code
tdcodeuuid/code
tdbudget_per_tx · budget_daily · budget_monthly · budget_total · merchant_policy
td014

trtdcodeagent_group_members/code
tdcodeuuid/code
tdgroup_id FK, agent_id, codeUNIQUE(group_id, agent_id)/code
td014

trtdcodeagent_registry/code
tdcodeuuid/code
tdcapabilities[], supported_protocols[], trust_score, kya_status
td106

trtdcodeapi_keys/code
tdcodeuuid/code
tdhashed key (SHA-256), scopes, environment
td001 / 076

tdemail UNIQUE, org_id, role, kyc_status
td077

trtdcodeba_session/code
tdcodetext/code
tdtoken UNIQUE, expires_at, user_id FK
td077

tdcodetext/code
tdkey (hashed), prefix, config_id (live/test), rate-limit fields, permissions jsonb

!-- Integrity mechanisms --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Integrity

## How the schema defends correctness.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Unique idempotency

div class="card-description"
codemerchant_checkout_sessions.idempotency_key/code has a partial UNIQUE index. Two
concurrent creates with the same key collide at the database — there is no "last write wins"
path that could double-pay a customer (migration 031).

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Row locks at claim time

div class="card-description"
Checkout claim uses codeSELECT … FOR UPDATE NOWAIT/code — if another worker already holds
the lock, the caller fails fast rather than waits, and the client retries. Prevents two
settlement workers from racing the same session.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Enum types for state machines

div class="card-description"
codeapproval_status/code, codeapproval_urgency/code, CHECK constraints on
codekya_status/code, codesettlement_type/code. Invalid states become database errors,
not runtime surprises.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Cascading deletes where ownership is clear

div class="card-description"
codeba_session.user_id → ba_user(id) ON DELETE CASCADE/code;
codemandate_state_transitions.mandate_id → spending_mandates(id) ON DELETE CASCADE/code.
Ownership deletions don't leave orphans.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Reconciliation queue survives restarts

div class="card-description"
If a ledger append fails after on-chain execution, the reconciliation entry lands in
codereconciliation_queue/code with codestatus='pending'/code — not in RAM. A worker
drains it on the next process boot (migration 057).

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Policy evidence is cryptographically hashed

div class="card-description"
codepolicy_versions.hash/code and codepolicy_decisions.evidence_hash/code mean a
policy decision can be proven-in-court without trusting the Sardis log pipeline. Migration 040
and 041.

!-- Full chip list --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Full catalogue

## All 107 migrations.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Source: codels packages/api/migrations//code on 2026-04-19.

div class="migration-chip-grid"
span class="migration-chip"001_initial_schema/span
span class="migration-chip"002_spending_policy_state/span
span class="migration-chip"003_ledger_compliance_tables/span
span class="migration-chip"004_approvals/span
span class="migration-chip"005_audit_triggers/span
span class="migration-chip"006_wallet_freeze/span
span class="migration-chip"007_agent_spending_policy/span
span class="migration-chip"008_wallet_addresses_and_limits/span
span class="migration-chip"009_spending_state_persistence/span
span class="migration-chip"010_kyb_verifications/span
span class="migration-chip"011_sar_and_identity_persistence/span
span class="migration-chip"012_travel_rule/span
span class="migration-chip"013_ledger_merkle_receipts/span
span class="migration-chip"014_agent_groups/span
span class="migration-chip"015_card_and_ledger_persistence/span
span class="migration-chip"016_subscriptions/span
span class="migration-chip"017_erc4337_wallet_fields/span
span class="migration-chip"018_fiat_treasury_core/span
span class="migration-chip"019_canonical_ledger_state/span
span class="migration-chip"020_signup_email_index/span
span class="migration-chip"021_ledger_batch_and_audit/span
span class="migration-chip"022_organizations/span
span class="migration-chip"023_kya_state_persistence/span
span class="migration-chip"024_secure_checkout_jobs/span
span class="migration-chip"025_issuer_funding_attempts/span
span class="migration-chip"026_performance_indexes/span
span class="migration-chip"027_subscription_execution_fields/span
span class="migration-chip"028_circle_wallet_fields/span
span class="migration-chip"029_x402_challenges/span
span class="migration-chip"030_merchant_checkout/span
span class="migration-chip"031_checkout_hardening/span
span class="migration-chip"032_webhook_outbox/span
span class="migration-chip"033_wallet_verification/span
span class="migration-chip"034_user_auth/span
span class="migration-chip"035_transaction_caps/span
span class="migration-chip"036_execution_side_effects/span
span class="migration-chip"037_durable_idempotency/span
span class="migration-chip"038_execution_intents/span
span class="migration-chip"039_access_audit_log/span
span class="migration-chip"040_policy_versions/span
span class="migration-chip"041_policy_decisions/span
span class="migration-chip"042_data_governance/span
span class="migration-chip"043_sso_config/span

=====================================
# CONTRACTS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/contracts
=====================================

Canvas title="06 · Smart Contracts — Sardis Canvas" currentId="contracts"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 06 · Backend/span

## Smart Contracts

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis is deliberately thin on-chain. The heavy lifting — policy evaluation, mandate
verification, KYC, compliance — happens off-chain where it can be iterated safely. On-chain we
only deploy what needs public, auditable, tamper-evident behaviour: identity registries, an
evidence anchor, an escrow-based job primitive (ERC-8183), and a refund protocol.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Nine code.sol/code files live under codecontracts/src//code. Two are deprecated on
purpose (codeSardisPolicyModule/code → Zodiac Roles; codeSardisVerifyingPaymaster/code →
Circle Paymaster) — kept for reference, not deployed. The rest implement ERC-8004 (agent
identity), ERC-8183 (job escrow), a minimal ledger anchor, and Circle's audited
codeRefundProtocol/code vendored in-tree.

div class="stat-row"
div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"9
div class="stat-label"Contracts

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"Foundry
div class="stat-label"Build & test

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"0.8.20 – 0.8.24
div class="stat-label"Solidity

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"OpenZeppelin
div class="stat-label"Primitives

div class="stat-pill"div class="stat-num"ERC-8004 / 8183 / 4337
div class="stat-label"Standards

!-- Design principles --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Principles

## Why so little on-chain?

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Prefer audited standards over bespoke contracts

div class="card-description"
Wallet custody: Safe v1.4.1 (pre-deployed on every EVM chain, $100B+ TVL). Policy module:
Zodiac Roles (code0x9646fDAD…/code). Paymaster: Circle Paymaster
(code0x0578cFB2…/code). Sardis ships no custom money-handling contract if an audited one
fits — zero audit surface at MVP.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"On-chain for evidence, off-chain for decisions

div class="card-description"
Policy decisions live in Postgres (migration 041) because policies change. What goes on-chain
is the emMerkle root/em of a batch of those decisions (codeSardisLedgerAnchor/code) —
tamper-evidence without tying policy logic to chain latency or upgrade ceremony.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Standards-compliant identity & reputation

div class="card-description"
Agent identity is ERC-8004 (ERC-721 token + reputation + validation registries). A wallet can
prove "I am agent #42 in this registry" without trusting a Sardis API. Reputation is
submitted by clients, signed on-chain, and queryable by any counterparty.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"ERC-8183 for commerce, not custom escrows

div class="card-description"
Escrow-based agent commerce follows the ERC-8183 spec with a strict state machine and
selector-based hooks (codeIACPHook/code). Easier for counterparties to integrate than a
Sardis-only escrow; easier to audit.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Hook-dispatch over inheritance

div class="card-description"
codeSardisJobManager/code routes hook calls through codeIACPHook/code with a gas
limit — before-hooks can block, after-hooks are fire-and-forget. Keeps hook failures from
griefing the core flow while still letting composability extensions run.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Apache 2.0 for RefundProtocol

div class="card-description"
codeRefundProtocol.sol/code is Circle's audited implementation, vendored in-tree under
Apache 2.0 — not a rewrite. The point is to reuse the audit, not re-audit.

!-- Contracts --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The Catalogue

## Every contract, file by file.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Source: codecontracts/src//code. Purpose statements are quoted or paraphrased from each
file's NatSpec.

!-- SardisLedgerAnchor --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisLedgerAnchor.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"contracts/src/SardisLedgerAnchor.sol · Solidity ^0.8.20

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"Production/span
span class="cb-tag"Ownable/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
Stores Merkle roots of audit-log batches for tamper evidence. Minimal by design — the
ledger itself stays off-chain in Postgres; only the root crosses the chain boundary.
codeanchor(root, anchorId)/code writes once, never twice; codeverify(root)/code
returns the timestamp (0 if never anchored). This is the contract that makes "trust our
logs" obsolete.

dl class="defs"
dtKey API
ddcodeanchor(bytes32 root, string anchorId)/code · codeverify(bytes32) → uint256 timestamp/code

dtEvent
ddcodeAnchored(bytes32 indexed root, string anchorId, uint256 timestamp)/code

dtUpstream
ddcodesardis-ledger/code batches → on-chain call from ops service.

/dl

!-- SardisIdentityRegistry --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisIdentityRegistry.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.24 · implements ERC-8004 Identity

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-8004/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-721/span
span class="cb-tag"EIP-712/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-1271/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
ERC-8004 Identity Registry — on-chain agent identity as an ERC-721 token. Global addressing:
codeeip155:{chainId}:{registryAddress}#{agentId}/code. Three coderegister()/code
overloads (URI + metadata, URI only, bare). Agent-wallet linking verified via EIP-712
signature (EOA) or ERC-1271 (smart contract). Wallet is cleared on token transfer so the new
owner has to re-verify.

dl class="defs"
dtKey API
ddcoderegister(...)/code · codesetAgentWallet(agentId, wallet, deadline, sig)/code · codesetMetadata(agentId, key, value)/code

dtInvariant
ddReserved key codeagentWallet/code cannot be written via codesetMetadata/code — only via signed flow.

dtSpec
dda href="https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8004/a

/dl

!-- SardisReputationRegistry --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisReputationRegistry.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.24 · implements ERC-8004 Reputation

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-8004/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
Client-submitted on-chain feedback for agents. Each feedback is codeint128 value/code +
decimals + tags + endpoint + off-chain URI + hash. Revocation is soft (marked, never
deleted). Agent owners (or approved operators) can submit response records pointing at
their side of the story.

dl class="defs"
dtRules
ddSubmitter MUST NOT be the agent owner; valueDecimals in [0, 18]; only original submitter can revoke.

dtLinked to
ddcodeSardisIdentityRegistry/code (ERC-721) — feedback keyed by codeagentId/code.

/dl

!-- SardisValidationRegistry --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisValidationRegistry.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.24 · implements ERC-8004 Validation

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-8004/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
Agent owners request validation from a designated validator; the validator responds with a
score (0–100). Records are queryable by coderequestHash/code. Validators can respond
multiple times to update the score.

dl class="defs"
dtRecord shape
ddcodevalidatorAddress/code · codeagentId/code · coderesponse (uint8)/code · coderesponseHash/code · codetag/code · codelastUpdate/code.

dtImmutable
ddcodeidentityRegistry/code address is set at construction.

/dl

!-- SardisJobManager --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisJobManager.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.24 · implements ERC-8183

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-8183/span
span class="cb-tag"Escrow/span
span class="cb-tag"ReentrancyGuard/span
span class="cb-tag"Pausable/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
Escrow-based job primitive for agentic commerce. Full ERC-8183 state machine:
codeOpen → Funded → Submitted → Completed/Rejected/Expired/code with
codeclaimRefund/code on expiry. Hook dispatch through codeIACPHook/code — a
strongbefore/strong hook called with codeHOOK_GAS_LIMIT/code whose revert blocks the
action, and a fire-and-forget strongafter/strong hook whose revert is ignored. Bounded
gas prevents hook-griefing.

dl class="defs"
dtState machine
ddOpen → Funded (fund) · Open → Rejected (reject) · Funded → Submitted (submit) · Submitted → Completed (complete) · Submitted → Rejected (reject) · Funded/Submitted → Expired (claimRefund).

dtCaps (MVP)
dd1% fee cap, USDC-only, trust hooks disabled post-launch — relaxable post-audit.

dtSpec
dda href="https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8183" target="_blank" rel="noopener"eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-8183/a

/dl

!-- SardisJobRegistry --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisJobRegistry.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.24

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"Reputation/span
span class="cb-tag"Ownable/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
On-chain reputation store for ERC-8183 agents. Tracks completed / rejected jobs, earnings,
spending per agent. Only authorized writers (the Job Manager, trusted hooks) can update
records. Trust score is computed on-chain in basis points (0–10000):
codebaseScore = completed / (completed + rejected) × 10000/code + up to a 10% volume
bonus capped at 10000.

dl class="defs"
dtAuth model
ddcodeauthorizedWriters/code mapping — only owner can toggle.

dtCompanion of
ddcodeSardisJobManager/code — Manager calls into Registry on every complete/reject.

/dl

!-- RefundProtocol --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"RefundProtocol.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.24 · Circle, vendored

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag"Apache 2.0/span
span class="cb-tag"Circle (audited)/span
span class="cb-tag"EIP-712/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
Circle's codeRefundProtocol/code, vendored in-tree. Time-locked payments (up to 180
days) with arbiter-enabled early withdrawal authorised by EIP-712 signed
codeEarlyWithdrawalByArbiter/code messages. Sardis uses it for dispute-resolved refunds
so neither buyer nor merchant has to trust Sardis custody.

dl class="defs"
dtPayment struct
ddcodeto · amount · releaseTimestamp · refundTo · withdrawnAmount · refunded/code.

dtMax lockup
dd180 days (codeMAX_LOCKUP_SECONDS/code).

dtWhy in-tree
ddPin version under Sardis build/audit; swap upstream fixes with explicit review.

/dl

!-- SardisPolicyModule (DEPRECATED) --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisPolicyModule.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.20 · Safe module (reference only)

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag warn"Deprecated/span
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"Safe module/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
Safe module that enforces spending policies for AI agent wallets — per-tx + daily limits,
co-sign mode (10× limit when co-signed by Sardis), merchant allow/deny lists, token
allowlist, pause/unpause.
strongSuperseded/strong by the pre-deployed, audited Zodiac Roles module at
code0x9646fDAD06d3e24444381f44362a3B0eB343D337/code. Kept for reference; not deployed to
mainnet for MVP (no custom audit dependency).

dl class="defs"
dtStatus
ddDo-not-deploy. Any agent-wallet policy enforcement routes through Zodiac Roles.

/dl

!-- SardisVerifyingPaymaster (DEPRECATED) --
div class="contract-block"
div class="cb-head"
div
div class="cb-name"SardisVerifyingPaymaster.sol

div class="text-xs text-muted text-mono"Solidity ^0.8.20 · ERC-4337 paymaster (reference only)

div class="cb-tags"
span class="cb-tag warn"Deprecated/span
span class="cb-tag"MIT/span
span class="cb-tag"ERC-4337/span

div class="cb-body"
div class="cb-purpose"
ERC-4337 verifying paymaster with wallet allowlist, per-op cap, and daily sponsor cap.
Validation optionally consumes off-chain verifier signatures in codepaymasterData/code.
strongSuperseded/strong by Circle Paymaster
(code0x0578cFB241215b77442a541325d6A4E6dFE700Ec/code, permissionless, USDC gas). Kept
for reference; not deployed.

dl class="defs"
dtToday
ddPrimary: Circle Paymaster. Backup: Pimlico (sponsor model).

/dl

!-- Supporting directories --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Supporting dirs

## Hooks, interfaces, verifiers.

table class="data"
thead
trthPath
thRole

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesrc/hooks/SardisReputationHook.sol/code
tdIACPHook impl that writes into codeSardisJobRegistry/code on complete/reject — keeps reputation accounting in-contract.

trtdcodesrc/hooks/SardisTrustHook.sol/code
tdIACPHook impl gating ERC-8183 actions on a trust-score threshold from the reputation registry.

trtdcodesrc/interfaces/IACPHook.sol/code
tdSelector-based hook dispatch interface — beforeAction, afterAction.

trtdcodesrc/interfaces/IIdentityRegistry.sol/code
tdERC-8004 Identity Registry interface.

trtdcodesrc/interfaces/IReputationRegistry.sol/code
tdERC-8004 Reputation Registry interface.

trtdcodesrc/interfaces/IValidationRegistry.sol/code
tdERC-8004 Validation Registry interface.

trtdcodesrc/interfaces/IJob.sol/code
tdERC-8183 job interface — events, errors, state enum.

trtdcodesrc/interfaces/IJobRegistry.sol/code
tdReputation-writer interface for job outcomes.

trtdcodesrc/verifiers/FundingSufficiencyVerifier.sol/code
tdZK-proof verifier: "agent wallet has at least X without revealing balance."

trtdcodesrc/verifiers/IdentityProofVerifier.sol/code
tdZK-proof verifier for identity attestation without doxxing the agent.

trtdcodesrc/verifiers/MandateComplianceVerifier.sol/code
tdZK-proof verifier proving a transaction complies with a mandate without leaking the mandate body.

/tbody
/table

!-- Interaction diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Interaction

## How the contracts compose.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A job lifecycle touches identity, reputation, the job manager, and — when off-chain evidence
needs to be proven later — the ledger anchor.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
subgraph Identity[ERC-8004 Identity]
IR[SardisIdentityRegistry
ERC-721 agent token]
RR[SardisReputationRegistry
client feedback]
VR[SardisValidationRegistry
validator scores]
end
subgraph Commerce[ERC-8183 Commerce]
JM[SardisJobManager
escrow + state machine]
JR[SardisJobRegistry
on-chain reputation]
HK[Hooks
Reputation · Trust]
end
subgraph Refund[Dispute]
RP[RefundProtocol
Circle, Apache 2.0]
end
subgraph Evidence[Audit]
LA[SardisLedgerAnchor
Merkle roots]
end
IR -- RR
IR -- VR
IR -. agent id .- JM
JM -- HK
HK -- JR
JM -. dispute .- RP
OFF[sardis-ledger
off-chain batches] -- LA

[/diagram]

!-- Build / test --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Build & test

## Toolchain.

table class="data"
thead
trthWhat
thCommand
thNotes

/thead
tbody
trtdCompile
tdcodecd contracts && forge build/code
tdFoundry; Solidity 0.8.20–0.8.24.

trtdTest
tdcodecd contracts && forge test/code
tdFuzz + invariant tests for Job state machine + anchor.

trtdDeps
tdcodelib/forge-std/code · codelib/openzeppelin-contracts/code
tdBoth vendored as git submodules under codecontracts/lib//code.

trtdDeploy script
tdcodescripts/deploy-contracts.sh/code
tdPublic contract deployment helper with dry-run defaults and explicit broadcast confirmation for mainnet.

trtdDeploy target
tdBase mainnet (primary), Tempo mainnet
tdNeeds deployer wallet with ~$2–5 ETH on Base, Alchemy API key, BaseScan API key.

/tbody
/table

!-- External contracts Sardis depends on --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"External

## Audited contracts we rely on but don't deploy.

table class="data"
thead
trthContract
thAddress
thWhy we don't own it

/thead
tbody
trtdSafe Smart Accounts v1.4.1
tdCanonical per chain (pre-deployed)
td$100B+ TVL audit — wallet custody.

trtdZodiac Roles
tdcode0x9646fDAD06d3e24444381f44362a3B0eB343D337/code
tdAudited policy module; avoids Sardis-owned audit dependency.

trtdCircle Paymaster
tdcode0x0578cFB241215b77442a541325d6A4E6dFE700Ec/code
tdPermissionless USDC-as-gas; no deployment needed.

trtdCircle CCTP v2
tdUnified per chain (Circle)
tdNative cross-chain USDC burn/mint.

/tbody
/table

=====================================
# PAYMENT FLOW
https://canvas.sardis.sh/payment-flow
=====================================

Canvas title="07 · Payment Flow — Sardis Canvas" currentId="payment-flow"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 07 · Flows/span

## Payment Flow · end-to-end

p class="hero-lead"
One call — codesardis.pay(to, amount, reason)/code — traverses fourteen distinct stages
before it returns. This canvas walks every one, with the actual file behind it, the latency
budget, and what happens when that stage fails closed.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Every payment is four things in sequence: strongidentity/strong (who is calling),
strongapproval/strong (is this allowed by policy + compliance + mandate),
strongexecution/strong (get an MPC signature, broadcast), strongreceipt/strong (ledger
row, Merkle batch, webhook). Fail-closed at every gate. Budget ≈ 2s p99 excluding chain
confirmation.

!-- Sequence diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"One request

## The full sequence — every actor, every message.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
An agent running in LangChain calls codesardis.pay()/code. The sequence below is what
really happens. Each bubble maps to a step in the walkthrough below.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent (LangChain)
participant SDK as sardis-sdk-python
participant MW as FastAPI Middleware
participant RP as require_principal
participant O as PaymentOrchestrator
participant PP as PreExecutionPipeline
participant PV as MandateVerifier
participant CE as ChainExecutor
participant WM as WalletManager
participant TK as Turnkey MPC
participant RPC as Alchemy RPC
participant CH as Base / Tempo
participant LG as sardis-ledger
participant AN as LedgerAnchor.sol
participant OB as webhook outbox

A-SDK: client.payments.create(to, amount, reason)
SDK-MW: POST /v1/payments Authorization: Bearer sk_…
MW-MW: CORS · HSTS · CSP · rate limit (Redis)
MW-RP: extract principal (API key → agent/org)
RP-O: orchestrator.execute(intent)
O-PP: pipeline.run(intent, principal)
PP-PP: spending_policy ✓
PP-PP: KYC / AML (sardis-compliance) ✓
PP-PP: mandate delegation ✓
PP-PP: rate limit ✓
PP-PP: anomaly score ✓
PP-PV: AP2 / TAP / x402 chain ✓
PV--PP: verified
PP--O: ApprovedIntent
O-CE: execute(approved_intent)
CE-CE: choose chain + token + route
CE-WM: request signature (payload)
WM-TK: signPayload(org, sub-org, key)
TK--WM: signed tx
WM--CE: signed tx
CE-RPC: eth_sendRawTransaction
RPC-CH: broadcast
CH--RPC: tx hash
RPC--CE: receipt
CE--O: PaymentResult
O-LG: append ledger_entry + policy_decision
LG-AN: Merkle batch → anchor (async)
O-OB: enqueue webhook (outbox)
O--MW: 200 PaymentResult
MW--SDK: 200 JSON
SDK--A: { tx_hash, status, receipt }

[/diagram]

!-- Stage 1 — SDK call --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Stage 1 · Agent surface

## From Python line to HTTP request.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"01

div
div class="step-title"Agent calls codesardis.pay()/code or a framework tool.

div class="step-body"
In LangChain the agent invokes a codePayTool/code from
codesardis-langchain/toolkit.py/code. In the bare Python SDK the call is
codeclient.payments.create(to, amount, reason)/code. In MCP it's a
codesardis_pay/code tool call from Claude Desktop / Cursor. All paths converge on the
same REST call — no framework gets a privileged fast path.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-sdk-python/src/sardis_sdk/client.py/code/span
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-langchain/src/sardis_langchain/tools.py/code/span
span class="failmode"fail: raised on client; no network call yet/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"02

div
div class="step-title"SDK marshals request, attaches API key.

div class="step-body"
Client reads codeSARDIS_API_KEY/code, serializes the intent to JSON, sets
codeAuthorization: Bearer sk_live_…/code and an idempotency key. TypeScript SDK does
the same via code@sardis/sdk/code with Zod validation.

div class="step-meta"
spanauth: API key HMAC-hashed at rest (SHA-256)/span
spanidempotency: client-generated uuid, enforced DB-side/span
span class="failmode"fail: network retry with same idempotency key — safe/span

!-- Stage 2 — Gateway --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Stage 2 · API gateway

## FastAPI receives the request.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"03

div
div class="step-title"Middleware stack runs — CORS, CSP, HSTS, rate limit.

div class="step-body"
Request lands on Cloud Run, enters FastAPI composition root
(codesardis-api/main.py/code). Middleware applies canonical prod origins, CSP/HSTS,
and the Redis-backed rate limiter. Rate limiter is strongfail-closed/strong in prod —
if Redis is unreachable the request is denied.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/main.py/code/span
spanenv: codeSARDIS_REDIS_URL/code required/span
span class="failmode"fail: 429 on limit; 503 if Redis down/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"04

div
div class="step-title"coderequire_principal/code extracts identity.

div class="step-body"
Dependency resolver hashes the incoming API key, looks up the principal (org + agent +
scopes) from Postgres, attaches it to the request. Better-auth JWTs (EdDSA via JWKS)
take the same slot for dashboard-originated calls. Missing/invalid key → 401, no
downstream work.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/money_movement/pay.py/code/span
spanprincipal: org_id, agent_id, scopes[], key_id/span
span class="failmode"fail: 401 — no orchestrator invocation/span

!-- Stage 3 — Pre-exec pipeline --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Stage 3 · The single gate

## PaymentOrchestrator + PreExecutionPipeline.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every path (wallets, mandates, ap2, pay) funnels through these two files. This is the
"policy before execution" invariant at the code level.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"05

div
div class="step-title"codePaymentOrchestrator.execute()/code enters.

div class="step-body"
The orchestrator receives the intent + principal. Builds a codePipelineContext/code
containing chain, token, mandate refs (if any), agent card, trust graph snapshot. Calls
the pipeline.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/orchestrator.py/code/span
spaninvariant: codechain_executor/code is private — only orchestrator touches it/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"06

div
div class="step-title"Spending policy check.

div class="step-body"
Evaluates natural-language-derived rules against the intent — merchant allowlist, daily
cap, per-merchant cap, category block. Returns allow / deny / require_approval. Denial
ends the flow here with a reason code that's written to codepolicy_decisions/code.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/spending_policy.py/code/span
span class="failmode"fail-closed: missing policy → deny/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"07

div
div class="step-title"KYC / AML screen.

div class="step-body"
Hits codesardis-compliance/code which calls Didit (KYC status cached) + Elliptic
(AML/sanctions). Missing or expired KYC for the principal → deny. Sanctioned
counterparty → deny + SAR queue.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance/kyc.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis_compliance/pep.py/code, codesar.py/code/span
span class="failmode"fail-closed: compliance provider down → deny/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"08

div
div class="step-title"Mandate delegation check.

div class="step-body"
If the intent references a spending mandate, verifies the delegator's authority is still
valid, remaining budget covers the amount, and the mandate hasn't been revoked.
Decrements budget atomically via DB row lock.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/authority/mandate_delegation.py/code/span
spanSQL: codeSELECT … FOR UPDATE/code on mandate row/span
span class="failmode"fail-closed: race → NOWAIT lock, 409 to caller/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"09

div
div class="step-title"Rate limiter + anomaly score.

div class="step-body"
Per-agent + per-org buckets in Redis. Anomaly model (sardis-guardrails) scores the
intent against learned baselines — amount z-score, velocity, new counterparty, unusual
time-of-day. Score above threshold routes to approval queue instead of denying.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-guardrails/src/sardis_guardrails/rate_limiter.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/anomaly_detection.py/code/span
span class="failmode"fail: over threshold → approval queue, not denial/span

!-- Stage 4 — Protocol verification --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Stage 4 · Protocol verification

## If the request came in under a standard, verify the chain.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"10

div
div class="step-title"AP2 / TAP / x402 / A2A mandate chain verification.

div class="step-body"
If the intent carries a protocol envelope, the matching verifier walks the mandate chain —
Ed25519 / ECDSA-P256 sigs on Intent, Cart, Payment mandates; TTL; replay cache lookup in
Redis + Postgres archive. An unverifiable chain ends the flow.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-protocol/src/sardis_protocol/ap2.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis_protocol/x402_settlement.py/code, codeerc8122.py/code/span
spanreplay: codesardis_protocol/replay_cache_redis.py/code/span
span class="failmode"fail-closed: invalid sig → deny; replay → 409/span

!-- Stage 5 — Execution --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Stage 5 · Execution

## Chain selection, MPC signing, broadcast.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"11

div
div class="step-title"codeChainExecutor/code picks chain + token + route.

div class="step-body"
Reads the request's target chain (default "base"), selects the token (USDC / EURC /
PYUSD), and picks a route — direct transfer, CCTP bridge, paymaster-sponsored. Tempo
has its own dedicated executor.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/smart_router.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis-chain/src/sardis_v2_chain/executor.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis_chain/tempo/executor.py/code/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"12

div
div class="step-title"WalletManager requests an MPC signature.

div class="step-body"
codesardis-wallet/manager.py/code hands the payload to the active custody provider
(Turnkey default). For Turnkey: organization + sub-org + private key reference. The
key never leaves the provider — Sardis receives a signed transaction, not a key.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/manager.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis_wallet/tempo_keychain.py/code, codecircle_client.py/code/span
span class="failmode"fail-closed: provider down → 503, no broadcast/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"13

div
div class="step-title"Broadcast via Alchemy RPC.

div class="step-body"
Signed transaction submitted to codeSARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL/code (env-driven, Alchemy
primary, public RPC fallback). Tempo uses coderpc.tempo.xyz/code. Executor watches
for the receipt with bounded retries.

div class="step-meta"
spanprovider: Alchemy (Base/Ethereum/Polygon/Arbitrum/Optimism)/span
spanprovider: Tempo public RPC/span
span class="failmode"fail: receipt timeout → mark pending, reconciler will finalize/span

!-- Stage 6 — Receipt + audit --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Stage 6 · Receipt + audit

## Ledger row, Merkle anchor, webhook.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"14

div
div class="step-title"Ledger entry + policy decision rows written.

div class="step-body"
A single transaction writes codeledger_entry/code (the movement) plus
codepolicy_decision/code (why it was allowed) in Postgres. Both rows reference each
other. This is the evidence base for disputes and regulator queries.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-ledger/src/sardis_ledger/models.py/code/span
spanfile: codesardis_ledger/hybrid.py/code (ImmuDB optional)/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"15

div
div class="step-title"Merkle batch → on-chain anchor.

div class="step-body"
A background job rolls recent ledger entries into a Merkle tree and anchors the root
via codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code. After anchoring, any ledger row can be proven
to have existed at or before a given block.

div class="step-meta"
spanfile: codepackages/sardis-ledger/src/sardis_ledger/anchor.py/code/span
spancontract: codecontracts/src/SardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code/span
spanasync: batched, ~5 min cadence in prod/span

div class="step"
div class="step-num"16

div
div class="step-title"Webhook enqueued via outbox; SDK returns receipt.

div class="step-body"
Response assembled (codePaymentResult/code), returned up the stack. Webhook for
codepayment.succeeded/code / codepayment.denied/code enters the outbox table —
delivered at-least-once by the outbox worker with exponential backoff and event-id
dedup.

div class="step-meta"
spantable: codewebhook_outbox/code/span
spandedup: codeevent_id/code uniqueness + delivery tracking/span
span class="failmode"fail: delivery 5xx → retry with backoff, alert after N failures/span

!-- Latency budget --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Latency budget

## Where the 2-second p99 budget goes.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Indicative p99 figures, excluding chain confirmation. Agents wait for the HTTP response;
chain finality is tracked asynchronously and surfaced via webhook.

table class="data"
thead
trthStage
thp50
thp99
thNotes

/thead
tbody
trtd01–02 · SDK marshal
td1 ms
td3 ms
tdLocal JSON + HTTP; ignored at SLO level.

trtd03 · Middleware + rate limit
td5 ms
td25 ms
tdRedis round-trip dominates.

trtd04 · require_principal
td8 ms
td30 ms
tdPostgres principal lookup (cached after first hit).

trtd06–09 · Pre-exec pipeline
td40 ms
td180 ms
tdPolicy eval + compliance cache + anomaly score.

trtd10 · Protocol verify (if AP2/TAP)
td12 ms
td60 ms
tdSignature verify + replay lookup.

trtd11 · Chain selection
td3 ms
td10 ms
tdPure Python; no I/O.

trtd12 · MPC signature (Turnkey)
td350 ms
td900 ms
tdLargest single slice. Cold org-auth worse.

trtd13 · Broadcast (eth_sendRaw)
td120 ms
td400 ms
tdAlchemy p99; confirmation is async.

trtd14 · Ledger write
td12 ms
td40 ms
tdSingle Postgres tx.

trtd15 · Merkle anchor
tdasync
tdasync
tdBackground job; not on the hot path.

trtd16 · Webhook enqueue
td3 ms
td10 ms
tdInsert into outbox only.

trtdstrongTotal (hot path)/strong
tdstrong~560 ms/strong
tdstrong~1.9 s/strong
tdWithin the 2s p99 SLO; MPC dominates.

/tbody
/table

!-- Failure modes --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Failure behaviour

## What happens when each stage fails.

table class="data"
thead
trthStage
thMode
thObservable effect

/thead
tbody
trtdRate limit
tdfail-closed
td429; if Redis unreachable in prod → 503 (no silent pass-through).

trtdPrincipal lookup
tdfail-closed
td401; no orchestrator entry.

trtdSpending policy
tdfail-closed
td403 with reason code; codepolicy_decision/code row written.

trtdCompliance (Didit/Elliptic)
tdfail-closed
td403; override only via codeSARDIS_AGIT_FAIL_OPEN=true/code (dev only).

trtdMandate
tdfail-closed
td409 on race (NOWAIT); 403 on insufficient budget.

trtdAnomaly
tdfail-soft
tdAbove threshold → approval queue, HTTP 202 codepending_approval/code.

trtdProtocol verify
tdfail-closed
td403 invalid sig; 409 replay.

trtdMPC provider
tdfail-closed
td503; no broadcast, no ledger row.

trtdBroadcast
tdfail-open to reconciler
tdMarks pending; reconciliation queue finalizes.

trtdLedger write
tdfail-closed
tdWhole request rolled back; response 500. Broadcast + ledger share a DB tx where possible.

trtdAnchor
tdeventual
tdAsync; delayed batches alerted via ops channel.

trtdWebhook delivery
tdat-least-once
tdOutbox retries with backoff; 5xx after N attempts alerts operator.

/tbody
/table

!-- Who does what --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Package ownership

## Which package owns each stage.

dl class="defs"
dtAgent surface
ddsardis-sdk-python · sardis-sdk-js · sardis-langchain (+ other adapters)

dtGateway
ddsardis-api · middleware + routers

dtOrchestration
ddsardis-core · orchestrator.py + pre_execution_pipeline.py

dtPolicy + compliance
ddsardis-core · spending_policy.py; sardis-compliance; sardis-guardrails

dtProtocol verification
ddsardis-protocol · ap2.py · x402_settlement.py · erc8122.py · replay_cache_redis.py

dtChain execution
ddsardis-chain · executor.py · smart_router.py · tempo/executor.py

dtWallet + MPC
ddsardis-wallet · manager.py · tempo_keychain.py · circle_client.py

dtAudit
ddsardis-ledger · anchor.py · immutable.py · hybrid.py · plus SardisLedgerAnchor.sol

/dl
