/Canvas


=====================================
# POLICY ENGINE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/policy-engine
=====================================

Canvas title="08 · Policy Engine — Sardis Canvas" currentId="policy-engine"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 08 · Flows/span

## Policy Engine

p class="hero-lead"
The hard part of agent payments isn't access to money — it's controlling authority over money.
The policy engine is Sardis's answer: natural-language intent → structured rule → enforced on every
transaction, logged with a reason code, anchored on-chain. This canvas walks the whole pipeline.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
codeSpendingPolicy.evaluate()/code in
codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/spending_policy.py/code runs twelve checks in a fixed
order; the first failure short-circuits with a reason code. Above it sits a natural-language parser
(Groq Llama 3.3 70B, regex fallback). Below it sits an approval queue, a decision log, a simulation
sandbox, and — for mandates that clear the gate — on-chain enforcement via ERC-8183.

!-- Thesis --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Thesis

## Why "authority" — not "access" — is the problem.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Giving an agent a wallet takes ten minutes. Giving an agent a wallet it emcannot misuse/em
is the actual product. Every design choice in this engine follows from that.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Access is easy. Revocation is hard.

div class="card-description"
An MPC signer solves "the agent can pay." It does nothing about "the agent decided to pay
$40K to a phishing address at 3am because a jailbreak prompt told it to." Policy is the layer
that says no on the agent's behalf, embefore/em the signer is even asked.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Authority must be structured, not trusted.

div class="card-description"
Natural language ("$500/day on API credits, openai.com only") is the eminput/em, not the
enforcement. The parser converts intent into a typed codeSpendingPolicy/code dataclass with
hard caps, allowlists, time windows — data structures that can be evaluated in 1ms on every tx.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Fail-closed, with a reason.

div class="card-description"
Every denial returns a machine-readable reason code
(codeper_transaction_limit/code, codemerchant_denied/code, codegoal_drift_exceeded/code).
No silent rejects, no vague "declined." Agents, operators, and regulators see the same root cause.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Evidence beats logs.

div class="card-description"
Every allow and every deny writes a row to the decision log. Batches are Merkle-rooted and
anchored by codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code. "Trust our logs" is not a defense; a Merkle
proof is.

!-- NL → structured --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Pipeline

## Natural language → structured policy.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Operators type English. Agents hit a typed dataclass. The parser bridges the two and is
implemented twice — once as an LLM call, once as a regex fallback — so the stack never hard-fails
on a missing API key.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Input (what the operator types)

precodeSpend up to $500/day on API credits,
only to api.openai.com and api.anthropic.com.
Require human approval above $200.
Block gambling (MCC 7995) and adult (MCC 5967)./code

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Output (what the engine sees)

div class="json-block"SpendingPolicy(
agent_id = "agent_123",
trust_level = TrustLevel.MEDIUM,
limit_per_tx = Decimal("200"),
daily_limit = TimeWindowLimit(
limit = Decimal("500"),
window = "daily",
scope = SpendingScope.COMPUTE,
),
merchant_rules = [
MerchantRule(merchant_id="api.openai.com", allowed=True),
MerchantRule(merchant_id="api.anthropic.com", allowed=True),
],
blocked_merchant_categories = ["gambling", "adult"],
approval_threshold = Decimal("200"),
)

div class="grid grid-2 mt-24"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Primary: Groq Llama 3.3 70B

div class="card-description"
codeNLPolicyParser/code in codenl_policy_parser.py/code. Uses
codellama-3.3-70b-versatile/code via the Groq OpenAI-compatible endpoint
(codehttps://api.groq.com/openai/v1/code). ~200ms typical; open-source model so there's no
proprietary lock-in on the parsing layer itself. Falls through to OpenAI if
codeGROQ_API_KEY/code is unset and codeOPENAI_API_KEY/code is present.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Fallback: regex parser

div class="card-description"
codeRegexPolicyParser/code in the same file. Pattern-matches common phrasings
("$X/day", "only to Y", "block Z") so policy creation still works in airgapped / sandbox
environments with no LLM key. Less expressive, but never hallucinates a field. Invariants live
in codeImmutableParserHardLimits/code — hard ceilings the LLM can't raise.

!-- Dimensions --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Dimensions

## What a policy can actually control.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Each field on codeSpendingPolicy/code is a lever. Most deployments only touch three or four;
the rest exist for edge cases that show up at scale.

table class="data"
thead
trthDimension
thField on SpendingPolicy
thWhat it controls

/thead
tbody
trtdPer-transaction cap
tdcodelimit_per_tx/code
tdMax single payment. Category-specific overrides via code_get_effective_per_tx_limit()/code.

trtdLifetime cap
tdcodelimit_total/code + codespent_total/code
tdCumulative ceiling across the policy's lifetime.

trtdDaily / weekly / monthly
tdcodedaily_limit/code, codeweekly_limit/code, codemonthly_limit/code
tdcodeTimeWindowLimit/code objects — rolling windows, race-safe reads from codepolicy_store/code.

trtdMerchant allow / block
tdcodemerchant_rules/code
tdPer-merchant caps, category matching. Case-insensitive — deliberate, prevents casing-trick bypass.

trtdMCC blocking
tdcodeblocked_merchant_categories/code
td4-digit Merchant Category Codes (e.g. 7995 gambling, 5967 adult).

trtdScope restriction
tdcodeallowed_scopes/code
tdcodeSpendingScope/code enum: retail, compute, agent_to_agent, etc. codeALL/code is the wildcard.

trtdChain / token allowlist
tdcodeallowed_chains/code, codeallowed_tokens/code
tdRestrict which rails a policy authorizes (e.g. "Base + USDC only").

trtdDestination allowlist
tdcodeallowed_destination_addresses/code / codeblocked_destination_addresses/code
tdHard address pins — for treasury sweeps, known suppliers.

trtdApproval threshold
tdcodeapproval_threshold/code
tdAbove this amount, route to human queue instead of auto-approving.

trtdGoal-drift guard
tdcodemax_drift_score/code
tdIf the agent has drifted from its stated task (codegoal_drift_detector.py/code), deny.

trtdKYA attestation gate
td(via codekya_client/code arg)
tdRequire on-chain agent identity attestation before high-value flows.

trtdMerchant trust
td(via codemerchant_trust_service/code)
tdFirst-seen or low-trust merchants get extra scrutiny regardless of other rules.

trtdPre-auth hold
tdcoderequire_preauth/code, codemax_hold_hours/code
tdForce a two-step "authorize then capture" instead of a single settlement.

/tbody
/table

!-- Evaluation order --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Evaluation

## Twelve checks, fixed order, first fail wins.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
The order in codeSpendingPolicy.evaluate()/code is not arbitrary — cheap local checks run
first, DB and on-chain lookups later. Short-circuiting saves latency on the common "just blatantly
over the limit" case.

div class="check-list"
div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Amount validation
div class="check-desc"codeamount 0/code and codefee = 0/code. Reason codes: codeamount_must_be_positive/code, codefee_must_be_non_negative/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Scope allowed
div class="check-desc"codescope ∈ allowed_scopes/code (or codeALL/code). Reason: codescope_not_allowed/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"MCC not blocked
div class="check-desc"code_check_mcc_policy(mcc_code)/code — 4-digit category screen.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Per-transaction limit
div class="check-desc"Effective cap = min(category override, trust-tier cap, global cap). Reason: codeper_transaction_limit/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Lifetime cap
div class="check-desc"From codepolicy_store/code in prod, in-memory in dev. Reason: codetotal_limit_exceeded/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Time-window limits
div class="check-desc"Daily / weekly / monthly rolling windows. Reasons: codedaily_limit_exceeded/code, codeweekly_limit_exceeded/code, codemonthly_limit_exceeded/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"On-chain balance
div class="check-desc"If coderpc_client/code provided — is the wallet actually fundable? Reason: codeinsufficient_balance/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Merchant rules
div class="check-desc"Allowlist, blocklist, per-merchant caps. Reason: codemerchant_denied/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Goal drift
div class="check-desc"codedrift_score max_drift_score/code → deny. Reason: codegoal_drift_exceeded/code.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Merchant trust
div class="check-desc"First-seen or low-trust merchants fail unless the policy explicitly allows unknown destinations.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"Approval threshold
div class="check-desc"If codeamount ≥ approval_threshold/code: return code(True, "requires_approval")/code and hand off to approval queue.

div class="check-item"divdiv class="check-title"KYA attestation
div class="check-desc"For high-value paths, verify on-chain agent identity via codekya_client/code. No attestation → deny.

!-- Decision tree --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Decision tree

## What evaluate() looks like as a flowchart.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart TD
A[Payment request] -- B{amount 0 & fee = 0?}
B -- no -- D1[deny: amount_must_be_positive]
B -- yes -- C{scope allowed?}
C -- no -- D2[deny: scope_not_allowed]
C -- yes -- D{MCC blocked?}
D -- yes -- D3[deny: mcc_blocked]
D -- no -- E{per-tx cap OK?}
E -- no -- D4[deny: per_transaction_limit]
E -- yes -- F{lifetime cap OK?}
F -- no -- D5[deny: total_limit_exceeded]
F -- yes -- G{time-window OK?}
G -- no -- D6[deny: daily/weekly/monthly_limit_exceeded]
G -- yes -- H{balance sufficient?}
H -- no -- D7[deny: insufficient_balance]
H -- yes -- I{merchant allowed?}
I -- no -- D8[deny: merchant_denied]
I -- yes -- J{drift max?}
J -- no -- D9[deny: goal_drift_exceeded]
J -- yes -- K{amount &ge; approval_threshold?}
K -- yes -- L[requires_approval → queue]
K -- no -- M{KYA attested?}
M -- no -- D10[deny: kya_required]
M -- yes -- N[ALLOW → orchestrator → signer]

[/diagram]

!-- Reason codes --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Reason codes

## Every denial is explainable.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
These strings are the contract between engine, operator dashboard, and the decision log. Don't
invent new ones casually — they're indexed, alerted on, and quoted in incident reports.

table class="data"
theadtrthReason code
thMeaning
thTypical fix

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeOK/code
tdAllowed.
td—

trtdcoderequires_approval/code
tdAllowed if an approver signs off.
tdRoute to codeApprovalService/code.

trtdcodeamount_must_be_positive/code
tdBad input.
tdSDK-level validation should catch first.

trtdcodefee_must_be_non_negative/code
tdBad input.
tdGas estimator bug — investigate.

trtdcodescope_not_allowed/code
tdSpending category not in allowlist.
tdExpand codeallowed_scopes/code or re-route request.

trtdcodemcc_blocked/code
tdMerchant category blocked (e.g. gambling).
tdConfirm with operator before unblocking.

trtdcodeper_transaction_limit/code
tdSingle payment above cap.
tdRaise codelimit_per_tx/code or split payment.

trtdcodetotal_limit_exceeded/code
tdLifetime ceiling hit.
tdTop up codelimit_total/code or rotate policy.

trtdcodedaily_limit_exceeded/code
tdDaily window exhausted.
tdWait for reset or raise window.

trtdcodeinsufficient_balance/code
tdWallet can't cover.
tdFund wallet or route via CCTP.

trtdcodemerchant_denied/code
tdMerchant blocked or not on allowlist.
tdReview allowlist.

trtdcodegoal_drift_exceeded/code
tdAgent off-task per drift detector.
tdInvestigate agent — potential jailbreak or goal-hijack.

trtdcodekya_required/code
tdNo on-chain agent attestation.
tdRun KYA onboarding for the agent.

/tbody
/table

!-- Mandates + ERC-8183 --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"On-chain enforcement

## Spending mandates — from a Python object to an EVM guard.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
A policy is off-chain: Sardis trusts itself. A emspending mandate/em is the same policy
cryptographically committed — first to Sardis's append-only log via AGIT, then to an on-chain
guard via ERC-8183. If Sardis is compromised, the chain still enforces the cap.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Off-chain: AGIT policy chain

div class="card-description"
codeAgitPolicyEngine/code in codeagit_policy_engine.py/code. Every
codecommit_policy()/code hashes the policy and links it to the prior commit, forming a
tamper-evident chain. codeverify_policy_chain(agent_id)/code proves the current policy
descends from the original — a policy flip doesn't go unnoticed.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"On-chain: ERC-8183 guard

div class="card-description"
codeERC8183PolicyGuard/code in codeerc8183_policy_guard.py/code. Before funding or
settlement, the guard calls codeevaluate_funding/code / codesubmit_funding/code /
codesubmit_settlement/code — the solidity-side hooks reject if the on-chain policy record
doesn't match. Caps currently enforced: 1% fee ceiling, USDC-only, trust hooks disabled
(post-audit relaxation path).

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Why both?

div class="card-description"
Off-chain policy runs on every request — cheap, flexible, covers all rails including fiat.
On-chain policy is the last line when counterparty trust in Sardis drops to zero (regulator,
enterprise treasury, dispute). They agree in the common case; they diverge only when Sardis
itself is the attacker, and the on-chain side wins.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"ERC-20 hook surface

div class="card-description"
The guard publishes three evaluation points on-chain:
codeevaluate_funding/code (pre-transfer sanity), codesubmit_funding/code (commits
the intent to a job), codesubmit_settlement/code (finalizes the settlement hash).
Each raises codeERC8183PolicyDenied/code on mismatch.

!-- Delegation --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Delegation

## Parent → child mandate trees.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Real agent systems are multi-agent. A team lead agent hires helpers; a workflow fans out to
sub-tasks. Each child needs spending authority — but strictly less than the parent's, never more.
That's what codemandate_tree.py/code enforces.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"MandateTreeNode

div class="card-description"
A node holds the mandate (limits, scopes, expiry) plus a list of children. codeis_leaf()/code
and codetotal_delegated()/code let callers reason about the tree without walking it manually.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"MandateTreeValidator.validate_delegation()

div class="card-description"
Invariants: child codelimit_per_tx/code ≤ parent's; child codetotal_delegated ≤ parent.limit_total/code;
child expiry ≤ parent expiry; scope must be a subset. Violation → deny. This is the "can't
issue more authority than you have" rule, enforced structurally.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"build_mandate_tree()

div class="card-description"
Reconstructs the full tree from the DB for audit and visualization. The dashboard's mandate
view reads from this; investigators reading a decision log can reconstruct the delegation
chain that authorized a specific payment.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Revocation cascades

div class="card-description"
Revoking a parent mandate invalidates every descendant at the next evaluation. No "zombie
sub-agents kept spending after the main agent was killed" story. This matters for incident
response.

!-- Approvals --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Approvals

## When policy says "maybe."

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
A request above codeapproval_threshold/code isn't denied — it's queued. A human operator,
a second agent, or a group vote can release it. codeApprovalService/code in
codeapproval_service.py/code owns the lifecycle.

table class="data"
theadtrthMethod
thWhat it does

/thead
tbody
trtdcodecreate_approval()/code
tdOpens an approval record tied to the pending mandate. Notifies via codeApprovalNotifier/code (Slack / email / webhook).

trtdcodeapprove(approval_id, approver_id)/code
tdMarks approved; orchestrator resumes execution on next poll or event.

trtdcodedeny(approval_id, reason)/code
tdHard deny — decision log captures the human reason. The mandate is archived, not executable.

trtdcodecancel(approval_id)/code
tdWithdrawn by the agent (e.g. order was rescinded upstream).

trtdcodeexpire_pending()/code
tdCron-called. Approvals have a TTL — stale requests don't execute days later when no one remembers.

trtdcodelist_pending()/code
tdPowers the operator dashboard's approval inbox.

/tbody
/table

!-- Decision log --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Decision log

## Every allow, every deny, one row.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
The policy decision log is separate from the payment ledger — it captures policy outcomes
whether or not a payment ever executed. A denied request still has to be evidenced: that's how
the agent owner proves to a regulator that the system was working, not failing.

dl class="defs"
dtTable
ddcodepolicy_decisions/code (see canvas 05 · Database).

dtRows written when
ddEvery call into codeSpendingPolicy.evaluate()/code, plus every approval transition.

dtFields
ddagent_id, mandate_id, reason_code, amount, merchant, chain, token, decided_at, approver_id (if any), evidence_hash.

dtAnchoring
ddBatched into Merkle roots by codesardis-ledger/code, anchored via codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code.

dtExport
ddcodeevidence_export/code router produces signed, proof-bearing CSV/JSON for audits.

/dl

!-- Simulation --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Simulation

## Test a policy before it enforces anything.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Policy changes are risky: tighten too much, block legitimate spending; loosen too much, invite
incidents. The codepolicy_simulation/code router
(codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/policy/policy_simulation.py/code) replays historical
traffic against a proposed policy and returns what would have changed — allows that became denies,
denies that became allows, new approval routes.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Replay

div class="card-description"
Takes a time range of real mandates, runs them through the draft policy without touching
wallets or chains. Output: diff of outcomes, broken down by reason code.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"What-if

div class="card-description"
Operator uploads a synthetic batch ("ten $150 API spends, two $2K one-offs"). Engine returns
the full evaluation trail — same twelve checks, no side effects.

!-- Kill switch --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Incident response

## Kill switch — the big red button.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
When something is clearly wrong ("every agent is suddenly trying to pay the same new address"),
the operator needs one action that stops everything. The emergency router is that action.

dl class="defs"
dtRouter
ddcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/operations/emergency.py/code

dtDependency
ddcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/kill_switch_dep.py/code — enforced on every write path.

dtEffect
ddGlobal flag flips; codeSpendingPolicy.evaluate()/code short-circuits to deny with reason codekill_switch_active/code; no new mandates accept; in-flight signatures are aborted at the wallet manager.

dtScope modes
ddGlobal, per-tenant, per-agent, per-chain — you don't always want to freeze everyone.

dtAudit
ddActivation, reason, operator identity, and deactivation all log to the decision stream.

dtRecovery
ddManual — intentionally. Restoring spending after a kill requires explicit operator action, not a timeout.

/dl

!-- Key files --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Files to open when you want to dig in.

table class="data"
theadtrthFile
thRole

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/spending_policy.py/code
tdThe engine. codeSpendingPolicy/code, codeTimeWindowLimit/code, codeMerchantRule/code, codeevaluate()/code.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/nl_policy_parser.py/code
tdNatural-language → typed policy. Groq primary, OpenAI fallback, regex last resort.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/agit_policy_engine.py/code
tdAGIT: commits, chain verification, policy history, diff across versions.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/erc8183_policy_guard.py/code
tdOn-chain guard — evaluate/submit funding + settlement.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/approval_service.py/code
tdApproval lifecycle: create, approve, deny, expire.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/mandate_tree.py/code
tdParent/child mandate invariants.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/goal_drift_detector.py/code
tdDrift scoring — input to the drift check.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/kya_trust_scoring.py/code
tdTrust tiers + per-tier caps merged into per-tx limit.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/policy/policy_simulation.py/code
tdWhat-if / replay endpoints.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/operations/emergency.py/code
tdKill-switch activation and status.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/kill_switch_dep.py/code
tdFastAPI dependency that enforces the flag on every write.

/tbody
/table

section class="section"

## Follow-ups.

div class="grid grid-2"

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# WALLET MPC
https://canvas.sardis.sh/wallet-mpc
=====================================

Canvas title="09 · Wallet + MPC — Sardis Canvas" currentId="wallet-mpc"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 09 · Flows/span

## Wallet + MPC

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis never holds a private key. Every signature is produced by an external MPC provider —
Turnkey by default, Fireblocks / Circle / Lit as swap-ins — behind a single abstract port.
This canvas maps the interface, the providers, and the signing path from orchestrator to chain.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
codeMPCSignerPort/code in codepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/executor.py/code is the
contract. codeTurnkeyMPCSigner/code, codeFailoverMPCSigner/code, and
codeLocalAccountSigner/code implement it. Wallet lifecycle is owned by
codeWalletManager/code in codesardis-wallet/manager.py/code. On EVM chains, wallets are
Safe Smart Accounts v1.4.1 (pre-deployed). On Tempo, signing is bespoke via
codeTempoKeychainProvider/code.

!-- Thesis --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Thesis

## "Non-custodial" is not a slogan — it's a blast-radius argument.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Keys never live in Sardis memory

div class="card-description"
Not in env vars, not in Redis, not in a service's process memory. Every signature is a remote
call to a provider (Turnkey, Fireblocks, Circle, Lit) that holds the key material in HSMs or
threshold-split shards. Sardis sees request → signature, never the key.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Blast radius analysis

div class="card-description"
If the API server is compromised: attacker gets request metadata, not funds. If the database
is exfiltrated: wallet addresses leak, keys don't. If an agent is prompt-injected: the signer
still only acts on policy-approved intents. Three independent breaches are required to move
money, which is exactly the point.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Pluggable by contract, not by config

div class="card-description"
The codeMPCSignerPort/code interface has three methods. Any provider that implements them
is a drop-in. Turnkey was default; Fireblocks/Circle/Lit are integrated; the port makes
migration a config change, not a refactor. Lock-in on custody would be the worst kind of lock-in.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Failover is a first-class concern

div class="card-description"
codeFailoverMPCSigner/code wraps an ordered list of providers: primary fails → next.
Custody providers occasionally have regional outages; we don't want a Turnkey incident to be a
Sardis incident. The port makes this tractable.

!-- MPC port --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Interface

## The signing port — three methods.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Any MPC provider that implements codeMPCSignerPort/code can drive Sardis. That's the whole
contract. Everything else — transaction envelopes, chain selection, ERC-4337 userOp hashing — is
handled by the executor before it hits the port.

precodeclass MPCSignerPort(ABC):
async def sign_transaction(
self, wallet_id: str, tx: TransactionRequest
) - str: ... # returns signed raw tx hex

async def get_address(
self, wallet_id: str, chain: str
) - str: ... # derives the address for a chain

async def sign_user_operation_hash(
self, wallet_id: str, user_op_hash: str
) - str: ... # ERC-4337 userOp signature/code

p class="text-muted mt-16" style="max-width:760px;"
All three calls happen over the network — they're async by design. The caller (codeChainExecutor/code)
never assumes key locality.

!-- Provider comparison --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Providers

## What each implementation actually is.

table class="data"
thead
trthProvider
thImplementation file
thKey material
thTypical latency
thNotes

/thead
tbody
tr
tdTurnkey span class="badge badge-success"default/span

tdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/turnkey_client.py/code, codeTurnkeyMPCSigner/code in codeexecutor.py/code

tdHSM-backed, P-256 stamp auth

td~200–400ms

tdOrganization per tenant, sub-org per agent group. Ethereum + Solana signing. Primary production signer.

tr
tdFireblocks

tdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/fireblocks_signer.py/code

tdThreshold MPC (Fireblocks SGX + network)

td~600–1200ms

tdEnterprise-grade governance, policy engine on the provider side. Higher latency, richer workflows. Used where the customer mandates it.

tr
tdCircle

tdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/circle_signer.py/code + codesardis-wallet/circle_client.py/code

tdCircle Developer-Controlled Wallets (MPC)

td~400–800ms

tdNatural pairing for USDC-native flows. Wallet sets = per-tenant scoping.

tr
tdLit Protocol

tdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/lit_signer.py/code

tdDecentralized threshold (Lit nodes)

td~800–1500ms

tdDecentralized custody option — trust split across Lit's node set rather than a single provider. Useful for "no single custodian" narratives.

tr
tdSimulated

tdcodeSimulatedMPCSigner/code in codeexecutor.py/code

tdNone — deterministic hex

td1ms

tdDev/test only. Never accepted in a live chain executor — guarded by config.

tr
tdLocal

tdcodeLocalAccountSigner/code in codeexecutor.py/code

tdLocal secp256k1 key (env var)

td5ms

tdDev / CI only. Exists because integration tests need a real signature. Not to be used in production — and the config validator refuses it when codeSARDIS_CHAIN_MODE=live/code.

tr
tdFailover

tdcodeFailoverMPCSigner/code in codeexecutor.py/code

tdWraps others

tdPrimary + fallback

tdTries codeprimary.sign_*()/code; on provider error, falls through to next. Emits observability event on every failover.

/tbody
/table

!-- Turnkey model --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Turnkey

## The organization model.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Turnkey is multi-tenant by design. Sardis uses that structure as the isolation boundary:
one organization per customer / tenant, one sub-organization per agent group, one wallet per agent.
Permission grants cascade down; revocation cascades down; nothing cross-cuts.

dl class="defs"
dtConfig
ddcodeTurnkeyConfig/code in codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py/code (env vars: codeTURNKEY_API_KEY/code, codeTURNKEY_ORGANIZATION_ID/code).

dtOrg per tenant
ddRoot organization is Sardis. Each customer onboarded gets a sub-org with its own API user and quorum rules.

dtSub-org per group
ddAgent groups (codeagent_groups.py/code) map to further sub-orgs — keys for one group can't sign for another.

dtWallet per agent
ddEach codeWallet/code row corresponds to a Turnkey wallet; codewallet_id/code is the Turnkey wallet id.

dtAddress derivation
ddPer-chain via codeTurnkeyMPCSigner.get_address(wallet_id, chain)/code — one seed → deterministic addresses for every supported chain.

dtStamp auth
ddEvery request is signed with a P-256 "API stamp" — no bearer tokens, no replay. Turnkey rejects stale requests at the edge.

dtVerification guard
ddcodesettings.turnkey_configured/code in codeconfig.py/code must be true in codelive/code mode; otherwise chain executor refuses to start.

/dl

!-- Lifecycle --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Lifecycle

## From empty slot to retired wallet.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Wallets are stateful: they're created, they sign, sometimes their keys rotate, sometimes they
get frozen, eventually some are archived. codeWalletManager/code in
codesardis-wallet/manager.py/code owns the full lifecycle.

div class="lifecycle"
div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"01

div class="step-title"create

div class="step-desc"codecreate_turnkey_wallet()/code / codecreate_circle_wallet()/code. Row in codewallets/code, wallet record at provider.

div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"02

div class="step-title"derive

div class="step-desc"codederive_address(wallet_id, chain)/code. Per-chain address cached in DB.

div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"03

div class="step-title"sign

div class="step-desc"First signing request after policy approval. codeactivity_monitor/code logs it.

div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"04

div class="step-title"rotate

div class="step-desc"coderotate_wallet_key()/code / codeemergency_key_rotation()/code. New key material, same wallet id.

div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"05

div class="step-title"freeze

div class="step-desc"codeis_frozen=TRUE/code (migration 006). Signing refused pre-flight.

div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"06

div class="step-title"recover

div class="step-desc"codesetup_social_recovery()/code / codeinitiate_recovery()/code — guardian-quorum path.

div class="lifecycle-step"
div class="step-label"07

div class="step-title"archive

div class="step-desc"Wallet retired; history + keys preserved at provider for audit.

!-- Signing sequence --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Signing flow

## Orchestrator → Wallet → Provider → Chain.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant O as PaymentOrchestrator
participant WM as WalletManager
participant Ex as ChainExecutor
participant P as MPCSignerPort
participant TK as Turnkey API
participant RPC as Alchemy RPC
participant C as Base / Tempo

O-WM: request signature for approved intent
WM-WM: load wallet row, is_frozen? kill-switch?
WM-Ex: build TransactionRequest (nonce, gas, chain)
Ex-P: sign_transaction(wallet_id, tx)
P-TK: P-256-stamped request
TK--P: signed raw tx
P--Ex: 0x... signed tx
Ex-RPC: broadcast_transaction(signed_tx)
RPC-C: submit
C--RPC: tx hash + receipt
RPC--Ex: SubmittedTx
Ex--O: result, receipt hash

[/diagram]

!-- Smart accounts --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Smart accounts

## Safe v1.4.1 on every EVM chain.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Agent wallets on EVM are emSafe Smart Accounts/em, not plain EOAs. The singleton is
pre-deployed; Sardis deploys a CREATE2 proxy per wallet. This gets us ERC-4337 compatibility,
modular policy, and social recovery — without paying audit costs on a bespoke account contract.

dl class="defs"
dtHelper
ddcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/safe_account.py/code

dtSingleton
ddcode0x41675C099F32341bf84BFc5382aF534df5C7461a/code (Safe v1.4.1 canonical, pre-deployed on all major EVM chains)

dt4337 module
ddSafe4337Module — required for UserOp execution

dtDeployment
ddCREATE2 proxy via Safe's canonical ProxyFactory. Address is empredictable before deployment/em via codepredict_safe_address()/code — we can share deposit addresses with users before any gas is spent.

dtPolicy wiring
ddZodiac Roles module (codezodiac_roles.py/code) is attached via codeenableModule/code in setup — pre-audited, avoids a custom-module audit for MVP.

dtERC-4337 stack
ddcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/erc4337//code — codebundler_client.py/code, codeentrypoint.py/code, codeuser_operation.py/code, codepaymaster_client.py/code.

/dl

!-- Paymasters --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Gas

## Paymasters — users don't pay ETH.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Asking an agent or merchant to "hold ETH for gas" is a UX dead end. Sardis uses a paymaster on
every supported EVM chain so gas is either paid in USDC (Circle) or sponsored (Pimlico).

table class="data"
theadtrthPaymaster
thModel
thClient
thWhen it's used

/thead
tbody
tr
tdCircle Paymaster v0.8 span class="badge badge-success"primary/span

tdUser pays gas in USDC. Permissionless, no API key.

tdcodeCirclePaymasterClient/code in codeerc4337/paymaster_client.py/code

tdDefault for agent + merchant userOps on Base, Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism (mainnet + testnet).

tr
tdPimlico

tdSardis sponsors gas (paid out of operating account).

tdcodePaymasterClient/code in the same file

tdFallback when Circle is unavailable, and for flows where the platform picks up gas (onboarding, demos, first-transaction incentives).

/tbody
/table
div class="callout mt-16"
span class="callout-title"Invariant/span
Gas-payment choice is visible in the codeSubmittedTx/code metadata and the payment ledger. No
silent sponsorship — operators can always tell who paid what.

!-- Tempo --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Tempo

## Why Tempo needs its own keychain.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Tempo is EVM-compatible at the execution layer but has a different account/permission model —
the Keychain precompile at code0xAAAAAAAA00000000000000000000000000000000/code with
per-Access-Key spending limits and a custom 86-byte signature format. That's not something a
generic ERC-4337 signer handles — it gets its own provider.

dl class="defs"
dtProvider
ddcodeTempoKeychainProvider/code in codepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/tempo_keychain.py/code

dtExecutor
ddcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/executor.py/code (dedicated — not shared with EVM executor)

dtAccount helper
ddcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/tempo_accounts.py/code

dtSignature format
ddcode0x03 || root_address (20 bytes) || inner_secp256k1_sig (65 bytes)/code — 86 bytes total. Built by codebuild_keychain_signature()/code.

dtAccess keys
ddcodeAccessKeyConfig/code maps Sardis policy directly to on-chain key parameters: codetoken_limits/code, codeallowed_targets/code, codemax_calls_per_day/code, codeexpires_at/code. A Sardis spending policy becomes an on-chain, Tempo-enforced access key.

dtRoot custody
ddRoot key stays in Turnkey. Access keys are short-lived agent keys provisioned per mandate.

dtFee payment
ddcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/fee_payer.py/code — Tempo's native fee-payer abstraction, USDC/pathUSD gas.

/dl

!-- Freeze --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Freeze

## The wallet-level kill switch.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Freeze is narrower than the global kill switch: it targets a single wallet, not the platform.
It's the right response when KYC changes, an incident is localized, or a mandate is revoked —
without disrupting the rest of the tenant.

dl class="defs"
dtColumns
ddcodewallets.is_frozen/code, codefrozen_at/code, codefrozen_by/code, codefreeze_reason/code — added in migration code006_wallet_freeze.sql/code.

dtEnforcement
ddcodeWalletManager/code checks codeis_frozen/code before dispatching to the signer port. Signers never see the request.

dtCompliance hook
ddKYC denial, AML hit, or kill-switch activation can all flip the freeze flag. Every flip is logged with the reason.

dtUnfreeze
ddExplicit operator action, audit-logged. No auto-expiry — a freeze stays a freeze until someone decides otherwise.

/dl

!-- Groups + multisig --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Shared wallets

## Agent groups + multisig.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Not every wallet is for a single agent. Team wallets, treasury wallets, and high-value flows
need m-of-n approvals. Sardis supports that at the signer level, not just at the policy level.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Agent groups

div class="card-description"
codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/agent_groups.py/code. A group has members,
group-level policies, and a shared wallet. Turnkey sub-orgs back the grouping so the custody
model agrees with the product model.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Multisig signer logic

div class="card-description"
codepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/multisig.py/code. codeApprovalThreshold/code,
codeMultisigSigner/code, codeTransactionApproval/code — collects the required quorum
before the MPC signer is asked to produce a final signature.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Social recovery

div class="card-description"
codesetup_social_recovery()/code / codeinitiate_recovery()/code on
codeWalletManager/code. A guardian set can rotate keys if the primary signer is lost —
no "the founder died and the keys died with him" failure mode.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Session keys

div class="card-description"
codesession_manager.py/code. Short-lived signing sessions scoped to specific agent flows.
Analogous to Tempo Access Keys but at the platform layer for non-Tempo chains.

!-- Key files --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Files worth reading in order.

table class="data"
theadtrthFile
thRole

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/executor.py/code
tdcodeMPCSignerPort/code, codeTurnkeyMPCSigner/code, codeFailoverMPCSigner/code, codeLocalAccountSigner/code, codeChainExecutor/code, codeChainRPCClient/code.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/manager.py/code
tdcodeWalletManager/code / codeEnhancedWalletManager/code — create, rotate, freeze, recover, spend-limit wiring, session mgmt.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/turnkey_client.py/code
tdRaw Turnkey client (P-256 stamping, create_wallet, sign_transaction).

trtdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/circle_client.py/code
tdCircle Developer-Controlled Wallet client.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/fireblocks_signer.py/code
tdFireblocks signer implementation of the port.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/lit_signer.py/code
tdLit Protocol signer implementation.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/safe_account.py/code
tdSafe v1.4.1 helpers — setup calldata, CREATE2 prediction.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/erc4337/paymaster_client.py/code
tdCircle Paymaster + Pimlico clients.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/tempo_keychain.py/code
tdTempo Access Key provisioning + keychain signature construction.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/executor.py/code
tdTempo-specific executor (non-EVM flow).

trtdcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/multisig.py/code
tdm-of-n approval logic for shared wallets.

trtdcodepackages/api/migrations/006_wallet_freeze.sql/code
tdFreeze columns + index.

/tbody
/table

/Canvas


=====================================
# SETTLEMENT
https://canvas.sardis.sh/settlement
=====================================

Canvas title="10 · Settlement & Chains — Sardis Canvas" currentId="settlement"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 10 · Flows/span

## Settlement & Chains

p class="hero-lead"
Once a transaction is policy-approved and signed, it has to actually move money — and money
moves on many different rails. Sardis supports six EVM chains, Tempo, Circle's Arc, and fiat
off-ramps through Stripe, Striga, and Bridge. This canvas walks the rail map, the cross-chain
path via CCTP, and the reconciliation machinery that keeps it all honest.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Chains are defined in codeChainConfig/code
(codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py/code); token contracts live in
codetokens.py/code. codeChainExecutor/code in
codepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/executor.py/code owns submission; CCTP v2 handles
cross-chain USDC; Stripe / Striga / Bridge handle fiat off-ramp. Settlement state is explicit
(migration 048), provider-rail is tracked (053), and a persistent reconciliation queue (057)
catches anything that falls off.

!-- Supported chains --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Rails

## Supported chains — the authoritative list.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
The mainnets below are defined directly in codeChainConfig/code. Any chain with a token
contract in codetokens.py/code can be added to that list with a single entry — no executor
changes needed for EVM-family networks.

table class="data"
thead
trthChain
thChain ID
thRPC default
thTokens
thStatus

/thead
tbody
trtdBase
td8453
tdcodehttps://mainnet.base.org/code (Alchemy in prod via codeSARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL/code)
tdUSDC, EURC
tdspan class="badge badge-success"mainnet/span primary

trtdTempo
td4217
tdcodehttps://rpc.tempo.xyz/code
tdUSDC (pathUSD), USDC.e, EURC
tdspan class="badge badge-success"mainnet/span live since 2026-03-18

trtdEthereum
td1
tdAlchemy
tdUSDC, USDT, PYUSD, EURC
tdmainnet (token addresses configured)

trtdPolygon
td137
tdAlchemy
tdUSDC, USDT, EURC
tdmainnet

trtdArbitrum
td42161
tdAlchemy

tdmainnet

trtdOptimism

tdmainnet

trtdSolana

td84532

tdspan class="badge"testnet/span

trtdArc Testnet
td~421614 (confirm w/ docs)
tdcodehttps://rpc.testnet.arc.network/code

tdspan class="badge"testnet/span Circle USDC-native L1

/tbody
/table

!-- Tokens --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Tokens

## The token registry.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
codeTokenType/code enum + codeTOKEN_REGISTRY/code in
codetokens.py/code. Each token carries decimals, issuer, peg metadata, and a
codecontract_addresses: dict[chain, address]/code. A token is considered "supported on a
chain" iff the chain key exists in that dict.

table class="data"
thead
trthToken
thDecimals
thIssuer
thPeg
thChains with contract configured

/thead
tbody
trtdUSDC
td6
tdCircle
tdUSD
tdbase, ethereum, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, solana, base_sepolia, arc_testnet, tempo (pathUSD), tempo_testnet, morph

trtdUSDC.e (bridged, TIP-20)
td6
tdCircle (bridged)
tdUSD
tdtempo

trtdUSDT
td6
tdTether
tdUSD
tdethereum, polygon, arbitrum, optimism, solana

trtdPYUSD
td6
tdPayPal
tdUSD
tdethereum, solana

trtdEURC
td6
tdCircle
tdEUR (1.08)
tdbase, ethereum, polygon, arc_testnet, tempo, tempo_testnet

/tbody
/table
div class="callout mt-16"
span class="callout-title"TIP-20 note/span
On Tempo, bridged USDC uses a TIP-20 codetransferWithMemo/code ABI — see the
codeis_tip20/code flag on codeTokenMetadata/code. The Tempo executor branches on this;
other chains use the standard ERC-20 ABI.

!-- Base primary --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Base

## The primary production rail.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Why Base is default

div class="card-description"
Coinbase-native L2, Circle Paymaster v0.8 works permissionlessly with no API key, sub-cent
fees, mature bundler ecosystem (Pimlico + Alchemy), Safe v1.4.1 singleton pre-deployed.
For USDC-denominated agent flows, Base is the path of least resistance.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"RPC strategy

div class="card-description"
Alchemy primary via codeSARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL/code, public codemainnet.base.org/code
as fallback. codeChainRPCClient/code in codeexecutor.py/code handles retries and
failover between endpoints.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Checkout default

div class="card-description"
codeSARDIS_CHECKOUT_CHAIN/code defaults to codebase/code. "Pay with Sardis" resolves
to a Base USDC transfer unless the merchant explicitly opts into another rail.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Smart contracts here

div class="card-description"
codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code, ERC-8183 stack, reference Safe modules — all intended
first for Base mainnet (and mirrored to Tempo). See canvas 06 · Contracts.

!-- Tempo deep dive --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Tempo

## Why Tempo got first-class status.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Tempo launched mainnet on 2026-03-18 alongside Stripe's MPP (Merchant Payment Protocol) — we
shipped Sardis live on it on day one. It's not just another EVM chain; it's payments-native.

dl class="defs"
dtChain
ddchain_id 4217, RPC codehttps://rpc.tempo.xyz/code

dtArchitecture
dd100K+ TPS target; EVM-compatible execution; Keychain precompile for agent permissions; fee-payer abstraction for gas-in-USDC.

dtOwnership
ddMatt Huang (Paradigm) CEO, $500M Series A at $5B valuation.

dtDedicated executor
ddcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/executor.py/code (not shared with EVM executor — TIP-20 ABI + keychain sigs + fee-payer flow).

dtKeychain
ddcodepackages/sardis-wallet/src/sardis_wallet/tempo_keychain.py/code — maps Sardis policy to on-chain Access Keys. See canvas 09.

dtFee payer
ddcodetempo/fee_payer.py/code — native gas-abstraction support, no separate paymaster.

dtTokens
ddpathUSD (USDC) at code0x20c0000000000000000000000000000000000000/code; USDC.e (bridged) at code0x20C000000000000000000000b9537d11c60E8b50/code; EURC at code0x20c0000000000000000000001621e21f71cf12fb/code.

dtDEX integration
ddcodetempo/dex.py/code — native DEX for on-chain swaps when cross-currency flows stay on Tempo.

/dl

!-- CCTP v2 --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Cross-chain

## CCTP v2 — USDC across chains without bridges.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Circle Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol v2: burn USDC on source, mint USDC on destination. No
wrapped assets, no pooled liquidity, no bridge counterparty risk. Sardis uses it wherever a
payment starts on one chain and settles on another.

dl class="defs"
dtImplementation
ddcodepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/cctp.py/code — codeCCTPBridgeService/code, codeBridgeTransfer/code, codeBridgeStatus/code

dtConstants
ddcodecctp_constants.py/code — domain IDs, TokenMessenger / MessageTransmitter addresses per chain

dtForwarding helper
ddcodecctp_forwarding.py/code — destination-chain forwarding so the minted USDC lands where the agent actually wants it, not at the messenger address

dtUnified addresses
ddCCTP v2's big win — same token messenger address on every chain, so integrations aren't n² chain pairs.

dtTypical use
ddAgent holds USDC on Base; merchant is on Polygon; codeCCTPBridgeService.bridge_usdc()/code burns on Base, mints on Polygon, forwards to merchant address in one flow.

/dl
div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent (Base)
participant Ex as ChainExecutor
participant TMb as TokenMessenger (Base)
participant Attest as Circle Attestation
participant TMp as MessageTransmitter (Polygon)
participant M as Merchant (Polygon)

A-Ex: bridge_usdc(amount, to=Polygon, recipient=M)
Ex-TMb: depositForBurnWithCaller(amount, domain=polygon, ...)
TMb--Ex: burn event + message hash
Ex-Attest: poll attestation for message
Attest--Ex: signed attestation
Ex-TMp: receiveMessage(message, attestation)
TMp-M: mint USDC → recipient
Ex--A: BridgeStatus.COMPLETED, dst tx hash

[/diagram]

!-- Fiat off-ramps --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Fiat off-ramps

## USDC → merchant bank, three providers.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
Merchants eventually want fiat in their bank account. Sardis doesn't pick one provider — it
picks the cheapest path per currency and region. The selector lives in
codeCrossCurrencySettlementService/code.

div class="grid grid-3"
div class="rail-card"
div class="rail-label"Stripe Connect

div class="rail-title"US / USD · ACH

p class="text-sm text-muted"USDC → USD → merchant bank. Connected Account model; Sardis handles the conversion and Stripe handles the ACH settlement.

div class="rail-card"
div class="rail-label"Striga

div class="rail-title"EU / EUR · SEPA + vIBANs

p class="text-sm text-muted"EURC → EUR → SEPA. Per-merchant vIBANs so inbound SEPA lands against a known merchant identity. Fee ~0.3%.

div class="rail-card"
div class="rail-label"Bridge

div class="rail-title"Multi-rail · ACH / SEPA / PIX / Wire

p class="text-sm text-muted"Provider abstraction over bank-settlement rails worldwide. Fee ~0.5% on USDC → USD. Used when Stripe / Striga don't cover the geography.

## Path selection

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
codeCrossCurrencySettlementService.get_settlement_quotes()/code enumerates viable paths per
request, scores by total fee + estimated time, and returns quotes. codeselect_optimal_path()/code
picks the best. The path identifiers are stable: codeusdc_bridge_usd/code,
codeusdc_grid_fx_eur/code, codeeurc_striga_eur/code, codedirect_stablecoin/code.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
P[Payment settles in USDC] -- Q{Merchant currency?}
Q -- USD (US) -- S1[Stripe Connect: USDC → USD → ACH]
Q -- EUR (EU) -- S2[Striga: EURC → EUR → SEPA]
Q -- EUR (fallback) -- S3[Bridge: USDC → Grid FX → EUR → SEPA]
Q -- BRL -- S4[Bridge: USDC → PIX]
Q -- USD (non-Stripe) -- S5[Bridge: USDC → ACH/Wire]
Q -- Stablecoin -- S6[Direct: stablecoin-to-stablecoin, no FX]

[/diagram]

!-- FX --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"FX

## Quotes and cross-currency settlement.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
When source and destination currency differ, a quote has to be recorded: the rate, the fee
breakdown, the expected received amount. That quote is part of the payment's evidence record —
disputes reference it directly.

dl class="defs"
dtService
ddcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/cross_currency_settlement.py/code — codeCrossCurrencySettlementService/code

dtQuote type
ddcodeSettlementQuote/code: path, source & target currency/amount, exchange_rate, total_fee_cents, fee_breakdown, estimated_time_seconds, codefee_percent/code property

dtResult type
ddcodeSettlementResult/code: settlement_id, path, amounts, status, codeprovider_tx_ids[]/code, metadata

dtFX helpers
ddcodefx_quote.py/code (quote construction), codefx_signer.py/code in codesardis-chain/code (chain-side FX signing), Grid FX + Circle Cross-Currency adapters (codecircle_cross_currency.py/code).

dtFee defaults (bps)
ddBridge 50, Grid FX 50, Striga swap 30. Configurable at service init.

/dl

!-- Settlement tracking --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Tracking

## Settlement is explicit, not best-effort.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
"Payment succeeded" has two meanings: the chain accepted the tx, and the merchant's money
actually arrived. The codesettlement_records/code table captures the full lifecycle of the
second one.

dl class="defs"
dtMigration
ddcodepackages/api/migrations/048_settlement_tracking.sql/code

dtTable
ddcodesettlement_records/code

dtKey fields
ddcodesettlement_id/code (unique), codeintent_id/code, codereceipt_id/code, codemode/code, codestatus/code, codeamount/code, codecurrency/code, codefee/code, codenetwork_reference/code, codecredential_id/code

dtCard-path fields
ddcodeauthorization_status/code, codecapture_status/code, codedispute_status/code, codereversal_reference/code, codeliability_party/code

dtTimeline
ddcodeinitiated_at/code → codeconfirmed_at/code → codesettled_at/code (or codefailed_at/code). codeexpected_settlement_at/code powers the "stuck settlements" alert.

dtIndexes
ddcodeidx_stl_intent/code, codeidx_stl_status(status, mode)/code, partial codeidx_stl_pending/code (initiated/pending_confirmation only), partial codeidx_stl_disputes/code.

/dl

!-- Provider rail --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Provider rail

## Which rail actually processed a payment.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
The same logical payment can ride different provider rails depending on routing. Operators
need to see which one in every row — for reconciliation, cost analysis, and incident response.

dl class="defs"
dtMigration
ddcodepackages/api/migrations/053_provider_rail_tracking.sql/code

dtColumns on codetransactions/code
ddcodeprovider_rail VARCHAR(50)/code, codeprovider_payment_reference VARCHAR(255)/code

dtValid values
ddcodestripe_spt/code, codevisa_tap/code, codemastercard_agent_pay/code, codecircle_cpn/code, codebridge_ach/code, codebridge_sepa/code, codebridge_pix/code

dtIndex
ddcodeidx_transactions_provider(provider_rail, created_at)/code — powers the "costs by rail this month" dashboard query.

dtWhy it matters
ddWhen a provider has an incident, operators need a one-query answer to "which payments are currently on that rail?" — without this column, you're grepping JSON metadata at 3am.

/dl

!-- Reconciliation --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Reconciliation

## Persistent queue — because chains reorg and RPCs flake.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
If the on-chain tx lands but the ledger append fails, we can't just forget about it. The
reconciliation queue holds the half-written payment until the ledger append succeeds.
Persistence means a process restart doesn't lose a cent.

dl class="defs"
dtMigration
ddcodepackages/api/migrations/057_reconciliation_queue.sql/code

dtTable
ddcodereconciliation_queue/code

dtSchema
ddcodeentry_type TEXT/code, codepayload_json JSONB/code, codestatus/code (pending / processing / resolved / failed), coderetry_count/code, codecreated_at/code, codeprocessed_at/code

dtIndex
ddPartial codeidx_recon_queue_status/code WHERE status='pending' — the worker only ever scans pending rows.

dtWhy it exists
ddReplaces a prior codeInMemoryReconciliationQueue/code that lost entries on restart. Any payment where ledger append fails after chain execution now persists its reconciliation state across process restarts.

dtOperator behavior
ddAlert fires when coderetry_count N/code or codestatus = 'failed'/code. Human-in-the-loop resolution.

/dl

!-- Adjacent services --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Supporting services

## Executor-adjacent modules worth naming.

table class="data"
theadtrthFile
thRole

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis_chain/executor.py/code
tdcodeChainExecutor/code, codeChainRPCClient/code, codeSubmittedTx/code, codeGasEstimate/code, codeGasPriceProtection/code, codeTransactionRequest/code.

trtdcodesardis_chain/nonce_manager.py/code / coderedis_nonce_manager.py/code
tdPer-wallet nonce coordination — Redis-backed in prod to survive concurrent submissions.

trtdcodesardis_chain/gas_optimizer.py/code
tdGas pricing + spike protection (via codeGasPriceProtection/code).

trtdcodesardis_chain/mev_protection.py/code
tdPrivate transaction submission where available — Flashbots-style for flows where MEV risk is real.

trtdcodesardis_chain/confirmation.py/code
tdReceipt polling, reorg handling.

trtdcodesardis_chain/permit2.py/code
tdUniswap Permit2 for gasless approvals where relevant.

trtdcodesardis_chain/cdp_swap.py/code
tdCoinbase CDP swap service integration.

trtdcodesardis_chain/liquidity_router.py/code
tdRouting for cross-venue swap execution.

trtdcodesardis_chain/simulation.py/code
tdPre-flight tx simulation — catch reverts before broadcast.

trtdcodesardis_chain/provider_tracker.py/code
tdRecords provider rail choice → feeds the codeprovider_rail/code column in migration 053.

trtdcodesardis_chain/deposit_monitor.py/code
tdWatches deposit addresses for inbound USDC (checkout funding detection).

trtdcodesardis_chain/bridges//code + codebridge.py/code + codebridge_xyz.py/code
tdBridge provider adapters (Bridge.xyz etc.) alongside CCTP.

trtdcodesardis-core/fiat_orchestrator.py/code
tdOrchestrates the USDC → fiat handoff (Stripe / Striga / Bridge path selection).

/tbody
/table
