=====================================
# CHECKOUT
https://canvas.sardis.sh/checkout
=====================================

Canvas title="11 · Checkout & Merchants — Sardis Canvas" currentId="checkout"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 11 · Product/span

## Checkout & Merchants

p class="hero-lead"
The public repo keeps the checkout session engine, hardening migrations, API routes, and
merchant SDK contracts. Hosted browser checkout UI and embed implementation live in the
private product repo until Sardis deliberately publishes that widget as a stable OSS surface.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
A merchant creates a span class="code-inline"merchant_checkout_session/span. A customer
receives a span class="code-inline"client_secret/span, presents it to a checkout client,
connects a wallet or uses a managed wallet path, signs a USDC transfer, and Sardis streams
the outcome back. All public endpoints are span class="code-inline"client_secret/span-scoped
so sessions cannot be enumerated and double-pay races cannot land.

!-- Two products --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Products

## One session engine, two front doors.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Both products create rows in the same codemerchant_checkout_sessions/code table and emit
the same webhook envelope. The difference is who holds the wallet and where the crypto knobs live.

div class="split-2"
div class="product-card"
div class="product-sub"Pay with Sardis

## Crypto-native checkout — customer brings the wallet.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Drop-in checkout, similar in shape to Stripe Checkout but paying in USDC. Customer connects
MetaMask / Coinbase Wallet / any WalletConnect provider, or fiat-onramps into a fresh wallet
via Coinbase CDP. Settlement lands on the merchant's configured address (or connected merchant bank
account for USD payout).

ul

- Client-secret-scoped API for browser or server checkout clients

- External wallet confirmation and server-managed payment paths

- Optional onramp integration for fiat-first customers

- EIP-191 signature binds payer wallet to session

- SSE stream for real-time status in the merchant UI

/ul

div class="product-card"
div class="product-sub"Sardis Connect

## Zero-crypto merchant SDK — three lines of code.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
For API providers who want to charge AI agents without ever touching a chain.
code@sardis/connect/code wraps an Express/Next.js route, serves a
code/.well-known/sardis.json/code manifest, and reports usage to Sardis. The merchant
sees a USD balance; Sardis handles wallet creation, settlement, and payout.

ul

- codepackages/sardis-connect/code (Python) · codepackages/sardis-connect-js/code (TS)

- codePricedEndpoint/code / codeUsageRecord/code / codeServiceManifest/code models

- Protocol-agnostic: codeaccepts: ["sardis", "x402", "mpp"]/code

- Per-call, per-unit, and subscription pricing tiers

- Payout via connected merchant bank · no wallet UX on the merchant side

/ul

!-- Pay with Sardis flow --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Pay with Sardis · End-to-end

## Seven steps from merchant intent to settled USDC.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every step corresponds to a real endpoint in
codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/merchant_checkout.py/code. Public endpoints
live under the code/sessions/client/{client_secret}/.../code namespace; merchant endpoints
require a principal.

div
div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"01

div class="step-body"
strongMerchant creates session./strong
codePOST /v1/merchant/checkout/sessions/code (authenticated with merchant principal) →
inserts a row into codemerchant_checkout_sessions/code with amount, currency, chain,
settlement target, optional codeidempotency_key/code and codeembed_origin/code.
Server generates a codeclient_secret/code (24 random bytes, base64).

div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"02

div class="step-body"
strongRedirect or embed./strong
Merchant passes the codeclient_secret/code to a checkout client. Hosted UI is product-private;
the public contract is the client-secret-scoped session API and the webhook/SSE contract.

div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"03

div class="step-body"
strongCheckout client loads session./strong
A browser, mobile, or server checkout client fetches
codeGET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/details/code — returns the amount, merchant name,
and the codesettlement_address/code needed for client-side USDC transfers.

div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"04

div class="step-body"
strongWallet connect./strong
Customer connects via wagmi. Two paths converge here:
external wallet (codePOST /connect-external/code, EIP-191 signature verified server-side)
or CDP Onramp (codeGET /onramp-token/code, fiat → USDC in a fresh wallet). Either way,
codepayer_wallet_address/code is persisted on the session.

div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"05

div class="step-body"
strongPayment./strong
Customer signs a USDC codetransfer/code to codesettlement_address/code. Frontend
submits via viem; server records the tx hash via
codePOST /confirm-external-payment/code. Session flips codepending → confirmed/code.

div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"06

div class="step-body"
strongStreaming update./strong
Merchant systems and checkout clients subscribe to
codeGET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/stream/code (SSE). Polling fallback at
code.../details/code exists for clients that can't hold an SSE connection.

div class="step-row"
div class="step-num"07

div class="step-body"
strongSettlement + webhook./strong
Background job reconciles the on-chain receipt, computes
codeplatform_fee_amount/code and codenet_amount/code, flips status to
codesettled/code, and enqueues a codecheckout.session.settled/code webhook. Delivery
is tracked in codemerchant_webhook_deliveries/code keyed by codeevent_id/code for
dedup.

!-- Session lifecycle Mermaid --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"State machine

## Merchant checkout session lifecycle.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The session row moves through a narrow set of states. Every transition is audited; every
terminal state triggers a webhook.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

stateDiagram-v2
[*] -- created : POST /merchant/checkout/sessions
created -- pending : client loads /s/{client_secret}
pending -- expired : TTL elapsed
pending -- connected : connect-external (EIP-191 verified)
pending -- connected : onramp-token (CDP, payer wallet bound)
connected -- confirmed : confirm-external-payment (tx hash)
confirmed -- settled : reconciliation job · receipt verified
settled -- paid_out : connected merchant bank transfer (if USD payout)
pending -- failed : signature / balance / policy rejection
confirmed -- failed : tx reverted on-chain
settled -- [*]
paid_out -- [*]
expired -- [*]
failed -- [*]

[/diagram]

!-- Hardening --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Hardening

## Why public checkout is boring in a good way.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
codepackages/api/migrations/031_checkout_hardening.sql/code is the migration that
turned Pay with Sardis from "it works on the happy path" into something safe to point at the
open internet. Polar-inspired; every column earns its keep.

table class="data"
thead
trthControl
thColumn / Table
thWhat it prevents

/thead
tbody
tr
tdClient secret

tdcodemerchant_checkout_sessions.client_secret/code (UNIQUE)

tdSession ID enumeration. Public endpoints use code/sessions/client/{client_secret}/.../code, never the internal UUID.

tr
tdIdempotency key

tdcodemerchant_checkout_sessions.idempotency_key/code (UNIQUE, partial)

tdDuplicate session creation on merchant-side retry. DB-level uniqueness, not app-level.

tr
tdRow lock on pay

tdcodeSELECT ... FOR UPDATE NOWAIT/code in the pay path

tdDouble-pay races when a customer clicks twice or two tabs submit simultaneously.

tr
tdEmbed origin

tdcodemerchant_checkout_sessions.embed_origin/code

tdClickjacking / hostile iframe. Hosted clients must restrict codepostMessage/code to the declared origin.

tr
tdWebhook dedup

tdcodemerchant_webhook_deliveries.event_id/code (UNIQUE)

tdDouble-processing on retries. Merchants can safely use at-least-once delivery.

tr
tdFee transparency

tdcodeplatform_fee_amount/code · codenet_amount/code

td"Why did I get less than I charged?" support tickets. Full breakdown on every session.

tr
tdReusable links

tdcodemerchant_checkout_links/code table

tdOne-off sessions for static storefronts; merchant generates a slug, Sardis creates a fresh session per visitor.

tr
tdPayer binding

tdcodepayer_wallet_address/code (migration 033)

tdWrong-wallet payment. The onramp token endpoint validates the wallet matches the session binding.

/tbody
/table

!-- Hosted client boundary --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Hosted client boundary

## What remains private vs. public.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The public repo documents and tests the backend contract. The hosted React checkout client,
visual design, and embeddable script are product code and should be recovered into the
private product repo from git history if needed.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Public contract

div class="card-description"
ul style="margin:0; padding-left:18px;"

- Session creation and lookup endpoints.

- Client-secret-scoped public session operations.

- SSE status stream and webhook event envelope.

- Database idempotency, deduplication, and settlement state.

/ul

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Private product surface

div class="card-description"
ul style="margin:0; padding-left:18px;"

- Hosted browser checkout UI.

- Embeddable script and web component.

- Conversion, analytics, branding, and merchant dashboard loops.

- Provider-specific wallet UX decisions.

/ul

!-- CDP Onramp + external wallet --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Two ways to fund a payment

## External wallet · CDP Onramp.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Customers either already own crypto (external wallet path) or don't (CDP Onramp path).
Both converge at the same session state: a verified codepayer_wallet_address/code with
enough USDC to settle.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"External wallet — EIP-191 binding

div class="card-description"
Frontend: wagmi + viem + WalletConnect v2 + Coinbase Wallet SDK.
Flow: codeGET /connect-params/code returns the challenge to sign, customer signs with
wallet, frontend posts signature to codePOST /connect-external/code. Server verifies
the EIP-191 signature recovers the claimed address and persists it as
codepayer_wallet_address/code. On pay, customer signs the actual USDC transfer;
frontend reports the hash via codePOST /confirm-external-payment/code.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"CDP Onramp — fiat to USDC

div class="card-description"
For customers without crypto. Sardis mints an MPC wallet for the session, calls
codePOST api.developer.coinbase.com/onramp/v1/token/code with a CDP JWT, and returns a
hosted codepay.coinbase.com/buy/select-asset/code URL bound to that wallet.
The code/onramp-token/code endpoint validates the wallet address matches the
session — an attacker cannot redirect funds to a different wallet by tampering with the
query string.

!-- Sardis Connect --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Sardis Connect

## Three lines of middleware, zero crypto on the merchant side.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Sardis Connect is the opposite philosophy to Pay with Sardis. The merchant never sees a wallet,
never sees chain config, never handles stablecoin balances. They wrap their API with
codesardisConnect({ apiKey })/code, declare pricing, and get USD in their connected merchant bank
account. Sardis absorbs the crypto layer entirely.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Merchant code — TypeScript

div class="card-description"
pre class="code-block"codeimport { sardisConnect } from '@sardis/connect';
const sardis = sardisConnect({ apiKey: 'mch_live_xxx' });
app.use(sardis.middleware());/code

The middleware reads a priced endpoint manifest, rejects unpaid calls with
code402 Payment Required/code (x402-compatible), and forwards paid calls to the merchant
handler. Protocol-agnostic: one middleware accepts Sardis native, x402, and MPP.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Service manifest

div class="card-description"
Served at code/.well-known/sardis.json/code — agents discover pricing via
codeServiceManifest/code:
ul style="margin:6px 0 0; padding-left:18px;"

- codePricedEndpoint(path, method, price, pricing_model)/code

- codepricing_model/code: codeper_call/code · codeper_unit/code · codesubscription/code

- codeUsageRecord/code for metered billing (report units after the call)

- Tiered pricing via codePricingTier/code

/ul

!-- Merchant trust + checkout controls --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Risk + merchant-side controls

## Trust scoring and the merchant kill switch.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Two pieces of machinery sit next to the session engine: per-merchant trust profiles (Sardis
decides how much to trust the merchant) and checkout controls (the merchant decides what
shape of payments to accept).

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Merchant trust — migration 045

div class="card-description"
codemerchant_trust_profiles/code stores codetrust_level/code, a rolling
codetrust_score/code (default 0.3), codetransaction_count/code,
codetotal_volume/code, and codedispute_count/code. codeMerchantTrustService/code
adjusts approval thresholds: first-seen merchants face stricter KYC + lower velocity caps,
merchants with a dispute history get flagged for manual review. Verified merchants
(codeverified_at/code, codeverified_by/code) get the tightest loop.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Checkout controls router

div class="card-description"
codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/checkout_controls.py/code exposes
merchant-side knobs: amount caps, country allowlists, velocity limits, required
customer-KYC tier. codeGET /config/code / codePUT /config/code for configuration,
codePOST /incidents/code / codeGET /incidents/code for the merchant's audit trail of
auto-blocked or flagged sessions.

!-- Surface --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Endpoint surface

## What's actually wired up.

table class="data"
thead
trthEndpoint
thAuth
thPurpose

/thead
tbody
trtdcodePOST /v1/merchant/checkout/sessions/code
tdMerchant principal
tdCreate session (idempotent via codeidempotency_key/code).

trtdcodeGET /v1/merchant/checkout/sessions/{id}/code
tdMerchant principal
tdInspect session from merchant side.

trtdcodeGET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/details/code
tdPublic
tdSession detail for the checkout iframe.

trtdcodeGET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/connect-params/code
tdPublic
tdChallenge for EIP-191 signature.

trtdcodePOST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/connect-external/code
tdPublic
tdBind external wallet via EIP-191 signature.

trtdcodePOST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/pay/code
tdPublic
tdServer-signed pay path (Sardis-managed wallet).

trtdcodePOST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/confirm-external-payment/code
tdPublic
tdClient-signed USDC transfer; record tx hash.

trtdcodeGET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/balance/code
tdPublic
tdLive balance read for the funded wallet.

trtdcodeGET /sessions/client/{client_secret}/stream/code
tdPublic
tdSSE stream for status transitions.

trtdcodePOST /sessions/client/{client_secret}/onramp-token/code
tdPublic
tdCDP Onramp URL bound to payer wallet.

trtdcodePOST /merchants/{id}/links/code · codeGET /merchants/{id}/links/code
tdMerchant principal
tdReusable checkout links (codemerchant_checkout_links/code).

trtdcodePOST /stripe-connect/.../code (3 routes)
tdMerchant principal
tdOnboarding + payout target for USD settlement.

trtdcodeGET/PUT /checkout-controls/config/code
tdMerchant principal
tdVelocity / country / amount / KYC controls.

/tbody
/table

!-- Source of truth --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of truth

## Files to grep when something looks off.

table class="data"
thead
trthFile
thWhy it matters

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/merchant_checkout.py/code
tdAll code/sessions/.../code endpoints: create, connect, pay, confirm, stream, onramp.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/checkout.py/code
tdLegacy /checkout surface used by older integrations; kept for backwards compat.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/merchants.py/code
tdMerchant CRUD + checkout links + settlements lookup.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/checkout_controls.py/code
tdMerchant-side controls + incident log.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/providers/stripe_connect.py/code
tdconnected merchant bank onboarding for USD payout.

trtdcodepackages/api/migrations/031_checkout_hardening.sql/code
tdclient_secret · idempotency · embed_origin · checkout_links · webhook_deliveries.

trtdcodepackages/api/migrations/033_wallet_verification.sql/code
tdcodepayer_wallet_address/code column + codeexternal_wallet/code payment method.

trtdcodepackages/api/migrations/045_merchant_trust.sql/code
tdcodemerchant_trust_profiles/code scoring table.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-checkout/src/sardis_checkout//code
tdOrchestrator · sessions · webhooks · settlement · fraud · idempotency · partial payments.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect/src/sardis_connect//code
tdPython merchant SDK · models · middleware.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect-js/src//code
tdTypeScript merchant SDK · codemiddleware.ts/code · codetypes.ts/code.

/tbody
/table

!-- Cross-refs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/compliance"
div class="canvas-card-num"12 · Product

div class="canvas-card-title"Compliance Stack

div class="canvas-card-desc"KYC (Didit) + AML (Elliptic) — what runs before a checkout session is allowed to settle.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/protocols"
div class="canvas-card-num"13 · Product

div class="canvas-card-title"Protocol Gateway

div class="canvas-card-desc"x402 / MPP / AP2 — the alternate "front doors" Sardis Connect accepts.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/settlement"
div class="canvas-card-num"10 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Settlement & Chains

div class="canvas-card-desc"How USDC lands, how CCTP crosses chains, how payout hits connected merchant bank.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/payment-flow"
div class="canvas-card-num"07 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Payment Flow

div class="canvas-card-desc"The non-checkout agent payment path — same orchestrator, different front door.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# COMPLIANCE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/compliance
=====================================

Canvas title="12 · Compliance Stack — Sardis Canvas" currentId="compliance"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 12 · Product/span

## Compliance Stack

p class="hero-lead"
The SDK is the distribution. Compliance is the moat. No fintech partner, no regulated merchant,
and no enterprise buyer will let an AI agent move real money through them without seeing a
credible KYC + AML + sanctions + travel-rule + SAR stack behind the API. Sardis ships one.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Six pillars: strongKYC/strong for humans (Didit), strongKYB/strong for organizations,
strongKYA/strong for agents, strongAML + sanctions/strong for addresses and counterparties,
strongTravel Rule/strong for cross-VASP transfers above $3K, and strongSAR/strong for
regulator-bound suspicious activity reports. All of it runs inside the
codePreExecutionPipeline/code on the fail-closed side of the gate.

!-- Why --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Why this is the moat

## Anyone can write a payment SDK. Almost nobody ships compliance.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every payments-for-agents startup demo looks the same on day one: a wallet, a signed
transaction, a receipt. The hard part is day 90 — when a regulated merchant wants to onboard,
the bank wants a BSA report, the auditor asks for retention policy, and the OFAC list just
got a new entry. These aren't optional; they're the line between a demo and a business.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"What partners ask for

div class="card-description"
Stripe Connect onboarding · Circle business account · Bridge originator · Striga banking —
every one of these started with the same question: em"show us your KYC / AML / SAR
program and your retention policy"/em. Without those, there is no bank rail, no payout,
no card issuing. Sardis exists as a platform because the compliance stack exists first.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Why the SDK doesn't replace it

div class="card-description"
SDKs are distribution — they get Sardis into an agent. They don't move money, don't hold
KYC records, and don't satisfy a regulator. The compliance layer is what makes the SDK's
output actually spendable. You can't fork your way around FinCEN 5-year retention.

!-- Six pillars --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The six pillars

## What actually runs.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Each pillar is a concrete module in codepackages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance//code
with its own provider abstraction, its own persistence, and its own tests.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="pillar-card"
div class="pillar-sub"Pillar 1 · KYC

## Know Your Customer — for human users.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Primary provider: strongDidit/strong (YC W26, $0.33/check, 500 free/mo). Document +
liveness + face match. Persona and Coinbase verifications sit behind the same interface.
Module: codesardis_compliance/kyc.py/code + provider codeproviders/didit.py/code.
Persistence: codekyc_verifications/code (fixed to TEXT agent_id + codekyc_status/code
on users in migration 090).

div class="pillar-card"
div class="pillar-sub"Pillar 2 · KYB

## Know Your Business — for organizations.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Required before an organization can deploy agents that move money above threshold. Module:
codesardis_compliance/kyb.py/code. Table: codekyb_verifications/code (migration
010) — inquiry_id, provider, status, verified_at, expires_at, business_name. Triggered at
organization onboarding, rechecked on anniversary.

div class="pillar-card"
div class="pillar-sub"Pillar 3 · KYA

## Know Your Agent — the Sardis original.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Human KYC and business KYB don't cover the thing actually signing: the agent. KYA captures
agent identity (Ed25519/ECDSA-P256 keys), attestation, heartbeat, and state transitions
(register · check · upgrade · suspend · reactivate · stale sweep). Module:
codesardis_compliance/kya.py/code. Ties to codeagit_policy_engine/code at decision
time.

div class="pillar-card"
div class="pillar-sub"Pillar 4 · AML + Sanctions

## OFAC, PEP, adverse media, on-chain.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Providers behind one interface: OFAC, OpenSanctions, Chainalysis, Scorechain, Watchman,
Elliptic (planned). Screens emaddresses/em (on-chain counterparty), ementities/em
(PEP / sanctions lists), and emtransactions/em (combined). Module:
codesanctions.py/code · codepep.py/code · codeadverse_media.py/code. Blocked
addresses cached with TTL.

div class="pillar-card"
div class="pillar-sub"Pillar 5 · Travel Rule

## FATF R.16 — originator + beneficiary data.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Migration 012 defines codetravel_rule_transfers/code: transfer_id, amount, originator
and beneficiary JSONB, protocol (e.g., Notabene). Triggered above $3K USD / $1K EUR.
Module: codetravel_rule.py/code + codeproviders/notabene.py/code. Without this,
VASP-to-VASP transfers are not legally clean.

div class="pillar-card"
div class="pillar-sub"Pillar 6 · SAR

## Suspicious Activity Reports — FinCEN 5-year retention.

p class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
codesuspicious_activity_reports/code (migration 011) — sar_id, internal_reference,
activity_type, priority, subject, filing_deadline, report_data. Auto-drafted from anomaly
scoring; filed by humans. Module: codesar.py/code. Five-year retention is a hard legal
constraint; the schema is designed for it.

!-- KYC onboarding flow --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"KYC · Didit flow

## Signup to codesk_live_/code key in four steps.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Router: codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/compliance/kyc_onboarding.py/code. Webhook
delivery is where a dashboard user becomes a fully provisioned Sardis tenant.

div
div class="check-row"
div class="check-tag"01

div class="check-body"
strongInitiate./strong codePOST /v1/onboarding/kyc/initiate/code creates a Didit
session (document + liveness). Returns a hosted URL the dashboard redirects to.

div class="check-row"
div class="check-tag"02

div class="check-body"
strongUser verifies./strong On Didit's hosted UI. No PII crosses Sardis at this step.

div class="check-row"
div class="check-tag"03

div class="check-body"
strongWebhook./strong codePOST /v1/onboarding/kyc/webhook/code — Didit posts the
decision with codex-api-key/code auth. Row inserted into codekyc_verifications/code;
codeusers.kyc_status/code flipped to codeverified/code / codedenied/code.

div class="check-row"
div class="check-tag"04

div class="check-body"
strongLive key issued./strong On codeverified/code, the user becomes eligible for
a production API key (codesk_live_.../code). Before that, only codesk_test_/code
sandbox keys are issued. Retry endpoint: codePOST /v1/onboarding/kyc/retry/code.

!-- Pipeline Mermaid --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"At execution time

## What actually runs inside the Pre-Execution Pipeline.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The orchestrator calls the pipeline once. The pipeline runs the compliance checks in the
order below. Any fail-closed reject is audited with a reason code and surfaced via webhook.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant O as Orchestrator
participant P as PreExecutionPipeline
participant KYC as KYC (Didit)
participant KYA as KYA (agent)
participant S as Sanctions (OFAC / Chainalysis)
participant F as FIDES (trust graph)
participant TR as Travel Rule (Notabene)
participant SAR as SAR drafter
participant L as Ledger

O-P: execute(intent)
P-KYC: user.kyc_status == verified?
KYC--P: ok / denied
P-KYA: agent registered + active?
KYA--P: ok / stale / suspended
P-S: screen(payee, payer, amount)
S--P: clear / match / soft-match
P-F: trust_score(agent_did) ≥ threshold?
F--P: tier + signals
alt amount $3K
P-TR: originator/beneficiary ready?
TR--P: drafted / blocked
end
alt any anomaly signal
P-SAR: draft report
SAR--P: queued for human filing
end
P-L: log decision (approved | denied)
P--O: verdict

[/diagram]

!-- Providers table --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Providers

## Which provider covers which pillar.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every provider sits behind an interface in
codepackages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance/providers//code. Swaps are
cost-of-one-file changes (Persona → Didit cost one PR).

table class="data"
thead
trthPillar
thPrimary
thPluggable alternates
thStatus

/thead
tbody
trtdKYC (humans)
tdDidit
tdPersona, Coinbase Verifications, Privado ID
tdLive

trtdKYB (businesses)
tdPersona
tdCircle Compliance, MiCA-specific flow
tdLive

trtdSanctions (entities)
tdOpenSanctions
tdOFAC (direct), Watchman, Marble
tdLive

trtdSanctions (on-chain)
tdChainalysis
tdScorechain, Elliptic (planned)
tdLive (Chainalysis); Elliptic planned

trtdPEP / adverse media
tdOpenSanctions
tdMarble
tdLive

trtdTravel Rule
tdNotabene
td—
tdSchema + drafts live; VASP routing staged

trtdIdentity / DID
tdFIDES
tdPrivado ID
tdLive (migration 055)

trtdSAR authoring
tdSardis native
td—
tdDrafts auto-generated; filing is human.

/tbody
/table

!-- FIDES + KYA detail --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Agent-native additions

## FIDES + KYA — what human compliance frameworks can't cover.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
KYC/AML/Travel Rule were designed for humans and companies. Autonomous agents break both
axes — they don't have passports and they aren't LLCs. Sardis fills the gap with two
modules.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"FIDES — decentralized trust graph

div class="card-description"
Migration 055 adds codedid_mappings/code, codetrust_score_snapshots/code, and
codeagents.fides_did/code / codeagents.agit_repo_hash/code columns. An agent's
DID is verified via signature; its trust score is recomputed from on-chain behavior,
incident history, and cross-platform attestations. Router:
coderouters/fides_identity.py/code. This is Efe's other open-standards project,
embedded as a first-class compliance signal.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"KYA — agent lifecycle

div class="card-description"
Eleven endpoints under code/v1/kya/*/code: register, get, check, upgrade, suspend,
reactivate, heartbeat, stale-sweep. Module: codesardis_compliance/kya.py/code.
codeagit_policy_engine.py/code consumes KYA state at decision time — a suspended or
heartbeat-stale agent fails closed before the transaction reaches the wallet.

!-- Data governance + enterprise --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Data governance + enterprise controls

## Retention, export, SSO, and per-role RBAC.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Compliance doesn't end when the check passes. Regulators want retention schedules, GDPR
wants export + deletion, and enterprise buyers want SSO before they write a PO.

table class="data"
thead
trthConcern
thWhere it lives
thWhat it does

/thead
tbody
tr
tdPII classification

tdMigration 042 · codedata_classification/code

tdPer-column PII type + retention days + anonymize-on-expiry. Seeds: users.email (730d, anonymize), wallets.address (1095d), access_audit_log.ip (90d, anonymize), payer_wallet_address (365d, anonymize).

tr
tdRetention jobs

tdMigration 042 · codedata_retention_log/code

tdEvery purge run records rows purged, rows anonymized, duration. Operators can prove retention, not just promise it.

tr
tdGDPR export / deletion

tdRouter codedata_export.py/code · codecompliance_export.py/code

tdPer-subject export + delete endpoints. Export bundles are Merkle-anchored so users get tamper-evident copies.

tr
tdSAR 5-year retention

tdMigration 011 · codesuspicious_activity_reports/code

tdFinCEN hard requirement. Schema designed for long-horizon archival, not just working-set queries.

tr
tdSSO (SAML / OIDC)

tdMigration 043 · codesso_config/code

tdPer-tenant SSO configuration — required for regulated enterprise buyers.

tr
tdAdvanced RBAC

tdMigration 044 · codeadvanced_rbac/code

tdRole/permission matrix beyond owner/admin/viewer — needed for segregation of duties in banking-adjacent orgs.

tr
tdIdentity registry

tdMigration 011 · codeidentity_records/code

tdTAP/AP2 attestation keys with versioning + revocation — the audit trail that says "this agent claimed this key at this time."

/tbody
/table

!-- Certification program --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Certification program

## SOC 2 + PCI + ISO + GDPR + AIUC-1.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
External certifications that regulated buyers ask for, scoped to a one-year evidence
collection program with a dedicated Trust Control Dashboard running alongside Sardis.

table class="data"
thead
trthFramework
thScope
thWhy it matters

/thead
tbody
trtdSOC 2 Type II
tdSecurity, availability, confidentiality
tdEvery US enterprise procurement team asks for this. Without it, no deal.

trtdPCI DSS
tdVirtual cards flow (Stripe Issuing + planned direct)
tdCardholder data environment; required once Sardis Cards GA.

trtdISO 27001
tdISMS — information security management
tdEuropean + enterprise baseline; pairs with GDPR for EU buyers.

trtdGDPR
tdEU personal data
tdAligns with migration 042 retention + anonymize-on-expiry columns.

trtdAIUC-1
tdAI agent safety / governance
tdThe new piece. KYA + FIDES + policy decisions map to AIUC-1 controls directly.

/tbody
/table

!-- Endpoint surface --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Endpoint surface

## What compliance exposes over HTTP.

table class="data"
thead
trthEndpoint
thModule
thPurpose

/thead
tbody
trtdcodePOST /v1/onboarding/kyc/initiate/code
tdkyc_onboarding.py
tdStart Didit session for dashboard user.

trtdcodePOST /v1/onboarding/kyc/webhook/code
tdkyc_onboarding.py
tdDidit decision callback (x-api-key auth).

trtdcodePOST /v1/onboarding/kyc/retry/code
tdkyc_onboarding.py
tdReattempt after denial or document issue.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/kyc/verify/code
tdcompliance.py
tdGeneric KYC inquiry (provider-agnostic).

trtdcodeGET /v1/compliance/kyc/{agent_id}/code
tdcompliance.py
tdCurrent KYC status.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/kya/register/code
tdcompliance.py
tdRegister agent identity + attestation.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/kya/{id}/check/code · code/upgrade/code · code/suspend/code · code/reactivate/code · code/heartbeat/code
tdcompliance.py
tdAgent lifecycle state transitions.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/kya/stale/sweep/code
tdcompliance.py
tdBulk expire stale-heartbeat agents.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/sanctions/screen/code
tdcompliance.py
tdScreen an entity / address.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/sanctions/screen-transaction/code
tdcompliance.py
tdScreen a full transaction (payer + payee + amount).

trtdcodeGET /v1/compliance/sanctions/check/{address}/code
tdcompliance.py
tdCached lookup for a single address.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/sanctions/block/code
tdcompliance.py
tdManual block push (incident response).

trtdcodeGET /v1/compliance/audit/recent/code · code/audit/mandate/{id}/code · code/audit/verify-chain/code
tdcompliance.py
tdQueryable compliance audit trail.

trtdcodeGET /v1/compliance/audit/evidence/export/code
tdcompliance.py
tdSigned evidence bundle for auditors.

trtdcodePOST /v1/compliance/audit/evidence/verify-signature/code
tdcompliance.py
tdVerify a previously exported bundle.

trtdcodeGET /v1/compliance/webhooks/persona/verify/code
tdcompliance.py
tdPersona fallback webhook verification.

trtdcodeGET /v1/fides/.../code
tdfides_identity.py
tdDID resolution + trust score queries.

/tbody
/table

!-- Source of truth --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of truth

## Where each control actually lives.

table class="data"
thead
trthFile / migration
thWhat it owns

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-compliance/src/sardis_compliance//code
tdThe package. Modules: kyc · kyb · kya · sanctions · pep · adverse_media · travel_rule · sar · risk_scoring · audit_rotation · batch · reports · checks · marble · mica · circle_compliance · coinbase_verifications.

trtdcode…/providers//code
tddidit · chainalysis · notabene · ofac · opensanctions · privado_id · scorechain · watchman. Behind one interface.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/compliance/compliance.py/code
tdAll code/v1/compliance/*/code endpoints (KYC, KYA, sanctions, audit, evidence).

trtdcode…/routers/kyc_onboarding.py/code
tdDashboard-facing KYC onboarding (Didit flow).

trtdcode…/routers/fides_identity.py/code
tdFIDES DID + trust score queries.

trtdcode…/routers/compliance_export.py/code
tdGDPR / regulator export bundles.

trtdcodemigrations/010_kyb_verifications.sql/code
tdKYB tracking per organization.

trtdcodemigrations/011_sar_and_identity_persistence.sql/code
tdSAR (FinCEN 5-yr) + identity_records.

trtdcodemigrations/012_travel_rule.sql/code
tdFATF R.16 transfer records.

trtdcodemigrations/042_data_governance.sql/code
tdPII classification + retention + anonymize-on-expiry.

trtdcodemigrations/043_sso_config.sql/code
tdPer-tenant SSO config (SAML / OIDC).

trtdcodemigrations/044_advanced_rbac.sql/code
tdRole / permission matrix beyond basic tiers.

trtdcodemigrations/055_fides_integration.sql/code
tddid_mappings · trust_score_snapshots · agents.fides_did.

trtdcodemigrations/090_kyc_verifications_fix.sql/code
tdDashboard user ID compatibility + kyc_status on users.

/table

/Canvas


=====================================
# PROTOCOLS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/protocols
=====================================

Canvas title="13 · Multi-Protocol Gateway — Sardis Canvas" currentId="protocols"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 13 · Product/span

## Multi-Protocol Gateway

p class="hero-lead"
Agent payments aren't converging on one standard yet. Google, Visa, Mastercard, Coinbase,
Circle, and Stripe each have their own protocol. Sardis speaks all of them behind a single
orchestrator so a merchant, SDK, or agent doesn't pick a team. One code/pay/code front
door; the right verifier underneath.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Eight protocols wire into the same codePaymentOrchestrator/code:
strongAP2/strong, strongTAP/strong, strongx402/strong, strongA2A/strong,
strongACP/strong, strongMPP/strong (Stripe/Tempo), strongERC-8004/strong,
strongERC-8183/strong. Every one of them terminates at the same fail-closed gate,
runs on the same ledger, and emits the same webhook envelope.

!-- Gateway flow --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The gateway

## One entry, many verifiers.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Callers signal their protocol via route (code/v1/ap2/.../code,
code/v1/x402/.../code, code/v1/a2a/.../code) or via headers. Each protocol has a
dedicated verifier in codepackages/sardis-protocol//code; verified intents feed into
the same orchestrator that wallet-native calls use.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
subgraph Callers
A1[Agent via AP2]
A2[Visa rail · TAP]
A3[API provider · x402]
A4[Google A2A agent]
A5[ACP / Stripe MPP]
A6[On-chain ERC-8183 job]
end

A1 -- R1[ap2.py]
A2 -- R2[tap.py + visa_tap_webhooks.py]
A3 -- R3[x402.py]
A4 -- R4[a2a.py · a2a_payments.py]
A5 -- R5[acp.py · mpp.py]
A6 -- R6[erc8183_policy_guard.py]

R1 -- V[MandateVerifier]
R2 -- V
R3 -- V
R4 -- V
R5 -- V
R6 -- V

V -- G[PreExecutionPipeline
Policy + KYC + Sanctions + Trust]
G -- O[PaymentOrchestrator]
O -- C[ChainExecutor · MPC · Ledger]

[/diagram]

!-- Protocols: one card per --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Eight protocols

## Same structure for each.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
What it is · who runs it · when to reach for it · where Sardis implements it · the signature shape.

div class="grid grid-2"

!-- AP2 --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## AP2 — Agent Payment Protocol

span class="proto-tag"consortium/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Mandate chain protocol: emIntent → Cart → Payment/em. Each mandate is signed by the
previous actor (user authorizes agent, agent authorizes merchant, merchant authorizes rail).
The verifier walks the whole chain, checking Ed25519 / ECDSA-P256 signatures, TTLs, and
replay cache before the transaction is even scheduled.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddGoogle · PayPal · Mastercard · Visa consortium

dtUse when
ddB2B agent commerce · multi-party flows with human approval upstream

dtRouter
ddcoderouters/ap2.py/code → code/v1/ap2/payments/execute/code

dtVerifier
ddcodesardis_protocol/verifier.py/code · codemandate_cache.py/code

/dl
pre class="proto-code"POST /v1/ap2/payments/execute
{
"intent_mandate": { "user_did", "agent_did", "scope", "sig" },
"cart_mandate": { "cart", "merchant_did", "sig" },
"payment_mandate": { "amount", "rail", "sig" }
}

!-- TAP --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## TAP — Trust Anchor Protocol

span class="proto-tag"visa/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Visa's agent-identity attestation protocol. mTLS + TRID (Transaction Risk Indicator) for
card-rail transactions. The identity piece lives in
codepackages/sardis-protocol/src/sardis_protocol/tap.py/code; webhooks come in via
coderouters/visa_tap_webhooks.py/code. Pairs with AP2 when Visa is the rail.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddVisa

dtUse when
ddVisa card rail is part of the flow (card-present or card-not-present agent purchase)

dtRouter
ddcoderouters/visa_tap_webhooks.py/code

dtModule
ddcodesardis_protocol/tap.py/code · codetap_keys.py/code

/dl
pre class="proto-code"agent_id + tap_key (Ed25519)
→ challenge-response attestation
→ TRID score forwarded to Visa auth
→ rail approves / declines

!-- x402 --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## x402 — HTTP 402 auto-pay

span class="proto-tag"coinbase · circle/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Server responds code402 Payment Required/code with a payment challenge; client
auto-pays in USDC and retries. Revives HTTP's long-dormant 402 as the canonical
pay-per-call path for AI APIs. Sardis implements the verifier, settler, and challenge
generator, with ERC-3009 transfer support.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddCoinbase + Circle Gateway facilitators

dtUse when
ddAPI-to-API pay-per-call · AI inference · tool marketplaces

dtRouter
ddcoderouters/x402.py/code → code/verify/code · code/settle/code · code/challenges/code · code/dry-run/code

dtModules
ddcodex402.py/code · codex402_erc3009.py/code · codex402_settlement.py/code · codex402_upto.py/code

/dl
pre class="proto-code"GET /expensive-endpoint
← 402 Payment Required
{ "accepts": [{ "scheme": "exact", "asset": "USDC", "amount": "0.05" }] }
→ sign ERC-3009 transfer → POST /verify
→ receipt attached → retry original call

!-- A2A --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## A2A — Google Agent-to-Agent

span class="proto-tag"google/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Google's open protocol for agents from different platforms to discover each other, trust
each other, and transact. Sardis provides code/pay/code, message envelopes, and a
full trust-table API (peer discovery, relation CRUD, security-policy queries, audit).

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddGoogle (open)

dtUse when
ddAgents from different platforms transacting

dtRouter
ddcoderouters/a2a.py/code + coderouters/a2a_payments.py/code

dtKey endpoints
ddcode/pay/code · code/messages/code · code/trust/table/code · code/trust/check/code · code/trust/peers/code · code/trust/relations/code · code/trust/security-policy/code · code/trust/audit/recent/code

/dl
pre class="proto-code"POST /v1/a2a/pay
{ "from_agent_did", "to_agent_did", "amount", "currency", "reason" }
→ trust_check(from_agent, to_agent)
→ mandate issued if trust ≥ threshold
→ settlement via Sardis orchestrator

!-- ACP --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## ACP — Agent Commerce Protocol

span class="proto-tag"emerging/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Emerging standard for agent-mediated commerce — catalog, offer, order, settle.
Sardis ships the router; the protocol is deliberately thin so it can ride Sardis' own
orchestrator + ledger machinery rather than reinventing them.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddEmerging · multi-vendor spec

dtUse when
ddCatalog-first flows where discovery precedes payment

dtRouter
ddcoderouters/acp.py/code

dtVerbs
ddoffer · order · settle · attestation · payment-proof · dispute

/dl
pre class="proto-code"POST /v1/acp/offers — merchant posts
POST /v1/acp/orders — agent orders
POST /v1/acp/settle — orchestrator settles
POST /v1/acp/attest — evidence anchored

!-- MPP (Stripe / Tempo) --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## MPP — Merchant Payment Protocol

span class="proto-tag"stripe · tempo/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
Stripe's protocol layered on Tempo (100K+ TPS EVM L1). Sessions, execute, close, policy
evaluate/simulate, card issuance. Sardis is live on Tempo mainnet (chain_id 4217) via
the dedicated executor in codepackages/sardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo//code
and plugs MPP sessions into Sardis policy at codempp_gate/code.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddStripe + Tempo

dtUse when
ddHigh-throughput merchant flows · card issuance at scale

dtRouter
ddcoderouters/mpp.py/code

dtEndpoints
ddcode/sessions/code · code/sessions/{id}/execute/code · code/sessions/{id}/close/code · code/evaluate/code · code/simulate/code · code/cards/issue/code

/dl
pre class="proto-code"POST /v1/mpp/sessions → create
POST /v1/mpp/sessions/{id}/execute → pay
POST /v1/mpp/evaluate → policy decision only
POST /v1/mpp/cards/issue → issue a card

!-- ERC-8004 --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## ERC-8004 — Agent attestation

span class="proto-tag"erc/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
On-chain attestation standard for agent identity. Provides verifiable claims about
agent code hash, operator, and capabilities. Sardis implements the reader/writer at
codepackages/sardis-core/src/.../erc8004.py/code; it feeds into KYA and FIDES
signals consumed at the gate.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddERC standards track

dtUse when
ddRegulated deployments needing on-chain attestation of agent code + operator

dtModule
ddcodesardis_v2_core/erc8004.py/code

dtPairs with
ddKYA lifecycle · FIDES DIDs · AGIT repo hash

/dl
pre class="proto-code"agent register → on-chain attestation
{ agent_did, code_hash, operator_addr, sig }
reader verifies → feeds KYA + FIDES signals

!-- ERC-8183 --
div class="proto-card"
div class="proto-head"

## ERC-8183 — On-chain policy + reputation

span class="proto-tag"erc/span

div class="text-muted" style="font-size:13px;"
On-chain policy module + reputation registry + job market for agents. Three modules:
codeerc8183_policy_guard.py/code (enforces policy at chain edge),
codeerc8183_reputation.py/code (tracks post-trade reputation),
codeerc8183_job.py/code (job lifecycle). Launched with conservative caps (1% fee,
USDC-only, trust hooks disabled) — audit-driven relaxation path.

dl class="proto-kv"
dtWho
ddERC standards track · Sardis-authored implementation

dtUse when
ddOn-chain agent jobs · cross-platform reputation portability

dtModules
ddcodeerc8183_policy_guard.py/code · codeerc8183_reputation.py/code · codeerc8183_job.py/code

dtCaps
dd≤1% fee · USDC-only · trust hooks disabled pre-audit

/dl
pre class="proto-code"Job posted → policy_guard evaluates
→ agent accepts → work → settle
→ reputation registry updates score

!-- When to use what --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Decision matrix

## When to reach for which protocol.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Most real deployments use two at once (e.g., AP2 for the mandate chain, x402 for the actual
API call). The matrix below is the first-cut decision tree.

table class="data"
thead
trthUse case
thPrimary
thSecondary
thTradeoff

/thead
tbody
trtdAI agent buys an API call
tdx402
td—
tdLowest friction · assumes crypto rail · USDC only

trtdAgent books travel / multi-party purchase
tdAP2
tdTAP (if Visa)
tdHeaviest verification · strongest audit · slowest

trtdTwo agents on different platforms pay each other
tdA2A
tdAP2
tdBuilt-in trust graph · requires peer A2A support

trtdMerchant wants Stripe-native billing
tdMPP
tdx402
tdStripe-familiar · needs Tempo access

trtdCard-rail purchase from agent
tdTAP + AP2
td—
tdVisa-native · requires mTLS + Visa onboarding

trtdOn-chain job for an agent
tdERC-8183
tdERC-8004
tdFully on-chain · permissionless · 1% cap until audit

trtdNeed verifiable agent identity
tdERC-8004
tdFIDES (migration 055)
tdAnchored · interoperable · needs operator public key

trtdGeneric catalog + order flow
tdACP
tdx402 for payment leg
tdThin spec · rides Sardis orchestrator

/tbody
/table

!-- Shared primitives --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Shared primitives

## What every verifier reuses.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The reason supporting eight protocols is tractable: they all reduce to the same three
problems (signature verification, replay prevention, mandate archival). Sardis builds
those once.

table class="data"
thead
trthModule
thRole

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis_protocol/verifier.py/code
tdEd25519 / ECDSA-P256 / secp256k1 signature verification core.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/mandate_cache.py/code
tdCanonical mandate store — every protocol's signed payload lands here.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/replay_cache_redis.py/code
tdRedis-backed replay cache. TTL per protocol; required in prod.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/nonce_registry.py/code
tdPer-agent, per-protocol nonce tracking to defeat replay even without Redis.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/reason_codes.py/code
tdCanonical deny-reason catalog shared across protocols and audit.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/rate_limiter.py/code
tdProtocol-aware rate limiter (stricter for public protocols).

trtdcodesardis_protocol/payment_methods.py/code
tdRail selection — USDC / ERC-3009 / Visa / card / Lightning.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/fides_signing.py/code
tdFIDES DID signing adapter used by AP2 and A2A.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/eas_kya.py/code
tdEAS (Ethereum Attestation Service) bridge for KYA facts.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/zkpass_transgate.py/code
tdzkPass TransGate — zero-knowledge claims surfaced into protocols.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/paladin_privacy.py/code
tdPaladin privacy layer for sensitive mandate fields.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/kleros.py/code
tdKleros dispute adapter — protocol-level dispute resolution.

trtdcodesardis_protocol/schemas.py/code
tdCross-protocol Pydantic schemas; one definition per shape.

/tbody
/table

!-- Endpoint surface --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Endpoint surface

## Every protocol front door.

table class="data"
thead
trthProtocol
thEndpoint
thPurpose

/thead
tbody
trtdAP2
tdcodePOST /v1/ap2/payments/execute/code
tdVerify mandate chain and execute.

trtdTAP
tdcodePOST /v1/webhooks/visa-tap/code (via codevisa_tap_webhooks.py/code)
tdVisa TAP event ingestion.

trtdx402
tdcodePOST /v1/x402/verify/code
tdVerify a client-signed 402 payment payload.

trtdx402
tdcodePOST /v1/x402/settle/code
tdSettle a verified 402 payment on-chain.

trtdx402
tdcodeGET /v1/x402/settlements/{payment_id}/code
tdSettlement status lookup.

trtdx402
tdcodePOST /v1/x402/dry-run/code
tdServer-side simulation before signing.

trtdx402
tdcodePOST /v1/x402/challenges/code
tdIssue a 402 challenge (server-side helper).

trtdA2A
tdcodePOST /v1/a2a/pay/code
tdCross-platform agent payment.

trtdA2A
tdcodePOST /v1/a2a/messages/code
tdSigned inter-agent envelopes.

trtdA2A
tdcodeGET /v1/a2a/trust/table/code · code/trust/peers/code · code/trust/audit/recent/code
tdTrust graph queries.

trtdA2A
tdcodePOST /v1/a2a/trust/check/code · code/trust/relations/code · codeDELETE /trust/relations/code
tdTrust relation CRUD + check.

trtdA2A
tdcodeGET /v1/a2a/trust/security-policy/code
tdPublished security policy for peers.

trtdACP
tdcodePOST /v1/acp/offers/code · code/orders/code · code/settle/code · code/attest/code
tdFull ACP commerce loop.

trtdMPP
tdcodePOST /v1/mpp/sessions/code · code/execute/code · code/close/code
tdStripe/Tempo session lifecycle.

trtdMPP
tdcodePOST /v1/mpp/evaluate/code · code/simulate/code
tdPolicy decision without execution.

trtdMPP
tdcodePOST /v1/mpp/cards/issue/code
tdCard issuance via Stripe Issuing bridge.

trtdERC-8004
tdon-chain · read/write via SDK
tdAttestation read/write (not HTTP front door).

trtdERC-8183
tdon-chain · Sardis-deployed modules
tdPolicy guard / reputation / job registry.

/tbody
/table

tdAgent reputation registry.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/erc8183_job.py/code
tdAgent job market lifecycle.

=====================================
# SDKS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/sdks
=====================================

Canvas title="14 · SDKs & CLIs — Sardis Canvas" currentId="sdks"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 14 · Product/span

## SDKs & CLIs

p class="hero-lead"
Nine distinct surfaces ship the same Sardis API to wherever the agent actually lives —
a Python script, a Next.js app, a Claude Desktop chat, an n8n workflow, a Telegram
conversation, a checkout page, or a terminal. One auth model (codesk_/code API keys),
one semantic contract, nine idiomatic entry points.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
The SDK layer is intentionally thin: each binding wraps the same REST surface at
codeapi.sardis.sh/code with types, errors, retries, and pagination. Nothing "clever"
happens client-side — policy, mandate, and MPC signing stay server-authoritative.
The cleverness lives in how many environments the same API can reach without the
developer learning anything new.

!-- Surface grid --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Surface Map

## Nine packages, one API.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every box below is a real directory under codepackages//code. Version and published
artifact columns are pulled from the packages' own manifests.

div class="grid grid-3"

div class="surface-tile"
div class="surface-label"01 · Python

div class="surface-title"sardis-sdk

div class="surface-desc"Async-first httpx client. Resources for agents, wallets, mandates, payments, policies, holds, cards, fx, groups.

div class="chip-row mt-16"
span class="chip"v1.1.0/span
span class="chip"PyPI/span
span class="chip"py ≥3.12/span

div class="surface-tile"
div class="surface-label"02 · TypeScript

div class="surface-title"@sardis/sdk

div class="surface-desc"ESM + CJS + browser bundles. Zod types, 30 resource modules, streaming + interceptors + pagination.

div class="chip-row mt-16"
span class="chip"v1.1.0/span
span class="chip"npm/span
span class="chip"Node ≥18/span

div class="surface-tile"
div class="surface-label"03 · CLI (Python)

div class="surface-desc"Click-based. 17 command groups: auth, wallets, agents, payments, policies, mandates, holds, cards, chains, fiat, approvals, groups, spending, ledger, demo, init.

div class="surface-desc"Embeddable consumer widget — lets end users approve/deny agent spend in real time from the agent app.

div class="chip-row mt-16"
span class="chip"Python/span

!-- Auth model --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Auth

## One API-key model across every surface.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every surface authenticates with a prefixed secret key. Prefix encodes environment and
blast radius; coderequire_principal/code in the API gateway hashes the key (SHA-256),
looks up the owning merchant/agent, and attaches a principal to the request for the
orchestrator to consume.

table class="data"
thead
trthPrefix
thEnvironment
thUse
thRotation

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesk_live_…/code
tdProduction
tdReal money, real compliance, real rate limits.
tdHot-rotatable from dashboard; old key decays 24h.

trtdcodesk_test_…/code
tdSandbox
tdPolicy & mandate logic end-to-end, but settlement is simulated.
tdSelf-serve in dashboard.

trtdcodesk_dev_…/code
tdLocal
tdEmitted by codesardis auth login/code against local API.
tdNever leaves laptop.

trtdcodepk_live_…/code
tdPublic / client
tdCheckout, spend widget — rate-limited, read-biased scope only.
tdSafe to embed.

/tbody
/table

!-- Python SDK --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"01 · Python SDK

## codesardis-sdk/code — async httpx client.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-sdk-python//code. Async is the default
(codeAsyncSardisClient/code); sync exists for scripts. Connection pooling, retry with
exponential backoff, token refresh, and typed errors are built-in.

## Install & quickstart

precode# install
pip install sardis-sdk
# or
uv add sardis-sdk

# set API key
export SARDIS_API_KEY=sk_live_…/code

p class="mt-16 text-muted"
From codepackages/sardis-sdk-python/examples/quickstart.py/code — the canonical flow:
create agent → create wallet → parse a natural-language policy → pay → check balance.

precodeimport asyncio, os
from sardis_sdk import AsyncSardisClient

async def main():
async with AsyncSardisClient(api_key=os.environ["SARDIS_API_KEY"]) as client:
agent = await client.agents.create(name="My First Agent")
wallet = await client.wallets.create(agent_id=agent.agent_id, chain="base")

policy = await client.policies.parse(
text="Max $100 per transaction, $500 per day, no gambling",
)
await client.policies.apply(wallet_id=wallet.wallet_id, policy=policy)

payment = await client.payments.send(
wallet_id=wallet.wallet_id,
to="merchant_demo",
amount="25.00",
purpose="API usage - OpenAI",
)
print(payment.status, payment.tx_id)

balance = await client.wallets.get_balance(wallet.wallet_id)
print(balance.balance, balance.currency)

asyncio.run(main())/code

## Resources exposed on codeAsyncSardisClient/code

div class="chip-row"
span class="chip"agents/span
span class="chip"wallets/span
span class="chip"payments/span
span class="chip"policies/span
span class="chip"mandates/span
span class="chip"holds/span
span class="chip"cards/span
span class="chip"fx/span
span class="chip"groups/span
span class="chip"approvals/span
span class="chip"funding/span
span class="chip"evidence/span
span class="chip"escrow/span
span class="chip"exceptions/span
span class="chip"kill_switch/span

## Typed error handling

p class="text-muted"
Every error inherits from codeSardisError/code — catch the base, or narrow to
specifics. Source: codepackages/sardis-sdk-python/src/sardis_sdk/models/errors.py/code.

precodefrom sardis_sdk import (
AsyncSardisClient,
AuthenticationError, # 401 — bad or revoked key
RateLimitError, # 429 — quota exceeded (respects Retry-After)
ValidationError, # 400 — malformed request body
NotFoundError, # 404 — unknown wallet / agent / mandate
TimeoutError, # request-level timeout
ConnectionError, # transport failure before response
APIError, # 5xx catchall
)

try:
await client.payments.send(...)
except RateLimitError as e:
await asyncio.sleep(e.retry_after)
except AuthenticationError:
# rotate key and bail
raise
except APIError as e:
log.error("sardis %s: %s", e.status_code, e.code)/code

!-- JS SDK --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"02 · TypeScript SDK

## code@sardis/sdk/code — strict TS, Zod-validated.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-sdk-js//code. Ships three bundles from one source —
ESM (codedist/index.js/code), CJS (codedist/cjs/index.cjs/code), and a browser
build (codedist/browser/index.js/code). Strict TypeScript, Zod runtime validation at
boundaries, interceptors, abort signals, and auto-paginators.

precodenpm i @sardis/sdk
# or
pnpm add @sardis/sdk/code

precodeimport { SardisClient, RateLimitError, AuthenticationError } from "@sardis/sdk";

const client = new SardisClient({ apiKey: process.env.SARDIS_API_KEY! });

const agent = await client.agents.create({ name: "My First Agent" });
const wallet = await client.wallets.create({ agent_id: agent.agent_id, chain: "base" });

const policy = await client.policies.parse({
text: "Max $100 per transaction, $500 per day, no gambling",
});
await client.policies.apply({ wallet_id: wallet.wallet_id, policy });

try {
const payment = await client.payments.send({
wallet_id: wallet.wallet_id,
to: "merchant_demo",
amount: "25.00",
purpose: "API usage - OpenAI",
});
console.log(payment.status, payment.tx_id);
} catch (err) {
if (err instanceof RateLimitError) {
await new Promise(r = setTimeout(r, err.retryAfter * 1000));
} else if (err instanceof AuthenticationError) {
throw err;
}
}/code

## Resource modules (codesrc/resources//code)

div class="chip-row"
span class="chip"agents/span
span class="chip"wallets/span
span class="chip"payments/span
span class="chip"policies/span
span class="chip"mandate-delegation/span
span class="chip"holds/span
span class="chip"cards/span
span class="chip"fx/span
span class="chip"groups/span
span class="chip"approvals/span
span class="chip"kill-switch/span
span class="chip"ledger/span
span class="chip"evidence/span
span class="chip"escrow/span
span class="chip"treasury/span
span class="chip"subscriptions-v2/span
span class="chip"checkout/span
span class="chip"marketplace/span
span class="chip"streaming/span
span class="chip"webhooks/span
span class="chip"a2a/span
span class="chip"ucp/span
span class="chip"simulation/span
span class="chip"usage/span
span class="chip"batch/span
span class="chip"transactions/span
span class="chip"funding/span
span class="chip"payment-objects/span

## Auto-pagination

precodefor await (const agent of client.paginate(
(params) = client.agents.list(params),
{ limit: 100 },
)) {
console.log(agent.name);
}/code

!-- Python CLI --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"03 · CLI (Python)

## codesardis-cli/code — Click-based terminal tool.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-cli//code. One command group per resource, thin wrapper
around the Python SDK. Config lives in code~/.sardis/config.toml/code
(codecommands/auth.py/code writes it). See codecommands//code for the full tree.

precode# install
pip install sardis-cli

# log in — exchanges email/password or API key for a local token
sardis auth login

# scaffold a new project (API keys, .env, example agent)
sardis init my-agent

# list wallets for the current org
sardis wallet list

# create one
sardis wallet create --agent ag_123 --chain base

# set a natural-language policy
sardis policy set --wallet wal_abc \
--text "Max $100 per tx, $500/day, no gambling"

# pay
sardis pay --wallet wal_abc --to merchant_demo --amount 25.00 \
--purpose "OpenAI API usage"

# one-command end-to-end demo (uses sandbox)
sardis demo run/code

## Command groups

div class="chip-row"
span class="chip"auth/span
span class="chip"init/span
span class="chip"agents/span
span class="chip"wallets/span
span class="chip"payments/span
span class="chip"policies/span
span class="chip"mandates/span
span class="chip"holds/span
span class="chip"cards/span
span class="chip"chains/span
span class="chip"fiat/span
span class="chip"approvals/span
span class="chip"groups/span
span class="chip"spending/span
span class="chip"ledger/span
span class="chip"demo/span

!-- Go CLI --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"04 · CLI (Go)

## codesardis/code binary — cross-platform release.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-cli-go//code. Built with GoReleaser; release metadata at
codedist/metadata.json/code. One static binary, six targets, Homebrew tap under
codedist/homebrew//code.

table class="data"
theadtrthTarget
thArtifact

/thead
tbody
trtddarwin / amd64
tdcodesardis_darwin_amd64.tar.gz/code

trtddarwin / arm64
tdcodesardis_darwin_arm64.tar.gz/code

trtdlinux / amd64
tdcodesardis_linux_amd64.tar.gz/code

trtdlinux / arm64
tdcodesardis_linux_arm64.tar.gz/code

trtdwindows / amd64
tdcodesardis_windows_amd64.zip/code

trtdwindows / arm64
tdcodesardis_windows_arm64.zip/code

/tbody
/table
pre class="mt-16"code# Homebrew (macOS/Linux)
brew install sardis/tap/sardis

# Verified checksums in dist/checksums.txt and dist/digests.txt
sardis --version # v0.1.0/code

!-- JS CLI --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"05 · CLI (JS)

## code@sardis/cli/code — the npm-native variant.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-cli-js//code. Same command surface as the Python CLI,
distributed via npm so Node-heavy teams can pin it in codepackage.json/code and keep
their agent toolchain in one registry.

precodenpx @sardis/cli auth login
npx @sardis/cli wallet list
npx @sardis/cli pay --wallet wal_abc --to merchant_demo --amount 25.00/code

!-- MCP --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"06 · MCP Server

## code@sardis/mcp-server/code — Claude, Cursor, Windsurf.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-mcp-server//code. Implements Model Context Protocol over
stdio; exposes 50 Sardis tools to any MCP-aware client. Tool list is declared in the
package's codemcp/code manifest (codepackage.json/code) — truth source below.

## Claude Desktop config

p class="text-muted"From codeCLAUDE_DESKTOP_CONFIG.md/code. macOS path:
code~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json/code.

precode{
"mcpServers": {
"sardis": {
"command": "npx",
"args": ["@sardis/mcp-server", "start"],
"env": {
"SARDIS_API_KEY": "your_sardis_key",
"SARDIS_WALLET_ID": "wal_YOUR_WALLET"
}
}
}
}/code

p class="text-muted mt-16"
Omit the codeenv/code block to run in simulated mode — no key needed, no funds move,
useful for demos and first-touch.

## Tools exposed (50)

p class="text-muted"Grouped by domain. Each one maps to one Sardis REST call.

## Wallets & balance

div class="chip-row"
span class="chip"sardis_get_balance/span
span class="chip"sardis_get_wallet/span
span class="chip"sardis_create_wallet/span
span class="chip"sardis_list_wallets/span
span class="chip"sardis_fund_wallet/span
span class="chip"sardis_withdraw/span
span class="chip"sardis_withdraw_to_bank/span
span class="chip"sardis_get_funding_status/span
span class="chip"sardis_get_withdrawal_status/span
span class="chip"sardis_list_funding_transactions/span

## Agents & groups

div class="chip-row"
span class="chip"sardis_create_agent/span

span class="chip"sardis_list_agents/span
span class="chip"sardis_update_agent/span
span class="chip"sardis_create_group/span
span class="chip"sardis_get_group/span
span class="chip"sardis_list_groups/span
span class="chip"sardis_add_agent_to_group/span
span class="chip"sardis_remove_agent_from_group/span
span class="chip"sardis_get_group_spending/span

!-- n8n --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"07 · n8n Node

## coden8n-nodes-sardis/code — community node.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/n8n-nodes-sardis//code. Ships one node
(codenodes/Sardis/Sardis.node.ts/code) plus a credential type
(codecredentials/SardisApi.credentials.ts/code) that securely stores the API key in
n8n's encrypted credential store. Requires coden8n-workflow ≥ 2.0/code.

precode# Install inside an n8n instance
npm i -g n8n-nodes-sardis

# Or add via n8n Community Nodes UI: Settings → Community Nodes → Install
# Package: n8n-nodes-sardis/code

p class="text-muted mt-16"
Example flow: codeGmail → Sardis: Request Approval → Wait → Sardis: Pay/code.
The codeSardis/code node exposes operations for pay, hold, approve, balance,
and webhook dispatch so the workflow can stay in one tool.

!-- Spend widget --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"08 · Spend Widget

## codesardis-spend-widget/code — consumer-side approval UI.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-spend-widget//code. An embeddable widget that consumer
agent apps drop in so the emend user/em — not the agent — confirms spend above a
threshold. Uses a codepk_live_…/code publishable key scoped to a single session;
sensitive operations still go through the server.

precodescript src="https://widget.sardis.sh/v1/spend.js" async/script

sardis-spend
publishable-key="pk_live_…"
session-id="ss_live_abc123"
on-approve="handleApprove"
on-deny="handleDeny"
/sardis-spend/code

p class="text-muted mt-16"
The widget subscribes over SSE to spend requests for the session and renders a native-feel
approve/deny sheet. Complements the Telegram bot surface for users who live in a browser
rather than in chat.

!-- Telegram bot --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"09 · Telegram Bot

## codesardis-telegram-bot/code — on-call surface.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at codepackages/sardis-telegram-bot//code. Python bot that mirrors the
approval / balance / kill-switch surface into Telegram. Built for solo operators and
on-call rotations who want real-time agent-spend pings in a chat they already have open.

precode# typical commands (the bot is side-loaded, not published)
/balance # shows wallet balances across chains
/approvals # lists pending approvals, with approve/deny buttons
/kill wallet # pauses all outbound spend for a wallet
/status # last 10 ledger decisions/code

!-- Sequence: how surfaces share one API --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Shape

## Nine entry points, one pipeline.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Whichever surface originates the call, the request hits the same API gateway and
the same codePaymentOrchestrator/code. This is what makes "add a new surface"
cheap — the trust model doesn't change.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
PY["Python SDK"] -- API
TS["TS SDK"] -- API
CLI["sardis-cli (py)"] -- API
GO["sardis (go binary)"] -- API
JCLI["@sardis/cli"] -- API
MCP["@sardis/mcp-server"] -- API
N8N["n8n node"] -- API
WID["spend-widget"] -- API
TG["telegram-bot"] -- API
API["api.sardis.sh · require_principal"] -- O["PaymentOrchestrator"]
O -- PP["Pre-Execution Pipeline"]
PP -- CX["Chain / Wallet / Rails"]

[/diagram]

!-- Release cadence --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"10 · Release Cadence

## How SDKs actually ship.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Two workflows in code.github/workflows//code drive publishing. Tag pushes trigger
production releases; codeworkflow_dispatch/code supports manual rc builds with a
TestPyPI / npm prerelease toggle. No SDK version leaves CI without green tests.

table class="data"
thead
trthWorkflow
thTriggers
thTargets
thRegistries

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcoderelease-python-sdk.yml/code

tdTag codepy-sdk-v*/code or manual dispatch

tdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-python/code

tdPyPI (prod) / TestPyPI (prerelease)

tr
tdcoderelease-python-integrations.yml/code

tdTag codepy-integration-v*/code

tdsardis-langchain, sardis-crewai, sardis-autogpt, …

tdPyPI / TestPyPI

tr
tdcoderelease-npm.yml/code

tdTag codemcp-v*/code, codesdk-js-v*/code, codeai-sdk-v*/code, codeconnect-js-v*/code

tdMCP server, TS SDK, AI SDK, Connect JS

tdnpm

tr
tdcodesign-artifacts.yml/code

tdOn release builds

tdAll published artifacts

tdSigstore attestations

/tbody
/table

!-- Source of truth --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Files to read if the above drifts.

table class="data"
theadtrthFile
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-python/pyproject.toml/code
tdPython SDK version, deps, classifiers.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-python/src/sardis_sdk/client.py/code
tdcodeAsyncSardisClient/code construction, retry, error mapping.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-python/examples/quickstart.py/code
tdThe canonical end-to-end flow.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-js/package.json/code
tdTS SDK version, bundle exports.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-js/src/index.ts/code
tdPublic surface: client, errors, telemetry, pagination.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-sdk-js/src/resources//code
td30 resource modules.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-cli/src/sardis_cli/commands//code
tdOne file per command group.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-cli-go/dist/metadata.json/code
tdGoReleaser build metadata + commit.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-mcp-server/package.json/code
tdcodemcp.tools[]/code — the real tool list, not this page.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-mcp-server/CLAUDE_DESKTOP_CONFIG.md/code
tdOS-specific config paths + prod/simulated snippets.

trtdcodepackages/n8n-nodes-sardis/package.json/code
tdn8n node + credential registration.

trtdcode.github/workflows/release-python-sdk.yml/code
tdPython publish logic.

trtdcode.github/workflows/release-npm.yml/code
tdnpm publish logic for 4 packages.

/tbody
/table
