=====================================
# INTEGRATIONS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/integrations
=====================================

Canvas title="15 · AI Framework Integrations — Sardis Canvas" currentId="integrations"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 15 · Product/span

## AI Framework Integrations

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis is the universal payment layer for every major agent framework. Not "works with
a few" — every one that matters today ships a Sardis adapter in
codepackages//code, with the same policy engine, the same MPC wallets, and the same
ledger underneath. The developer writes agent code; Sardis makes its money verifiable.

div class="row row-wrap mt-24" style="gap:12px;"
span class="badge"19 published adapters/span
span class="badge"70K+ SDK installs/span
span class="badge"LangChain v1.0.0/span
span class="badge"MCP-first/span

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"Positioning/span
Frameworks come and go; money movement doesn't. By landing in every agent framework's
native idiom, Sardis positions itself as the neutral payments substrate — the same way
Stripe became neutral for web checkout. Developers don't choose "payments" any more than
they choose "HTTPS"; they pick a framework and Sardis is already there.

!-- Why adapters exist --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Shape

## Every adapter is the same three-line pattern.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A framework adapter doesn't re-implement Sardis — it translates. Each package imports
codesardis-sdk/code (or code@sardis/sdk/code), wraps Sardis primitives
(codepay/code, codecheck_balance/code, codeset_policy/code,
codelist_transactions/code, codecheck_policy/code) as emnative tools in that
framework's tool protocol/em, and exposes them as a codeSardisToolkit/code.
The agent author writes code that looks native; the bytes on the wire are identical
across every framework.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
LC["LangChain agent"] -- AL["sardis-langchain"]
CA["CrewAI crew"] -- AC["sardis-crewai"]
AG["AutoGPT block"] -- AA["sardis-autogpt"]
OA["OpenAI Agents SDK"] -- AO["sardis-openai-agents"]
CS["Claude Agent SDK"] -- AK["sardis-agent-sdk"]
GD["Google ADK"] -- AD["sardis-adk"]
BU["Browser Use agent"] -- AB["sardis-browser-use"]
CK["Coinbase AgentKit"] -- AX["sardis-agentkit"]
VE["Vercel AI SDK"] -- AV["@sardis/ai-sdk"]
NG["n8n / Activepieces"] -- AN["node + piece"]
MCP["MCP host (Claude/Cursor)"] -- AM["@sardis/mcp-server"]

AL -- SDK["sardis-sdk (py) · @sardis/sdk (ts)"]
AC -- SDK
AA -- SDK
AO -- SDK
AK -- SDK
AD -- SDK
AB -- SDK
AX -- SDK
AV -- SDK
AN -- SDK
AM -- SDK

SDK -- API["api.sardis.sh"]
API -- O["PaymentOrchestrator → Pre-Execution Pipeline → MPC → chain"]

[/diagram]

p class="text-muted"
Adding a new framework is a one-day job: wrap the five primitive tools in its tool schema,
ship as a package, add an example. The policy engine, trust graph, and ledger don't change.

!-- Group 1: orchestration --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 01 · General-purpose orchestration

## Where most LLM agents actually live.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Six frameworks. If someone ships an "agent" in 2026, it is almost certainly built on one
of these. Each adapter returns a codeSardisToolkit/code whose code.get_tools()/code
plugs directly into that framework's agent loop.

div class="grid grid-2"

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"LangChain

div class="int-title"sardis-langchain · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
codeSardisToolkit/code → five codeBaseTool/codes (Pay, CheckBalance, SetPolicy,
CheckPolicy, ListTransactions) plus a codeSardisCallbackHandler/code that logs every
tool invocation to the Sardis ledger. Works with any LangChain agent (OpenAI Functions,
ReAct, tool-calling).

precodepip install sardis-langchain/code

precodefrom sardis_sdk import SardisClient
from sardis_langchain import SardisToolkit

client = SardisClient(api_key="sk_live_…")
tools = SardisToolkit(client=client, wallet_id="wal_abc").get_tools()
# pass tools to any LangChain agent/code

div class="int-meta mt-8"Exports: SardisToolkit · SardisCallbackHandler · SardisPayTool · SardisCheckBalanceTool · SardisSetPolicyTool · SardisCheckPolicyTool · SardisListTransactionsTool

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"CrewAI

div class="int-title"sardis-crewai · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Agent payment tools plus empre-built Sardis agents/em and task templates —
"Finance Officer," "Spending Approver" — that drop into a CrewAI crew.
Multi-agent crews share a wallet with per-agent policy overlays.

precodepip install sardis-crewai/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"AutoGPT

div class="int-title"sardis-autogpt · v2.0.0

div class="int-desc"
AutoGPT blocks that expose Sardis codepay/code, codecheck_balance/code, and
policy ops as first-class workflow nodes. 2.x bumped to match AutoGPT's block
architecture refactor.

precodepip install sardis-autogpt/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"OpenAI Agents SDK

div class="int-title"sardis-openai-agents · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Sardis tools as codeTool/code instances for OpenAI's Agents SDK — drop into
codeRunner/code to give any GPT-class model policy-controlled payments.
Complementary to codesardis-openai/code (classic function-calling).

precodepip install sardis-openai-agents/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Claude Agent SDK

div class="int-title"sardis-agent-sdk · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Tool definitions + codeSardisToolHandler/code for Anthropic's Agent SDK.
Covers pay, hold, escrow, subscription, FX quote, policy, balance,
transaction list, and mint — a wider surface than most adapters because Claude can
handle richer tool schemas.

precodepip install sardis-agent-sdk/code

div class="int-meta mt-8"Exports: SardisToolkit, SardisToolHandler, SARDIS_PAY_TOOL, SARDIS_CHECK_BALANCE_TOOL, SARDIS_CREATE_HOLD_TOOL, SARDIS_CREATE_ESCROW_TOOL, SARDIS_CREATE_SUBSCRIPTION_TOOL, SARDIS_GET_FX_QUOTE_TOOL, SARDIS_SET_POLICY_TOOL, SARDIS_MINT_PAYMENT_TOOL, SARDIS_LIST_TRANSACTIONS_TOOL, SARDIS_CHECK_POLICY_TOOL

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Google ADK

div class="int-title"sardis-adk · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
Wraps Sardis ops as codeFunctionTool/codes for Google's Agent Development Kit.
Ships a codecreate_sardis_agent()/code helper that returns a pre-configured ADK
agent with wallet + policy wired up.

precodepip install sardis-adk/code

!-- Group 2: browser/automation --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 02 · Browser & automation

## Agents that click, type, and execute shell.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Browser and code-execution agents have the biggest "oops I drained the wallet" risk —
they touch live websites and run arbitrary code. Every adapter here force-routes spend
through the policy engine before a browser action or shell command can spend money.

div class="grid grid-2"

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Browser Use

div class="int-title"sardis-browser-use · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Payment tools for the Browser Use agent. Converts an "agent shops on a real e-commerce
site" flow from "trust the DOM" to "Sardis hold + checkout mandate" — actions are
reversible until the agent explicitly captures.

precodepip install sardis-browser-use/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Stagehand / Browserbase

div class="int-title"sardis-stagehand

div class="int-desc"
Stagehand actions with a Sardis payment primitive attached. Agents running in
Browserbase sessions get the same hold/capture/policy model as any other Sardis agent.
TypeScript package.

precodenpm i sardis-stagehand/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Composio

div class="int-title"sardis-composio · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
Sardis tools in Composio's tool marketplace — so any agent already pulling tools from
Composio picks up Sardis without a code change beyond adding the tool name.

precodepip install sardis-composio/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"OpenClaw

div class="int-title"sardis-openclaw · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
OpenClaw skill — makes Sardis payments a native capability for OpenClaw's
computer-use agents. Same five-primitive pattern.

precodepip install sardis-openclaw/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"E2B

div class="int-title"sardis-e2b

div class="int-desc"
E2B sandbox template that ships Sardis SDK pre-installed and pre-authenticated to the
merchant's sandbox key. Agents that spin up E2B VMs to run code can immediately call
codesardis.pay(...)/code without a key-distribution dance.

precodee2b template init sardis-e2b/code

!-- Group 3: multi-agent / protocols --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 03 · Multi-agent protocols

## When agents talk to each other.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Agent-to-agent payments have a protocol problem before they have a payments problem.
Sardis ships first-class support for the two multi-agent standards that matter:
Google's A2A and Coinbase AgentKit.

div class="grid grid-2"

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Google A2A

div class="int-title"sardis-a2a · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Spec-compliant A2A server emand/em client. Agent card discovery at
code/.well-known/agent.json/code, JSON-RPC 2.0 task lifecycle, SSE streaming,
push notifications. Sardis extends with payment-domain skills (escrow, settlement,
trust verification) delivered as codeDataPart/code payloads — standards-conformant,
payments-aware.

precodepip install sardis-a2a/code

div class="int-meta mt-8"Exports: A2AClient, SardisAgentCard, A2APaymentRequest/Response, AgentDiscoveryService, SSE stream.

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Coinbase AgentKit

div class="int-title"sardis-agentkit · v0.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Sardis payment actions for Coinbase AgentKit. AgentKit handles on-chain identity;
Sardis handles policy and compliance on top — so a CDP-backed agent has
non-custodial wallets and natural-language spend rules without rebuilding either.

precodepip install sardis-agentkit/code

!-- Group 4: platform --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 04 · Platform

## Where agents run inside someone else's product.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Not every agent is a standalone script. These adapters plug Sardis into platforms where
non-engineers build agents: Vercel's AI SDK, n8n, Activepieces, ChatGPT custom GPTs.

div class="grid grid-2"

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Vercel AI SDK

div class="int-title"@sardis/ai-sdk · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
Sardis tools packaged as Vercel AI SDK codetool()/code definitions. Drop into
codegenerateText/code, codestreamText/code, or codeToolLoopAgent/code.
Ships ESM + CJS + browser bundles; type-safe via Zod.

precodenpm i @sardis/ai-sdk/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"MCP Server

div class="int-title"@sardis/mcp-server · v1.1.0

div class="int-desc"
50 Sardis tools over Model Context Protocol. Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf, or any
MCP host. Simulated mode lets a new user try Sardis from Claude Desktop without an
account. See canvas 14 for the full tool list.

precodenpx @sardis/mcp-server start/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"n8n

div class="int-title"n8n-nodes-sardis · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
Community node + credential. Drop Sardis actions (pay, hold, approve, balance, policy
check) into any n8n workflow. Credential type stores API keys in n8n's encrypted store.

precodenpm i -g n8n-nodes-sardis/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"Activepieces

div class="int-title"@sardis/activepieces · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
Sardis piece for Activepieces workflow platform. Policy-controlled payments as a
drag-and-drop block for non-engineer builders.

precodenpm i @sardis/activepieces/code

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"ChatGPT Custom GPT

div class="int-title"sardis-gpt

div class="int-desc"
OpenAPI actions spec + GPT instructions. Gives a ChatGPT custom GPT the ability to
pay, check balance, and enforce policies through Sardis — no backend code required
beyond hosting the action endpoint (which Sardis provides).

div class="int-meta mt-8"Artifacts: codeopenapi-actions.yaml/code, codeinstructions.md/code.

div class="int-card"
div class="int-label"OpenAI function-calling

div class="int-title"sardis-openai · v1.0.0

div class="int-desc"
Classic OpenAI function-calling wrapper. Complements codesardis-openai-agents/code
for teams still on the bare Chat Completions API.

precodepip install sardis-openai/code

!-- Adapter anatomy --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Adapter Anatomy

## Every adapter exposes the same five primitives.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The surface area is deliberately tiny. If an agent can do these five things safely, every
meaningful agent-payment use case falls out: order an API call, pay a vendor, refill a
balance, split a cost between two agents, tighten a policy mid-run.

table class="data"
thead
trthPrimitive
thServer op
thTypical agent use

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepay(to, amount, purpose)/code
tdcodePOST /v1/payments/code via codePaymentOrchestrator/code
tdAny outbound payment — API, vendor, user refund.

trtdcodecheck_balance(wallet_id)/code
tdcodeGET /v1/wallets/{id}/balance/code
tdDecide whether to spend.

trtdcodeset_policy(text | structured)/code
tdcodePOST /v1/policies/parse + apply/code
tdSelf-limit before a risky sub-task.

trtdcodecheck_policy(intent)/code
tdcodePOST /v1/policies/check/code
tdDry-run: would this pay succeed?

trtdcodelist_transactions(wallet_id)/code
tdcodeGET /v1/transactions/code
tdAgent retrospection / reporting.

/tbody
/table

!-- Integration telemetry --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Observability

## Where integration usage lands.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every tool invocation tags its payment with a framework hint (SDK-level
codeX-Sardis-Framework/code header). The public repo preserves that transport contract;
hosted analytics and dashboard visualizations live in the private product repo.

table class="data"
thead
trthSurface
thWhat it records

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeX-Sardis-Framework/code
tdFramework hint attached by SDKs and adapters.

trtdcodepackages/api/code
tdNormalizes framework metadata at the API boundary.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-ledger/code
tdPersists framework context next to transaction/evidence records where applicable.

trtdPrivate dashboard
tdAggregates per-framework spend, transaction counts, and policy hits.

/tbody
/table

!-- Release --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Release

## How integrations ship.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Python integrations go via code.github/workflows/release-python-integrations.yml/code
(tag pattern codepy-integration-v*/code). JS integrations go via
coderelease-npm.yml/code (tags: codeai-sdk-v*/code, codemcp-v*/code, etc.).
Each tag cuts a version-stable release; prerelease toggles go to TestPyPI / npm dist-tag.

table class="data"
theadtrthAdapter
thRegistry
thRelease tag prefix

/thead
tbody
trtdsardis-langchain
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-langchain-v*/code

trtdsardis-crewai
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-crewai-v*/code

trtdsardis-autogpt
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-autogpt-v*/code

trtdsardis-openai / openai-agents
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-openai-v*/code / codepy-openai-agents-v*/code

trtdsardis-agent-sdk
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-agent-sdk-v*/code

trtdsardis-adk / a2a
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-adk-v*/code / codepy-a2a-v*/code

trtdsardis-agentkit / browser-use
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-agentkit-v*/code / codepy-browser-use-v*/code

trtdsardis-composio / openclaw
tdPyPI
tdcodepy-composio-v*/code / codepy-openclaw-v*/code

trtd@sardis/ai-sdk
tdnpm
tdcodeai-sdk-v*/code

trtd@sardis/mcp-server
tdnpm
tdcodemcp-v*/code

trtd@sardis/activepieces
tdnpm
tdcodeactivepieces-v*/code

trtdn8n-nodes-sardis
tdnpm
tdcoden8n-v*/code

trtdsardis-stagehand
tdnpm
tdcodestagehand-v*/code

trtdsardis-e2b
tdE2B template registry
td—

trtdsardis-gpt
tdChatGPT actions (hosted)
td—

/tbody
/table

=====================================
# EXTERNAL
https://canvas.sardis.sh/external
=====================================

Canvas title="17 · External Services — Sardis Canvas" currentId="external"

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 17 · Ops/span

## External Services

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis is not a vertical monolith. It is a control plane above a carefully chosen set of
third-party services — MPC custody, KYC, sanctions, card issuers, PSPs, ramps, RPC, rails,
observability. This canvas enumerates every one, where it's wired, and why it beat its
alternative.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"Single source of truth/span
Every config class below lives in
codepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py/code. Every env var uses a typed
prefix (e.g. codeTURNKEY_/code, codeSARDIS_STRIGA_/code) so a missing value fails
fast at boot, not at the first payment.

!-- Group 01 MPC --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 01 · MPC / Signing

## Private keys never live in Sardis.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every wallet signature routes through an MPC provider. Turnkey is the default;
Fireblocks, Circle's Programmable Wallets, and Lit Protocol are pluggable behind the
same interface. Canvas a href="/wallet-mpc"09/a covers the wallet lifecycle.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thConfig class
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdTurnkey
tdcodeTurnkeyConfig/code
tdcodeTURNKEY_/code
tdLive (primary)
tdSub-org per tenant, API-signed requests, deterministic HD addressing. Keys never leave Turnkey.

trtdFireblocks
tdpluggable
td—
tdInterface-ready
tdEnterprise alternative; picked on a per-merchant basis when compliance requires SOC2 Type II from the custody provider.

trtdCircle Programmable Wallets
tdpluggable
td—
tdInterface-ready
tdFalls out naturally where Circle CPN / Gateway are already wired.

trtdLit Protocol
tdpluggable
td—
tdResearch
tdThreshold MPC with policy at the key — future path for "self-custodial agent" narratives.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 02 KYC --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 02 · KYC / AML

## Compliance without a compliance team.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Canvas a href="/compliance"12/a has the full flow. Didit won the KYC slot on
cost + velocity; Elliptic remains the AML/sanctions screen.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thPurpose
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdDidit
tdKYC (identity verification)
tdcodeDIDIT_/code
tdLive (primary)
td500 free/month, $0.33/check after; YC W26; clean v3 API with x-api-key auth; webhook working end-to-end. Replaced iDenfy (abandoned) and Persona (rejected).

trtdElliptic
tdAML + sanctions screening
tdcodeELLIPTIC_/code
tdLive
tdChain-aware sanctions screening; covers OFAC + sectoral lists; embedded in the Pre-Execution Pipeline as a fail-closed check.

trtdPersona
td—
td—
tdRejected
tdPriced for enterprise; minimum commit too high for a single-founder startup.

trtdiDenfy
td—
td—
tdAbandoned
tdEarlier integration; replaced by Didit after onboarding friction.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 03 Cards --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 03 · Card issuing

## Virtual cards + agent cards.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Multi-provider by design — codeCardStackConfig/code routes between Stripe, Lithic,
Rain, Bridge, and Striga. One interface, five issuers. Stripe is the launch default;
Lithic stays in sandbox until funding allows the $5M program commit.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thConfig class
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdStripe Issuing
tdcodeStripeConfig/code
tdcodeSTRIPE_/code
tdLive (primary)
tdAccessible without $5M program commit; pairs with existing Stripe Connect + Treasury plumbing. codestablecoin_issuing_enabled/code flag flips on the USDC-settled variant.

trtdLithic
tdcodeLithicConfig/code
tdcodeLITHIC_/code
tdSandbox
tdDeep controls (ASA webhooks, on-card policies). Held back until funding qualifies for the program.

trtdRain
tdcodeRainConfig/code
tdcodeRAIN_/code
tdInterface-ready
tdStablecoin-native card program; drops in via codeCardStackConfig/code with zero core changes.

trtdBridge Cards
tdcodeBridgeCardsConfig/code
tdcodeBRIDGE_/code
tdInterface-ready
tdMulti-rail (ACH/SEPA/PIX/wire) — issuer + funding in one integration.

trtdStriga Cards
tdcodeStrigaConfig/code
tdcodeSARDIS_STRIGA_/code
tdInterface-ready
tdEEA issuance with a clean SEPA/vIBAN story; complements Bridge outside the US.

trtdCoinbase CDP (on-chain provider)
tdcodeCoinbaseConfig/code
tdcodeCOINBASE_/code
tdLive
tdOn-chain provider slot — wallets as cards when an agent wants to transact purely on-chain.

trtdCard stack router
tdcodeCardStackConfig/code
tdcodeSARDIS_CARDS_/code
tdLive
tdPicks primary / fallback / on-chain provider, with per-org overrides via JSON.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 04 PSPs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 04 · PSPs & Payment Rails

## Money movement beyond crypto.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thConfig class
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdStripe Connect
tdcodeStripeConfig/code
tdcodeSTRIPE_/code
tdLive
tdMerchant payouts (fiat off-ramp, US-first). codeconnected_account_map_json/code maps tenants to Stripe accounts.

trtdStripe Treasury
tdcodeStripeConfig/code
tdcodeSTRIPE_/code
tdLive
tdFinancial-account-backed balance behind Issuing.

trtdStripe MPP (Tempo)
tdcodeStripeConfig/code
tdcodeSTRIPE_/code
tdLive
tdEarly access since 2026-03-18 launch. Sardis is first-class on Tempo mainnet.

trtdStripe Billing
tdcodeStripeBillingConfig/code
tdcodeSTRIPE_BILLING_/code
tdReady
tdSubscription billing path for the operator-facing product (post Atlas entity).

trtdLightspark (Grid API)
tdcodeLightsparkConfig/code
tdcodeSARDIS_LIGHTSPARK_/code
tdSandbox
tdLightning + UMA (codeuma_domain=sardis.sh/code) + FX + payouts. Gated by codeenabled/code flag.

trtdBridge (multi-rail)
tdcodeBridgeCardsConfig/code
tdcodeBRIDGE_/code
tdInterface-ready
tdOne integration for ACH + SEPA + PIX + wire; selects by codedefault_rail/code + per-feature flags.

trtdStriga (EEA banking)
tdcodeStrigaConfig/code
tdcodeSARDIS_STRIGA_/code
tdSandbox
tdvIBANs, SEPA, Lightning, standing orders, KYC — EEA-native fiat rails.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 05 Ramps + fiat --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 05 · On/Off Ramps & Fiat Rails

## USD ↔ USDC without writing a bank.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thConfig class
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdCoinbase CDP Onramp
tdcodeCoinbaseConfig/code
tdcodeCOINBASE_/code
tdLive
tdHosted fiat → USDC onramp, zero infra cost. codetopup_api_key/code drives the top-up variant.

trtdBridge Offramp
tdcodeBridgeCardsConfig/code
tdcodeBRIDGE_/code
tdInterface-ready
tdUSDC → fiat via ACH/SEPA/PIX.

trtdCircle CPN
tdcodeCircleCPNConfig/code
tdcodeCIRCLE_CPN_/code
tdLive
tdCircle Payments Network — funding / payouts; codestrategy=fiat_first/code + codeprimary_adapter=circle_cpn/code by default in codeFundingRoutingConfig/code.

trtdCircle Gateway
tdcodeCircleGatewayConfig/code
tdcodeCIRCLE_GATEWAY_/code
tdGated
tdx402 facilitator / nanopayments, enabled via codex402_enabled/code.

trtdStripe (funding)
tdcodeStripeConfig/code
tdcodeSTRIPE_/code
tdLive
tdStripe-backed funding for Issuing top-ups.

trtdFunding router
tdcodeFundingRoutingConfig/code
td—
tdLive
tdStrategy: codefiat_first/code / codestablecoin_first/code / codehybrid/code. Primary + fallback adapters are explicit.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 06 Chain RPC --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 06 · Chain RPC

## Broadcast + read.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
RPC endpoints are env-var driven (codeSARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL/code, etc.) with public
RPC as a fallback. Canvas a href="/settlement"10/a covers multi-chain
settlement.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thEnv var
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdAlchemy (Base)
tdcodeSARDIS_BASE_RPC_URL/code
tdLive (primary)
tdFast, reliable, free-tier-generous; first choice for Base 8453.

trtdAlchemy (Ethereum/Polygon/Arbitrum/Optimism)
tdcodeSARDIS_chain_RPC_URL/code
tdLive
tdUniform provider across EVM chains.

trtdTempo RPC
tdcodeSARDIS_TEMPO_RPC_URL/code (default coderpc.tempo.xyz/code)
tdLive (mainnet 4217)
tdFirst-party Tempo RPC; dedicated executor in codesardis-chain/src/sardis_chain/tempo/executor.py/code.

trtdPublic RPCs
tdfallback list
tdFallback only
tdUsed if primary RPC fails; never primary in prod.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 07 Protocol rails --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 07 · Protocol Rails

## Card network + credential standards.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thConfig class
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdVisa TAP
tdcodeVisaTAPConfig/code
tdcodeVISA_TAP_/code
tdSandbox
tdToken Authentication Protocol — mTLS + TRID, signed tokens for agent identity at card-network level.

trtdMastercard Agent Pay / MDES
tdcodeMastercardConfig/code
tdcodeMASTERCARD_/code
tdSandbox
tdP12/PKCS12 certificate auth; MDES token provisioning for agent-initiated card transactions.

trtdDelegated Credentials
tdcodeDelegatedCredentialConfig/code
td—
tdInterface-ready
tdFramework for protocol-layer delegation (beyond AP2 / A2A).

trtdx402
tdcodeX402Config/code
tdcodeX402_/code
tdGated
tdcodeenabled/code / codeserver_enabled/code / codefacilitator_enabled/code toggles; networks codebase,polygon,ethereum/code; optional Permit2.

trtdERC-8183
tdcodeERC8183Config/code
td—
tdLive
tdOn-chain agent-payment standard; 1% fee cap, USDC-only MVP; canvas 06.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 08 DB / cache --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 08 · Database & Cache

## Stateful backbone.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thEnv var
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdNeon Postgres
tdcodeDATABASE_URL/code
tdLive
tdServerless Postgres; 107 migrations applied in order; branching for preview envs.

trtdUpstash Redis
tdcodeSARDIS_REDIS_URL/code
tdLive
tdRate-limiter (fail-closed if unreachable in prod), dedup store, replay cache, SSE fanout. Mandatory outside dev.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 09 Observability --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 09 · Observability

## Knowing when something's wrong.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thWhere it lives
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdPostHog
tdPrivate product dashboard + public landing telemetry where configured
tdLive
tdProduct analytics, funnels, retention, and feature flags outside the OSS protocol surface.

trtdSentry
tdcode@sentry/nextjs/code (dashboard) + Python Sentry in API
tdLive
tdErrors, performance, session replay. Integrated with Claude Code review via plugin.

trtdIntercom
tdcode@intercom/messenger-js-sdk/code
tdLive
tdIn-app support chat for operators and merchants.

trtdUptime (internal)
td24 GitHub Actions workflows (codeuptime*.yml/code)
tdLive
tdPeriodic health checks of API, landing, dashboard; metrics published as graph artefacts.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 10 Comms / Auth --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 10 · Comms & Auth

## Email + identity.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thWhere it lives
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdResend
tdAPI notifications + dashboard transactional email
tdLive
tdTransactional email (receipts, approvals, security alerts). Simple API, modern DX.

trtdbetter-auth
tdPrivate product dashboard + API JWKS bridge
tdLive
tdEdDSA/JWKS sessions, passkeys, org roles, and API-key issuance outside the public protocol repo.

trtdLegacy FastAPI JWT (HS256)
tdcodepackages/api/code
tdLive
tdDual-path auth for SDK clients; bridged to better-auth via JWKS.

trtdStripe Atlas
tdCompany entity
tdLive (since 2026-03-21)
tdDelaware C-corp "Sardis Labs, Inc." formed via Atlas; enables production Stripe + banking relationships.

trtdPolar.sh
tdBilling (temp until Atlas Stripe clears)
tdLive
tdBridge revenue path: Polar now, Stripe once Atlas entity is fully wired.

trtdTurnstile + CaptchaFox
tdDashboard sign-up / reset
tdLive
tdBot-prevention on public auth endpoints.

/tbody
/table

!-- Group 11 FIDES (internal but external-facing) --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Group 11 · Trust Graph

## FIDES — decentralized trust signalling.

table class="data"
theadtrthService
thConfig class
thEnv prefix
thStatus
thWhy

/thead
tbody
trtdFIDES (Sardis protocol)
tdcodeFidesConfig/code
tdcodeSARDIS_FIDES_/code
tdLive
tdAgent + merchant trust graph consulted in the Pre-Execution Pipeline. Fail-closed if unreachable.

/tbody
/table

!-- Diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Shape

## How these services plug into the spine.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every external dependency is behind an interface in codesardis-core/code so
individual providers can be swapped without touching business logic — the Didit-over-Persona
swap happened with zero code churn outside the KYC adapter.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
API["Sardis API / Pre-Execution Pipeline"] -- KYC["Didit"]
API -- AML["Elliptic"]
API -- FID["FIDES trust graph"]
API -- ORCH["PaymentOrchestrator"]

ORCH -- MPC["Turnkey · Fireblocks · Circle · Lit"]
ORCH -- CHAIN["Alchemy / Tempo / public RPC"]

API -- FUND["Funding router"]
FUND -- CCPN["Circle CPN"]
FUND -- BRIDGE["Bridge (ACH/SEPA/PIX)"]
FUND -- STRIPE["Stripe Treasury + Connect"]
FUND -- ONRAMP["Coinbase CDP Onramp"]
FUND -- STRIGA["Striga (EEA)"]
FUND -- LS["Lightspark (Lightning / UMA)"]

API -- CARDS["CardStack router"]
CARDS -- STR["Stripe Issuing"]
CARDS -- LIT["Lithic"]
CARDS -- RAIN["Rain"]
CARDS -- BRC["Bridge Cards"]
CARDS -- STC["Striga Cards"]

API -- NET["Visa TAP · Mastercard MDES · x402 · ERC-8183"]
API -- DB["Neon Postgres"]
API -- REDIS["Upstash Redis"]

DASH["Dashboard · Next.js"] -- BA["better-auth (EdDSA/JWKS)"]
DASH -- POLAR["Polar.sh billing"]
DASH -- POSTHOG["PostHog"]
DASH -- SENTRY["Sentry"]
DASH -- INTERCOM["Intercom"]

[/diagram]

!-- Summary / status --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Snapshot

## Live vs. sandbox vs. interface-ready.

table class="data"
theadtrthStatus
thServices

/thead
tbody
trtdLive (production)
tdTurnkey, Didit, Elliptic, Stripe (Issuing + Treasury + Connect + MPP), Coinbase CDP (Onramp), Circle CPN, Alchemy (Base, Ethereum, Polygon, Arbitrum, Optimism), Tempo RPC, Neon, Upstash, better-auth, Polar.sh, PostHog, Sentry, Intercom, Resend, Turnstile, FIDES, ERC-8183.

trtdSandbox / gated
tdLithic, Striga, Lightspark, Visa TAP, Mastercard MDES, x402, Circle Gateway, Bridge Cards.

trtdInterface-ready
tdFireblocks, Circle Programmable Wallets, Lit Protocol, Rain, Bridge Offramp.

trtdRejected / retired
tdPersona (too expensive), iDenfy (onboarding friction), Lithic production (needs $5M program commit).

/tbody
/table

!-- Source of truth --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Go read the code, not this table, before quoting.

table class="data"
theadtrthQuestion
thFile

/thead
tbody
trtdComplete list of external configs?
tdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py/code (codegrep '^class.*Config'/code)

trtdChain RPC URLs + chain IDs?
tdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/config.py/code → codeChainConfig/code list

trtdToken contracts per chain?
tdcodepackages/sardis-core/src/sardis_v2_core/tokens.py/code

trtdCard routing?
tdcodeCardStackConfig/code in codeconfig.py/code

trtdFunding routing?
tdcodeFundingRoutingConfig/code in codeconfig.py/code

trtdDeployed env vars?
tdCloud Run (API) + Vercel (dashboard + landing + checkout) project settings — source of truth.

/tbody
/table

!-- Cross-refs --

div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper

/Canvas


=====================================
# CICD
https://canvas.sardis.sh/cicd
=====================================

Canvas title="18 · CI/CD — Sardis Canvas" currentId="cicd"

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 18 · Ops/span

## CI / CD

p class="hero-lead"
Twenty-five GitHub Actions workflows guard the repo. Four classes: tests on every PR,
security scans on every push and weekly, deploys on merge to codemain/code, publishes on tag.
One branch is production (codemain/code). Everything else is preview.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
codemain/code is the only long-lived branch. Every PR runs the full CI matrix (Python,
contracts, TS SDK, and landing) plus security. Merge to codemain/code triggers
path-scoped deploys: API → Cloud Run codeus-east1/code, Landing → Vercel.
Releases to PyPI and npm fire on version tags. Uptime pings every 10 minutes.

!-- Overview --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Overview

## Branch strategy and gating.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The default branch is codemain/code. PRs against it run the CI and security suites but
do not publish. Merges to codemain/code trigger the deploy workflows that match changed
paths — only the pieces that actually changed redeploy.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"What runs on a PR

div class="card-description"
codeci.yml/code (Python lint+test, contracts strict + gas, TS SDK,
landing build), codetest-api.yml/code (sardis-api scoped tests), codecodeql.yml/code
(Python + JS/TS), codesecret-scan.yml/code (gitleaks), codesecurity-scan.yml/code
(pip-audit + Trivy + Bandit), codefuzz.yml/code (atheris against spending_policy).
Path filters skip irrelevant workflows.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"What runs on merge to main

div class="card-description"
codedeploy-api-cloudrun.yml/code, codedeploy-landing.yml/code,
codedeploy.yml/code — each gated by a
codepaths:/code filter so a docs-only merge doesn't redeploy the API. Verification step
(codecurl /health/code) runs after each Cloud Run deploy.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"What runs on a version tag

div class="card-description"
coderelease-python-sdk.yml/code (PyPI / TestPyPI — codepy-sdk-v*/code),
coderelease-python-integrations.yml/code (batch publish for langchain/crewai/adk/agent-sdk/connect),
coderelease-npm.yml/code (codemcp-v*/code, codesdk-js-v*/code, codeai-sdk-v*/code,
codeconnect-js-v*/code), codepublish.yml/code, codesign-artifacts.yml/code.
Each verifies pyproject/package.json version matches the tag, runs tests, builds, publishes,
then smoke-installs from the registry.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"What runs on a schedule

div class="card-description"
codemonitoring.yml/code every 10 min (synthetic code/health/code probe +
Slack notify on failure); codeuptime*.yml/code (codeuptime/code,
codeuptime-response-time/code, codeuptime-graphs/code, codeuptime-static-site/code,
codeuptime-summary/code); codenightly-sandbox-e2e.yml/code 04:20 UTC;
codecodeql.yml/code weekly Monday; codescorecard.yml/code weekly Monday;
codefuzz.yml/code weekly Saturday.

!-- Pipeline diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Pipeline

## PR → preview → production.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Simplified view. Not every workflow is shown — see the tables below for full coverage.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
Dev[Developer branch] -- PR[PR against main]
PR -- CI[ci.yml + test-api.yml]
PR -- Sec[codeql · secret-scan · security-scan · fuzz]
PR -- PrevVercel[Vercel preview URLs]
CI -- Gate{All green?}
Sec -- Gate
Gate -- no -- Dev
Gate -- yes -- Merge[Merge to main]
Merge -- PathFilter{paths: changed?}
PathFilter -- sardis-api -- Cloudbuild[Cloud Build]
Cloudbuild -- CloudRun[Cloud Run · us-east1]
CloudRun -- HealthCheck[curl /health]
PathFilter -- apps/landing -- VercelLand[Vercel · sardis.sh]
Tag[Version tag py-sdk-v* / sdk-js-v* / ...] -- ReleaseMatrix[release-python-sdk · release-npm · release-python-integrations]
ReleaseMatrix -- Registry[PyPI · npm]
Registry -- Smoke[Smoke install from registry]

[/diagram]

!-- CI workflows --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"CI

## Test workflows.

table class="data"
thead
trthWorkflow
thTrigger
thJobs
thWhat it proves

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodeci.yml/code

tdpush main · PR

tdpython-lint-test · idempotency-replay-e2e · contracts-gas · contracts-strict · typescript-sdk · build-landing

tdruff + pytest with code--cov-fail-under=40/code on core/api, forge gas ceiling and full strict test suite (solc 0.8.24 pinned), TS SDK + MCP build and tests, landing typecheck + build.

tr
tdcodetest-api.yml/code

tdpush main · PR (scoped to codesardis-api//code and codesardis-core//code)

tdAPI Lint & Test

tdFaster feedback loop for API changes: ruff + pytest on api/core with codeSARDIS_ENVIRONMENT=dev/code.

tr
tdcodeci-integrations.yml/code

tdpush main · PR

tdper-framework integration builds

tdValidates that every framework SDK in codepackages/sardis-*/code still builds and imports.

tr
tdidempotency-replay gate (in codeci.yml/code)

tdPR

tdE2E script codescripts/release/idempotency_replay_e2e_check.sh/code

tdConfirms the Phase-3 dedup store and replay cache still refuse to double-execute a payment.

/tbody
/table

!-- Security workflows --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Security

## Scans and attestations.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every workflow pins actions by commit SHA, not tag. Default permissions are empty
(codepermissions: {}/code) and each job declares the minimum it needs.

table class="data"
thead
trthWorkflow
thTrigger
thWhat it does

/thead
tbody
trtdcodecodeql.yml/code
tdpush · PR · weekly Mon 05:30 UTC
tdCodeQL on Python and JavaScript/TypeScript. Uploads SARIF to GitHub Security.

trtdcodesecret-scan.yml/code
tdpush · PR
tdgitleaks full-history scan (codefetch-depth: 0/code). Fails the run on any detection.

trtdcodesecurity-scan.yml/code
tdpush · PR · weekly Mon 06:00 UTC
tdThree jobs: codepip-audit/code (Python deps), Trivy container scan of the built API image (fails on CRITICAL/HIGH, codeignore-unfixed/code), Bandit SAST on codesardis//code + codepackages/api/src/code + codepackages/sardis-core/src/code.

trtdcodescorecard.yml/code
tdpush · weekly Mon 06:00 UTC
tdOpenSSF Scorecard → SARIF → uploaded to GitHub Security. Tracks branch protection, token permissions, pinned deps, SAST, signed releases.

trtdcodesign-artifacts.yml/code
tdrelease tag
tdProduces signed build provenance for published artifacts. Complements npm code--provenance/code in coderelease-npm.yml/code.

trtdcodefuzz.yml/code
tdpush · PR · weekly Sat 08:00 UTC
tdatheris fuzzer against codetests/fuzz/fuzz_spending_policy.py/code, capped at codeFUZZING_DURATION=60/codes for PRs.

/tbody
/table

!-- Deploy workflows --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Deploy

## Production deploys.

table class="data"
thead
trthWorkflow
thTarget
thPath filter
thNotes

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcodedeploy-api-cloudrun.yml/code

tdCloud Run · codeus-east1/code · service codesardis-api/code

tdcodepackages/api/**/code, codesardis-core/**/code, codesardis-chain/**/code

tdGCP OIDC via Workload Identity Federation (no long-lived keys). Build through codecloudbuild.yaml/code, deploy, then codecurl /health/code — non-200 fails the run. If codeGCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER/code is missing, the job fails loudly with remediation instructions (previously silently skipped).

tr
tdcodedeploy-landing.yml/code

tdVercel · codesardis.sh/code

tdcodeapps/landing/**/code, codepnpm-lock.yaml/code

tdBuild job (typecheck + lint + build) on every PR for preview. Deploy job is codeif: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'/code — prod only.

tr
tdcodedeploy.yml/code

td(legacy orchestrator / retained)

td—

tdBroad-spectrum deploy workflow kept for manual codeworkflow_dispatch/code redeploys.

/tbody

!-- Release workflows --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Release

## Publish to registries.

table class="data"
thead
trthWorkflow
thTag pattern
thRegistry
thGuardrails

/thead
tbody
tr
tdcoderelease-python-sdk.yml/code

tdcodepy-sdk-v*/code

tdPyPI (or TestPyPI for codea/code/codeb/code/coderc/code)

tdVerifies codepyproject.toml/code + code__init__.py/code versions match the tag. Runs codepytest/code, codepython -m build/code, codetwine check/code, upload, then smoke-installs from the registry with 6-retry backoff.

tr
tdcoderelease-python-integrations.yml/code

tdcodepy-langchain-v*/code, codepy-crewai-v*/code, codepy-adk-v*/code, codepy-agent-sdk-v*/code, codepy-connect-v*/code

tdPyPI / TestPyPI

tdSame shape as sardis-sdk release: version consistency check, build, twine, upload, smoke install + import check.

tr
tdcoderelease-npm.yml/code

tdcodemcp-v*/code, codesdk-js-v*/code, codeai-sdk-v*/code, codeconnect-js-v*/code

tdnpm (code--access public --provenance/code)

tdVerifies codepackage.json/code version, runs pnpm tests + typecheck + build, codenpm pack --dry-run/code, asserts built artifacts exist (codedist/index.js/code / codedist/cli.js/code), publishes with codenext/code or codelatest/code dist-tag, smoke installs via codenpx/code or dynamic codeimport()/code.

trtdcodepublish.yml/code
tdmanual + tags
tdnpm / PyPI
tdUmbrella publish orchestrator.

trtdcoderelease-dry-run.yml/code
tdPR · workflow_dispatch
tdnone
tdDry-run of the whole release pipeline — builds, twine check, codenpm pack --dry-run/code — without publishing.

trtdcodesign-artifacts.yml/code
tdrelease tag
td—
tdSigns built artifacts; npm provenance is already set on coderelease-npm.yml/code.

/tbody
/table

!-- Monitoring --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Monitoring

## Synthetic checks from CI.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
GitHub Actions is cheap enough that we use it as a second uptime system alongside the
application's own health endpoints. Failures Slack us.

table class="data"
thead
trthWorkflow
thSchedule
thWhat it checks

/thead
tbody
trtdcodemonitoring.yml/code
tdevery 10 min
tdcodeGET /api/v2/health/code — asserts 200 + payload codestatus in {ok, healthy, partial}/code, warns if response time 2.0s, Slack-webhook notify on failure.

trtdcodeuptime.yml/code
tdscheduled
tdBase uptime probe aggregated into the public status page.

trtdcodeuptime-response-time.yml/code
tdscheduled
tdRecords response-time histograms.

trtdcodeuptime-graphs.yml/code
tdscheduled
tdGenerates historical graphs rendered on the status site.

trtdcodeuptime-static-site.yml/code
tdscheduled
tdBuilds and publishes the static status site.

trtdcodeuptime-summary.yml/code
tdscheduled
tdRolls checks into the summary feed.

/tbody

!-- Nightly + Docs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Nightly + Docs

## Long-running and documentation pipelines.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"codenightly-sandbox-e2e.yml/code

div class="card-description"
Runs 04:20 UTC. codebash scripts/ci/run_nightly_sandbox_smoke.sh/code against
codeSARDIS_SANDBOX_BASE_URL/code. Gated live E2E behind
codeRUN_LIVE_SANDBOX_TESTS=1/code (set via codeworkflow_dispatch/code input) —
when enabled, exercises Stripe, Lithic, and Coinbase CDP with real sandbox credentials.
45-minute timeout.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"codedocs.yml/code

div class="card-description"
Builds and deploys the documentation site on changes to codedocs//code. Mirrors the
Vercel shape of the public landing app: build on PR for preview, deploy on merge to codemain/code.

!-- Gunicorn + deploy gotchas --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Gotchas

## Deploy lessons paid for in incidents.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Things that silently failed at least once and now have permanent fixes in the deploy
workflow and container config. Worth knowing before you touch Cloud Run config.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Gunicorn 25.x — use call syntax, not code--factory/code

div class="card-description"
Launch the API as code"sardis_server.main:create_app()"/code. The code--factory/code
flag was removed in gunicorn 25.x; using it produces a cryptic boot failure that looks
like a module-not-found error. Docker codeCMD/code, codecloudbuild.yaml/code,
and codedeploy-api-cloudrun.yml/code encode the call form.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Install gunicorn + uvicorn inside code.venv/code

div class="card-description"
The container's system Python shouldn't host gunicorn — uv installs into code.venv/code,
so gunicorn must live there too. Otherwise Cloud Run boots with the wrong interpreter and
the app never binds its port, tripping the startup probe.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Always codegcloud ... --update-env-vars/code, never code--set-env-vars/code

div class="card-description"
code--set-env-vars/code silently wipes every other env var on the service. Use
code--update-env-vars/code to merge when changing a live service by hand; private
deployment helper scripts live outside the public repo.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Workload Identity Federation, not SA keys

div class="card-description"
codedeploy-api-cloudrun.yml/code authenticates through
codegoogle-github-actions/auth/code with
codeGCP_WORKLOAD_IDENTITY_PROVIDER/code + codeGCP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT/code. No
JSON key lives in GitHub secrets. If the WIF provider secret is missing, the job
fails loudly (older behaviour silently skipped — backend changes never shipped for weeks).

!-- Cross-refs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper

## Related canvases.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/architecture"
div class="canvas-card-num"01 · System

div class="canvas-card-title"Architecture Overview

div class="canvas-card-desc"The seven-layer stack these pipelines deploy.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/security"
div class="canvas-card-num"19 · Ops

div class="canvas-card-title"Security Model

div class="canvas-card-desc"Trust boundaries, fail-closed defaults, secret handling — all of which CI enforces.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# SECURITY
https://canvas.sardis.sh/security
=====================================

Canvas title="19 · Security Model — Sardis Canvas" currentId="security"

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 19 · Ops/span

## Security Model

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis handles money on behalf of AI agents that can be prompt-injected, API keys that can leak,
and merchants that may be malicious. The security model is a chain of small, auditable decisions
— every one of them fail-closed — anchored by an append-only ledger so nobody, including us, can
quietly edit history.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
No private keys in Sardis memory. No silent fallbacks. Every payment traverses the
codePreExecutionPipeline/code before a signature is requested. Every decision is written
to codepolicy_decisions/code and anchored on-chain. One kill switch freezes every wallet in
under a second.

!-- Trust boundaries --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Trust Boundaries

## Who trusts whom, and for what.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A trust boundary is where a request crosses from a less-trusted to a more-trusted context —
or vice versa. Every boundary is also an audit point. This is the actual topology, not an
aspirational one.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart TB
subgraph Untrusted[Untrusted zone]
Agent[AI Agent · LangChain / CrewAI / custom]
Merchant[Merchant checkout embed]
Public[Public internet]
end

subgraph Boundary1[Boundary 1 · API gateway]
ApiKey[API key + HMAC / JWT]
TAP[TAP verification]
CORS[CORS · CSP · HSTS]
RateLimit[Rate limiter · Redis]
end

subgraph SemiTrusted[Semi-trusted · Sardis core]
Orchestrator[PaymentOrchestrator]
Pipeline[PreExecutionPipeline]
Ledger[Ledger + policy_decisions]
end

subgraph Boundary2[Boundary 2 · MPC + chain]
Turnkey[Turnkey · sub-org per tenant]
Alchemy[Alchemy RPC]
Chain[Base / Tempo mainnet]
end

subgraph Boundary3[Boundary 3 · Compliance providers]
Didit[Didit KYC]
Elliptic[Elliptic AML]
Stripe[Stripe Connect / Issuing]
end

Agent -- ApiKey
Merchant -- CORS
Public -- TAP
ApiKey -- RateLimit
CORS -- RateLimit
TAP -- RateLimit
RateLimit -- Orchestrator
Orchestrator -- Pipeline
Pipeline -- Ledger
Pipeline -- Didit
Pipeline -- Elliptic
Orchestrator -- Turnkey
Turnkey -- Alchemy
Alchemy -- Chain
Orchestrator -- Stripe

[/diagram]

!-- Boundary details --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Boundary detail

## What each boundary asserts.

table class="data"
thead
trthBoundary
thDirection
thEnforced by
thFailure mode

/thead
tbody
trtdAgent → API
tduntrusted → semi
tdAPI key hashed SHA-256 at rest, CORS, rate limit, request-body size limit (codeRequestBodyLimitMiddleware/code)
tdDeny, log with request ID

trtdProtocol payload → API
tduntrusted → semi
tdTAP verification middleware (JWKS, 1h TTL, 4h max-stale before reject)
tdReject when JWKS unreachable beyond max-stale

trtdAPI → Orchestrator
tdsemi → semi
tdcoderequire_principal/code dependency, codePaymentOrchestrator.execute()/code as single chokepoint
tdNo chain_executor bypass — DI lockdown (Phase 2)

trtdOrchestrator → MPC
tdsemi → provider
tdTurnkey sub-org per tenant, ECDSA-signed API requests
tdSignature request denied by Turnkey policy

trtdOrchestrator → Compliance
tdsemi → provider
tdDidit KYC, Elliptic AML
tdUnreachable provider → deny (fail-closed)

trtdLedger → Chain
tdinternal → public
tdcodeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code Merkle root
tdBatch must settle on-chain before evidence export is authoritative

/tbody
/table

!-- Threat model --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Threat model

## Who breaks in, and what stops them.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Each row is an actor we actively design against. The "mitigations" column is code that
already exists — not a wishlist.

table class="data"
thead
trthActor
thAttack
thMitigations
thResidual risk

/thead
tbody
tr
tdCompromised agent

tdPrompt injection tricks the agent into calling codepay()/code with a hostile recipient / amount.

tdNon-custodial (no keys to steal), spending policy caps per-txn and per-window, AGIT fail-closed, anomaly scorer (codeanomaly_detection.py/code), human approval for risky actions (codeapproval_service.py/code).

tdAgent can drain up to policy cap. Mitigation: tight caps + approval thresholds.

tr
tdCompromised API key

tdKey leaks via logs, model output, or client-side reverse-engineer.

tdKeys hashed SHA-256 at rest, rate limiter fail-closed if Redis unreachable, anomaly detector flags new-geo usage, kill switch freezes wallets in one call.

tdSmall window before anomaly fires. Mitigation: customer rotation workflow + audit alerts.

tr
tdCompromised MPC provider

tdAttacker gains Turnkey org-level access.

tdSub-org per tenant — blast radius capped at one customer. Turnkey policy engine enforces the same spending rules server-side.

tdCatastrophic if root org compromised. Mitigation: provider abstraction lets us add Fireblocks/Circle/Lit as a second root.

tr
tdMalicious merchant

tdMerchant fakes agent signals to claim funds, or sets up checkout to phish users.

tdMerchant trust scoring, capability gating, embed-origin CORS restriction (codeembed_origin/code column, migration 031), session codeclient_secret/code + codeidempotency_key/code unique constraint, codeSELECT … FOR UPDATE NOWAIT/code on settlement.

tdPolicy-level abuse (false advertising etc.). Mitigation: legal review + offboarding pipeline.

tr
tdInsider (Sardis employee)

tdStaff modifies a ledger entry to hide a bad decision.

tdAppend-only codeledger_entries/code, codepolicy_decisions/code writes per request, Merkle-anchored batches via codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code, evidence export signed by chain state.

tdA re-anchor could theoretically rewrite recent history if insider controls the deployer key — but divergence is visible on-chain.

tr
tdReplay / forgery

tdAttacker re-submits a valid mandate to execute again.

tdPostgres + Redis replay cache (codeMandateArchive/code + codededup_store.py/code), nonce enforcement, DB-level codeidempotency_key/code unique constraint (migration 031), mandate TTLs.

tdLow — doubled on two independent stores.

tr
tdProvider outage

tdDidit / Elliptic / Turnkey down — tempts "just approve for now."

tdEvery pipeline step defaults to deny on timeout or error. No "allow on failure" path in prod.

tdAvailability hit — accepted trade-off against silent compromise.

/tbody
/table

!-- Fail-closed matrix --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Fail-closed

## What each layer does when something goes wrong.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The override flag codeSARDIS_AGIT_FAIL_OPEN=true/code exists for local dev only. Default
for codeagit_fail_open/code in codeconfig.py/code is codeFalse/code. Production
must deny.

table class="data"
thead
trthLayer
thControl
thOn normal path
thOn failure

/thead
tbody
trtdAPI gateway
tdRate limiter (Redis)
tdAllow under threshold
tdRedis unreachable → deny. No in-memory fallback in prod.

trtdAPI gateway
tdTAP JWKS fetch
tdVerify signature
tdJWKS fetch fails, stale cache codeSARDIS_TAP_JWKS_MAX_STALE_SECONDS/code → reject.

trtdPreExecutionPipeline
tdSpending policy
tdEvaluate against natural-language rule
tdEvaluator raises → deny + ledger entry with reason.

trtdPreExecutionPipeline
tdAGIT (Agent Identity and Trust)
tdChain-of-trust lookup
tdUnreachable → codesettings.agit_fail_open=False/code rejects. codecontrol_plane.py/code line 210.

trtdPreExecutionPipeline
tdKYA / FIDES
tdScore agent + counterparty
tdProvider error → deny.

trtdPreExecutionPipeline
tdMCC classifier
tdMap merchant category
tdUnknown MCC → deny (codemcc_service.py/code line 103).

trtdCompliance
tdAML / sanctions screening
tdGreen → proceed
tdNo sanctions service → deny deposit (codeinbound_payment_service.py/code). Any raised exception → deny.

trtdOrchestrator
tdExecutionMode
tdHonour explicit mode
tdViability check fails → codeFAIL_CLOSED/code, no silent fallback (codeexecution_mode.py/code).

trtdState machines
tdA2A escrow
tdOnly whitelisted transitions
tdInvalid transition → refuse (codea2a_escrow.py/code).

/tbody
/table

!-- Secrets + headers --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Secrets + transport

## Secrets management and header posture.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Secrets never in code

div class="card-description"
All provider credentials live in env vars loaded from Google Secret Manager or Vercel env.
codegitleaks/code runs on every PR with full history (codesecret-scan.yml/code,
codefetch-depth: 0/code). A matched leak fails the run — no merge.
API keys are stored in Postgres as SHA-256 hashes, not plaintext.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Middleware stack (sardis-api)

div class="card-description"
Order from codepackages/api/src/sardis_server/main.py/code: RequestId →
StructuredLogging → SecurityHeaders (CSP/HSTS) → RequestBodyLimit (10MB default, 1MB
webhooks, 512KB checkout, 64KB policies) → RateLimit → TAP → x402 → UsageMetering →
ActivityLogger → CORS (no wildcards — codesettings.allowed_origins_list/code) →
routers. Every step is fail-closed.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Webhook signatures

div class="card-description"
Required in all non-dev environments. HMAC via codeX-Sardis-Signature/code and
codeX-Sardis-Timestamp/code. Delivery uses the transactional outbox pattern —
codewebhook_outbox/code (migration 032) is written in the same transaction as the
business operation and flushed by a background worker. codeevent_id/code keyed dedup.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Replay + idempotency

div class="card-description"
Redis + Postgres replay caches for mandates (Phase 3 durable state).
codeidempotency_key/code DB-level unique constraint on checkout sessions
(migration 031). codeSELECT … FOR UPDATE NOWAIT/code prevents double-pay race at
settlement.

!-- TDD remediation --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Hardening history

## TDD remediation — three phases, March 2026.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The security posture above is the result of a deliberate three-phase hardening done in
March 2026. Each phase was merged incrementally with tests. Design doc:
codedocs/plans/2026-03-09-tdd-remediation-90-day-design.md/code.

table class="data"
thead
trthPhase
thFocus
thWhat shipped

/thead
tbody
tr
tdPhase 1

tdSecurity + correctness

tdOAuth CSRF fix, admin fail-closed defaults, webhook signatures required in prod, gitleaks in CI, group-policy TTL + budget decrement, queue interface fix, AGIT default fail-closed.

tr
tdPhase 2

tdSingle execution path

tdcodePreExecutionPipeline/code built; AGIT/KYA/FIDES hooks; router migrations (wallets, mandates, ap2) onto codePaymentOrchestrator/code; DI lockdown (codechain_executor/code private); unified codePaymentResult/code.

tr
tdPhase 3

tdDurable state + moat

tdRedis dedup store, Postgres reconciliation queue, Redis replay cache, rate limiter enforcement, attestation envelope endpoint, README maturity tiers.

/tbody
/table

!-- Kill switch --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Incident response

## Kill switch.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
If we see a confirmed compromise, one privileged call freezes every wallet. The action is
itself audited — see migration code064_emergency_freeze.sql/code.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"What gets frozen

div class="card-description"
All MPC signing requests denied at the orchestrator boundary, in-flight batches cancelled
before broadcast, background reconciliation suspended. Chain-side funds aren't moved —
they simply stop being signable from Sardis. Non-custodial works in our favour here.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Audit record

div class="card-description"
codeemergency_freeze_events/code: codeaction/code (codefreeze_all/code /
codeunfreeze_all/code), codetriggered_by/code, codewallets_affected/code,
codereason/code, codenotes/code, codecreated_at/code. Indexed descending on
codecreated_at/code so the operator dashboard can always show the latest action first.

!-- Evidence --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Audit evidence

## Every decision, exportable.

table class="data"
thead
trthLayer
thTable / file
thEvidence value

/thead
tbody
trtdEvery allow/deny
tdcodepolicy_decisions/code
tdReason code + rule snapshot at decision time. Regulators can see why a transaction was allowed, not just that it was.

trtdEvery money movement
tdcodeledger_entries/code
tdAppend-only. Every row carries the pre-execution decision ID.

trtdOn-chain proof
tdcodeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code
tdMerkle-root batches anchored on Base. Once anchored, re-ordering is detectable.

trtdRegulator package
tdcodeevidence_export/code router
tdProduces a signed bundle: policy_decisions + ledger rows + Merkle proof + chain tx hash.

trtdIncident response
tdcodeemergency_freeze_events/code (migration 064)
tdEvery freeze/unfreeze with actor and reason.

trtdWebhook delivery
tdcodewebhook_outbox/code (migration 032)
tdProof of at-least-once delivery with attempts + timestamps.

/tbody
/table

!-- Cross-refs --

div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper
