=====================================
# PROTOCOL OBJECTS
https://canvas.sardis.sh/protocol-objects
=====================================

Canvas title="20 · Protocol Objects — Sardis Canvas" currentId="protocol-objects"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 20 · Protocol/span

## Protocol Objects — the 10 signed artifacts.

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis v1.0 defines 10 protocol objects that chain together from delegation through settlement,
dispute resolution, and fulfillment. Every object is cryptographically signed, immutable once issued,
and references its predecessors. Together they form a complete audit chain — no trust in Sardis required.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
A reusable funding credential — card, wallet, bank credential, or API key — is the wrong primitive for
the agent economy. The right primitive is the strongOne-Time Payment Object/strong: a signed,
short-lived, context-bound instrument backed by a UTXO-style funding commitment, tied to a verified
agent and principal, bounded by policy, and auditable with a complete cryptographic trail.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Object chain

## From delegation to fulfillment in 10 objects.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 1 · Agent Certificate
span class="obj-num"Identity/span

div class="obj-purpose"Identifies the agent and binds it to an issuer and a principal context. Without cryptographic agent identity, you don't have agent payments — you have bot payments. TAP-compatible so agents can prove identity to any Visa-network merchant.

div class="obj-fields"agent_id · issuer_id · principal_id · agent_public_key (Ed25519 or P-256 JWK)
attestation_type (self_signed | issuer_attested | network_verified)
trust_tier (0–3) · capabilities (browse, pay, subscribe, delegate) · expires_at · signature

div class="obj-why"strongTrust tiers:/strong 0 = unverified ($10 max tx, email only) · 1 = issuer-attested ($500, KYC-lite) · 2 = network-verified ($10K, full KYC + TAP) · 3 = enterprise-grade (policy-defined, full IAM + continuous monitoring).

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 2 · Spending Mandate
span class="obj-num"Authorization/span

div class="obj-purpose"The signed statement of allowed authority from the principal — a programmable, cryptographically signed power of attorney for spending. Heart of the system. Expressible as an AP2 Verifiable Digital Credential, extended with agent-native fields (in_flight_limit, fx_policy, UTXO cell strategy).

div class="obj-fields"mandate_id · principal_id · agent_id · merchant_scope · category_scope
max_amount_per_tx · max_amount_per_period · period_type (daily | weekly | monthly)
currency_scope · rail_scope · approval_threshold · retry_limit
strongin_flight_limit/strong · fx_policy · valid_from · expires_at · policy_hash
revocation_handle · nonce · signature (Ed25519/P-256)

div class="obj-why"strongSecurity — in_flight_limit:/strong prevents the T4 "shadow lock" attack where a malfunctioning agent freezes all funds by requesting objects without presenting them. Agent must settle or cancel before minting new objects beyond the limit.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 3 · Funding Commitment
span class="obj-num"Reserve/span

div class="obj-purpose"Reserve-backed container bound to a specific mandate. Guarantees funds are available before any payment object is minted. In v0 this was a monolithic lock against a vault balance — v1 replaces it with a UTXO model to allow concurrent spending without race conditions.

div class="obj-fields"commitment_id · mandate_id
funding_source_type (custodial_fiat | smart_wallet | card | bank | stablecoin | credit_line)
funding_source_ref · total_committed · cell_strategy (pre_split | on_demand | hybrid)
default_cell_denomination · commitment_expiry · settlement_preferences
fallback_options · strongrefund_destination/strong · issuer_signature

div class="obj-why"strongSecurity — refund_destination:/strong must always point to a permanent Principal Master Treasury, never a temp vault or cell. Prevents the T6 "refund-to-closed-vault" failure where disputes resolve months after the original transaction and the funding container is gone.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 4 · FundingCell (UTXO)
span class="obj-num"Concurrency/span

div class="obj-purpose"A discrete, atomic unit of spendable value within a Funding Commitment. Inspired by Bitcoin's UTXO model: each cell has exactly one owner at any time; concurrent payments claim different cells; double-spending is structurally impossible without distributed consensus.

div class="obj-fields"cell_id (primary key for locking) · commitment_id · mandate_id
denomination (fixed) · state (AVAILABLE | CLAIMED | SPENT | RETURNED | EXPIRED | SPLIT | MERGED)
claimed_by · claimed_at · payment_object_id · parent_cell_id · vault_ref
created_at · expires_at · nonce · issuer_signature

div class="obj-why"strongWhy it beats a global counter:/strong no distributed locking. PostgreSQL codeFOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED/code ensures concurrent transactions never block each other. Cells split on-demand (an agent needing $7 from a $10 cell produces $7 CLAIMED + $3 AVAILABLE), merge in background to prevent fragmentation. Horizontally scalable.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 5 · One-Time Payment Object
span class="obj-num"Execution/span

div class="obj-purpose"The single-use executable payment artifact — what the agent presents to the merchant. Derived from the mandate + funding commitment cell(s). Every payment creates a fresh object; no credential is ever reused. This is the core innovation of the protocol.

div class="obj-fields"payment_object_id · mandate_id · commitment_id · cell_ids (UTXO inputs)
merchant_id · strongsession_hash/strong (sha256: merchant_id | cart_id | timestamp)
exact_amount · source_currency · target_currency · nonce · one_time_use (true)
expires_at (minutes–hours) · privacy_mode (transparent | hybrid | full_zk)
zk_proof (if non-transparent) · escrow_required · escrow_profile · dispute_profile
signature_chain [principal_sig, issuer_sig, agent_sig]

div class="obj-why"strongSecurity — session_hash:/strong binds payment to a specific merchant session/cart. Prevents the T3 relay/MITM attack where an intermediary intercepts a valid payment and replays it in a different shopping context.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 6 · Settlement Receipt
span class="obj-num"Proof/span

div class="obj-purpose"Canonical proof of what actually happened. Closes the audit chain. v1 adds fulfillment_status to track whether the agent acknowledged receipt of the purchased service/good, plus FX fields for cross-currency transactions.

div class="obj-fields"receipt_id · payment_object_id · settlement_rail · settlement_tx_ref
settled_amount · fees · fx_quote_id · actual_fx_rate · fx_fee
timestamp · status (settled | partial_settled | failed | reversed)
strongfulfillment_status/strong (pending_ack | acknowledged | dispute_auto_triggered)
merchant_signature · network_signature

div class="obj-why"strongfulfillment_status (new in v1):/strong if not acknowledged within escrow timeout, dispute_auto_triggered fires automatically. Closes the T11 "paid but not delivered" gap — settlement succeeds but merchant fails to provide the service.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 7 · EscrowHold
span class="obj-num"Trust/span

div class="obj-purpose"For transactions above the escrow threshold, funds land in escrow before the merchant receives them. Merchant sees cryptographic proof of locked funds and delivers the service; agent confirms receipt (or timelock expires); then escrow releases. If dispute: arbitration decides.

div class="obj-fields"escrow_id · payment_object_id · held_amount
held_at (issuer_vault | smart_contract) · contract_address · beneficiary
release_condition (AGENT_CONFIRM | TIMELOCK | ORACLE) · timeout_duration · auto_release_at
state (HELD | CONFIRMING | AUTO_RELEASING | RELEASED | DISPUTING | ARBITRATING | RESOLVED_*)
dispute_window · evidence_deadline · arbitration_endpoint · issuer_signature

div class="obj-why"strongDefault timeouts by transaction type:/strong API/SaaS = 1h escrow, 24h dispute, 3-day evidence · Software license = 24h / 7d / 5d · Physical goods = 14d / 30d / 7d · B2B services = 30d / 60d / 14d. Timeouts match delivery verification complexity.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 8 · FXQuote
span class="obj-num"Cross-currency/span

div class="obj-purpose"Records the cross-currency conversion applied during settlement. Linked from the Settlement Receipt. Time-bounded quote with explicit slippage tracking.

div class="obj-fields"fx_quote_id · source_currency · target_currency
quoted_rate · quoted_at · quote_valid_until (default 30s)
source_amount · target_amount · fx_fee · total_source_debit
slippage_actual_bps · provider · provider_tx_ref

div class="obj-why"Matched against the mandate's codefx_policy/code at verification time. If codeslippage_actual_bps/code exceeds codemax_slippage_bps/code or the provider isn't in codepreferred_fx_providers/code, the payment fails before settlement.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 9 · Subscription
span class="obj-num"Recurring/span

div class="obj-purpose"Recurring charge intents + billing cycles + dunning rules. Not a single mandate but a parent that emits child one-time payment objects on schedule. Covers SaaS subscriptions, usage-based billing, and agent-mediated BNPL (credit-backed mandates).

div class="obj-fields"subscription_id · parent_mandate_id · merchant_id
charge_intent · billing_cycle (ISO 8601 duration) · dunning_policy
amendment_protocol (price_creep_protection)
grace_period · usage_counter · countersignature (for usage-based)

div class="obj-why"strongPrice-creep protection:/strong if the merchant issues an amendment that exceeds codemandate.amendment_tolerance_bps/code, the subscription pauses pending principal re-signature. Prevents silent subscription inflation that consumer credit cards suffer from.

div class="obj-card"
div class="obj-header"

## 10 · Mandate Tree
span class="obj-num"Delegation/span

div class="obj-purpose"Hierarchical delegation. CFO creates a master mandate ($500K/quarter); VP Engineering creates a child mandate ($100K/quarter) under it; team lead creates a grandchild ($20K/month). Each level further constrains — never expands — the parent's bounds. Maps to how enterprise budgets actually work.

div class="obj-fields"tree_root_mandate_id · parent_mandate_id · depth · path
inherited_constraints (merchant_scope ∩, category_scope ∩, limits ≤ parent)
delegation_signature (parent signs child) · revocation_cascade

div class="obj-why"strongEnforcement rule:/strong child mandate constraints MUST be a strict subset of parent. Issuer rejects creation if any dimension expands. Revocation cascades down the tree — revoking a parent invalidates all descendants.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Chain

## How they reference each other.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Each object is signed and references its predecessors, forming a chain that any party can verify
independently. Canvas a href="/state-machine"21 · 22-State Machine/a shows how these objects
transition through states over time.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart LR
AC[Agent Certificate] -- SM[Spending Mandate]
SM -- FC[Funding Commitment]
FC -- FCell[FundingCell UTXO]
SM -- MT[Mandate Tree children]
SM -. recurring .- SUB[Subscription]
FCell -- POO[One-Time Payment Object]
SM -- POO
POO -- EH[EscrowHold optional]
POO -- SR[Settlement Receipt]
SR -- FXQ[FXQuote if cross-currency]
EH -- SR

[/diagram]

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Cross-refs

## Related canvases.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/state-machine"
div class="canvas-card-num"21 · Protocol

div class="canvas-card-title"22-State Machine

div class="canvas-card-desc"The lifecycle every Payment Object follows — issue through settle, escrow, dispute.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/policy-engine"
div class="canvas-card-num"08 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Policy Engine

div class="canvas-card-desc"The 12-check enforcement pipeline that evaluates every Spending Mandate.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/dual-rail"
div class="canvas-card-num"22 · Protocol

div class="canvas-card-title"Dual-Rail Architecture

div class="canvas-card-desc"Where these objects actually settle — internal ledger vs external rails.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/ecosystem-gap"
div class="canvas-card-num"23 · Protocol

div class="canvas-card-title"Ecosystem Gap Map

div class="canvas-card-desc"Why AP2/TAP/x402 need these objects — no existing standard covers the lifecycle.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# STATE MACHINE
https://canvas.sardis.sh/state-machine
=====================================

Canvas title="21 · 22-State Machine — Sardis Canvas" currentId="state-machine"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 21 · Protocol/span

## 22-State Machine — one Payment Object's full life.

p class="hero-lead"
Every Payment Object passes through a deterministic state machine covering the happy path, escrow hold,
partial settlement, dispute arbitration, FX routing, and fulfillment acknowledgment. 22 states, each with
strict allowed transitions and terminal conditions. This is the most comprehensive payment lifecycle
spec designed for agent commerce.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Card networks have one happy path and a dispute process bolted on. Sardis treats every possible
outcome — direct settle, escrow hold, timelock release, dispute file, arbitration decision, partial
settlement, refund, cancel, failure — as a first-class state with explicit transitions. An agent
payment never ends up in an undefined condition.

!-- Happy Path --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Happy path · 7 states

## Direct settlement — no escrow.

div class="row row-wrap" style="gap:8px; margin-bottom:20px;"
span class="state-pill sp-active"ISSUED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-active"PRESENTED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-active"VERIFIED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-happy"LOCKED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-happy"SETTLING/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-happy"SETTLED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-happy"FULFILLED/span

table class="data"
theadtrthState
thMeaning
thAllowed next

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeISSUED/code
tdPayment Object minted by Issuer; cell(s) CLAIMED
tdPRESENTED, REVOKED, EXPIRED

trtdcodePRESENTED/code
tdAgent presented object to merchant
tdVERIFIED, FAILED, EXPIRED

trtdcodeVERIFIED/code
tdMerchant verified claims (cleartext / hybrid / ZKP)
tdLOCKED, ESCROWED, FAILED

trtdcodeLOCKED/code
tdFunds locked — direct settlement (below escrow threshold)
tdSETTLING, CANCELLED

trtdcodeSETTLING/code
tdSettlement executing on chosen rail (may include FX)
tdSETTLED, PARTIAL_SETTLED, FAILED

trtdcodeSETTLED/code
tdFull settlement complete
tdFULFILLED, DISPUTED, REFUNDED

trtdcodeFULFILLED/code
tdAgent acknowledged receipt of service/good (terminal)
td— terminal —

/tbody
/table

!-- Escrow Path --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Escrow path · +5 states

## When delivery verification matters.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Transactions above the escrow threshold route through an explicit hold. Merchant sees cryptographic
proof of locked funds; agent confirms delivery (or timelock auto-releases); dispute routes to
arbitration with one of three outcomes.

div class="row row-wrap" style="gap:8px; margin-bottom:20px;"
span class="state-pill sp-active"VERIFIED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-escrow"ESCROWED/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-escrow"CONFIRMING/span
span class="text-faint"│ or/span
span class="state-pill sp-escrow"AUTO_RELEASING/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-happy"RELEASED/span
span class="text-faint"→ SETTLING → SETTLED → FULFILLED/span

table class="data"
theadtrthState
thMeaning
thAllowed next

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeESCROWED/code
tdFunds in escrow hold (above threshold)
tdCONFIRMING, AUTO_RELEASING, DISPUTING

trtdcodeCONFIRMING/code
tdAgent reviewing delivery from merchant
tdRELEASED, DISPUTING

trtdcodeAUTO_RELEASING/code
tdTimelock expired without dispute; auto-releasing to merchant
tdRELEASED

trtdcodeRELEASED/code
tdEscrow released to merchant
tdSETTLING, DISPUTING (within dispute_window)

trtdcodePARTIAL_SETTLED/code
tdPartial amount settled (less than locked)
tdUNLOCKING, DISPUTED

/tbody
/table
div class="callout callout-warn mt-16"
span class="callout-title"Default escrow timeouts/span
API/SaaS digital delivery = 1h escrow / 24h dispute / 3-day evidence · Software license = 24h / 7d / 5d
· Physical goods = 14d / 30d / 7d · B2B services = 30d / 60d / 14d.

!-- Dispute Path --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Dispute path · 5 states

## Three deterministic outcomes.

div class="row row-wrap" style="gap:8px; margin-bottom:20px;"
span class="state-pill sp-dispute"DISPUTING/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-dispute"ARBITRATING/span
span class="text-faint"→/span
span class="state-pill sp-term"RESOLVED_REFUND/span
span class="text-faint"│/span
span class="state-pill sp-term"RESOLVED_RELEASE/span
span class="text-faint"│/span
span class="state-pill sp-term"RESOLVED_SPLIT/span

table class="data"
theadtrthState
thMeaning
thAllowed next

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeDISPUTING/code
tdDispute filed, evidence collection phase
tdARBITRATING

trtdcodeARBITRATING/code
tdArbitration node reviewing submitted evidence
tdRESOLVED_REFUND, RESOLVED_RELEASE, RESOLVED_SPLIT

trtdcodeRESOLVED_REFUND/code
tdFull refund to principal (dispute upheld)
td— terminal —

trtdcodeRESOLVED_RELEASE/code
tdFull release to merchant (dispute rejected)
td— terminal —

trtdcodeRESOLVED_SPLIT/code
tdPartial refund + partial release (compromise)
td— terminal —

/tbody
/table

!-- Terminal States --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Terminal states · 10

## Every object lands somewhere explicit.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Success outcomes

div class="card-description"
span class="state-pill sp-happy"FULFILLED/span — agent acknowledged service delivery.

span class="state-pill sp-term"RESOLVED_RELEASE/span — arbitration ruled for merchant.

span class="state-pill sp-term"UNLOCKED/span — residual FundingCells returned to AVAILABLE.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Failure outcomes

div class="card-description"
span class="state-pill sp-term"REVOKED/span — pre-use revocation by principal or issuer.

span class="state-pill sp-term"EXPIRED/span — expiry time reached before use.

span class="state-pill sp-term"CANCELLED/span — pre-settlement cancellation → UNLOCKING.

span class="state-pill sp-term"FAILED/span — processing failure (may retry within mandate.retry_limit).

span class="state-pill sp-term"REFUNDED/span — post-settlement refund issued.

span class="state-pill sp-term"RESOLVED_REFUND/span — arbitration ruled for principal.

span class="state-pill sp-term"RESOLVED_SPLIT/span — arbitration ruled partial.

!-- Full diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"All transitions

## The full graph.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

stateDiagram-v2
[*] -- ISSUED
ISSUED -- PRESENTED
ISSUED -- REVOKED
ISSUED -- EXPIRED
PRESENTED -- VERIFIED
PRESENTED -- FAILED
PRESENTED -- EXPIRED
VERIFIED -- LOCKED: below threshold
VERIFIED -- ESCROWED: above threshold
VERIFIED -- FAILED
LOCKED -- SETTLING
LOCKED -- CANCELLED
ESCROWED -- CONFIRMING
ESCROWED -- AUTO_RELEASING
ESCROWED -- DISPUTING
CONFIRMING -- RELEASED
CONFIRMING -- DISPUTING
AUTO_RELEASING -- RELEASED
RELEASED -- SETTLING
RELEASED -- DISPUTING
SETTLING -- SETTLED
SETTLING -- PARTIAL_SETTLED
SETTLING -- FAILED
SETTLED -- FULFILLED
SETTLED -- DISPUTED: disputed
SETTLED -- REFUNDED
PARTIAL_SETTLED -- UNLOCKING
PARTIAL_SETTLED -- DISPUTING
DISPUTING -- ARBITRATING
ARBITRATING -- RESOLVED_REFUND
ARBITRATING -- RESOLVED_RELEASE
ARBITRATING -- RESOLVED_SPLIT
CANCELLED -- UNLOCKING
UNLOCKING -- UNLOCKED
FULFILLED -- [*]
RESOLVED_REFUND -- [*]
RESOLVED_RELEASE -- [*]
RESOLVED_SPLIT -- [*]
REVOKED -- [*]
EXPIRED -- [*]
UNLOCKED -- [*]
FAILED -- [*]
REFUNDED -- [*]

[/diagram]

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Why this matters

## The "undefined ending" problem.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Card networks

div class="card-description"
Happy path + dispute chargeback, bolted on. No state for "settled but not delivered." No state
for "partial settlement." Merchant and cardholder reconcile via support tickets; evidence is
assembled ad hoc; outcomes depend on issuer discretion.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Sardis

div class="card-description"
Every possible outcome is an explicit state with cryptographic evidence at each transition.
An agent running thousands of payments per hour needs deterministic end-states to trigger the
right downstream workflow — retry, escalate, refund, reconcile — without human judgment in the loop.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Cross-refs

## Related canvases.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/protocol-objects"
div class="canvas-card-num"20 · Protocol

div class="canvas-card-title"Protocol Objects (10)

div class="canvas-card-desc"The objects that travel through these states.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/payment-flow"
div class="canvas-card-num"07 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Payment Flow

div class="canvas-card-desc"Happy-path sequence expanded to real code references.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# DUAL RAIL
https://canvas.sardis.sh/dual-rail
=====================================

Canvas title="22 · Dual-Rail Architecture — Sardis Canvas" currentId="dual-rail"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 22 · Protocol/span

## Dual-Rail Architecture — internal ledger first.

p class="hero-lead"
Sardis v1.1 inverts the usual fintech stack: the stronginternal ledger is the primary rail/strong,
and external rails (ACH, card, stablecoin, wire, FX bridge) become on-ramp and off-ramp only. When both
parties are on Sardis, a payment completes in ~1ms with $0 fee and zero external API calls. When the
merchant is off-Sardis, we fall back to the cheapest external rail that matches the mandate's
coderail_scope/code. This is the PayPal model applied to agent commerce.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Rails are a settlement surface, not a settlement strategy. Most agent payments in a mature ecosystem
will be Sardis-to-Sardis — the same way most PayPal dollars never leave PayPal. External rails stay
as ramps; internal ledger carries the volume.

!-- Two rails --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The two rails

## Primary vs. fallback.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="rail-box"
div class="rail-meta"Primary rail

div class="rail-title"Sardis internal ledger

div class="rail-body"
An atomic, double-entry ledger inside Sardis. Payer's FundingCells (UTXO) debit in one row;
merchant's Sardis balance credits in another. Both writes happen in a single PostgreSQL
transaction with codeFOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED/code on the cell row.
ul style="padding-left:18px; margin:10px 0 0 0;"

- strong~1ms/strong end-to-end (database commit latency)

- strong$0/strong marginal fee (no external API calls)

- strongFull audit/strong — every ledger row hash-chained, Merkle-batched, on-chain anchored

- strongNo chain reorg risk/strong — database is final

- Reversible via arbitration up to codedispute_window/code expiry

/ul

div class="rail-box"
div class="rail-meta"Fallback rails

div class="rail-title"External settlement (when merchant is off-Sardis)

div class="rail-body"
The liquidity router selects the best rail based on: merchant's preferred settlement currency,
mandate's coderail_scope/code, FX slippage budget, real-time rail health.
ul style="padding-left:18px; margin:10px 0 0 0;"

- strongx402/strong — stablecoin, HTTP-native

- strongCard (Stripe Connect, Adyen)/strong — existing merchant account

- strongBank (ACH / RTP / SEPA / PIX / Wire)/strong — direct deposit

- strongFX bridge (Bridge.xyz, Wise)/strong — cross-currency

- strongMPP (Stripe Merchant Payment Protocol)/strong — Tempo settlement

/ul

!-- On-ramps --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"On-ramp

## How money enters Sardis.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Principals fund their vaults via any rail that makes sense for their treasury. Once on Sardis,
those funds become FundingCells and circulate between agents and merchants entirely inside the ledger.

div class="grid grid-3"
div class="card"div class="card-title"Bank transfer
div class="card-description"ACH pull, wire, SEPA, Faster Payments. Settles to principal's custodial vault in 1–3 business days (varies by rail).

div class="card"div class="card-title"Card funding
div class="card-description"Debit card load for quick top-up. Standard card processing fees apply. Covers agents that need liquidity inside minutes.

div class="card"div class="card-title"USDC / stablecoin
div class="card-description"On-chain deposit to a Sardis-controlled smart wallet. Near-instant, low fee. Most common for crypto-native principals.

div class="card"div class="card-title"Credit line
div class="card-description"A credit-backed mandate draws against a pre-approved revolving line; repayment schedules as a subscription mandate back to the principal's treasury.

div class="card"div class="card-title"Stablecoin bridges
div class="card-description"USDC.e and other variants bridge in via Circle CCTP or LayerZero; normalized to canonical USDC inside Sardis.

div class="card"div class="card-title"Smart wallet
div class="card-description"An ERC-4337 smart account pre-authorizes Sardis as a spender; agent payments draw down from there without per-tx signatures.

!-- Off-ramps --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Off-ramp

## How money leaves Sardis.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Only when the merchant is off-Sardis. The liquidity router picks the cheapest rail that matches the
merchant's preferred payout currency and the mandate's coderail_scope/code.

table class="data"
theadtrthRail
thTypical use
thLatency
thFee pattern

/thead
tbody
trtdx402 (stablecoin)
tdAPI monetization, agent-to-agent micro-pay
tdseconds
td~$0.005 per call

trtdStripe Connect (card)
tdPay with Sardis merchants, legacy e-commerce
td~3 seconds auth, T+2 payout
td2.9% + $0.30

trtdACH (US)
tdLarge US merchant payouts, vendor settlement
td1–3 business days (Same-day ACH 4 hrs)
td$0.25–$1

trtdSEPA (EU) / Faster Payments (UK)
tdEU/UK merchant payouts
tdseconds–hours
td€0.10–€0.50

trtdRTP (US real-time)
tdCritical payouts, gig economy settlement
tdseconds
td~$0.50

trtdPIX (Brazil), UPI (India)
tdLATAM / APAC merchant reach
tdseconds
tdfree–$0.10

trtdWire (domestic + SWIFT)
tdLarge B2B, enterprise reconciliation
tdhours–1 day
td$15–$40

trtdFX bridge (Bridge.xyz)
tdCross-currency settlement
tdseconds
td~25 bps

/tbody
/table

!-- Why --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Why this architecture

## The PayPal parallel.

div class="callout-box"
PayPal processes roughly strong95% of its dollar-flow inside its own ledger./strong Users top up
once, receive payments from other PayPal users, then off-ramp occasionally. The external rails (bank,
card) are funnels, not settlement. PayPal's moat isn't the rails — it's the closed loop.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Why it matters for agent commerce

div class="card-description"
Agents transact at sub-cent granularity. Running every micro-payment through card rails (2.9%+$0.30)
or ACH (min $0.25) is economically dead. The only way agent commerce scales is if ~90% of volume
stays on an internal ledger where marginal cost is a database write. Sardis is built around that
reality from day one.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Why external rails still matter

div class="card-description"
Merchants have suppliers, payroll, landlords — obligations that live in fiat. The off-ramp has to
work every day on time. Sardis doesn't pretend the dollar doesn't exist; it just doesn't route
agent-to-agent dollars through the ACH network.

!-- Diagram --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The loop

## Full diagram.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart TB
subgraph Onramp["On-ramp · external → Sardis"]
BT[Bank transfer]
CD[Card]
UC[USDC]
SC[Stablecoin]
CR[Credit line]
end
subgraph Internal["Sardis internal ledger · primary rail"]
PV["Payer vault
FundingCells (UTXO)"]
MB["Merchant account
Sardis balance"]
PC["Protocol core
+ escrow"]
ZK["ZKP +
recurring"]
ID["Identity +
trust"]
PV --|"atomic ledger transfer
~1ms, $0 fee"| MB
end
subgraph Decision
D{Merchant
off-Sardis?}
end
subgraph Offramp["Off-ramp · Sardis → external (fallback only)"]
X402[x402]
CS[Card · Stripe]
BA["Bank · ACH/RTP"]
FX[FX bridge]
WR[Wire]
end

BT -- PV
CD -- PV
UC -- PV
SC -- PV
CR -- PV
MB -- D
D --|No| MB
D --|Yes fallback| X402
D --|Yes fallback| CS
D --|Yes fallback| BA
D --|Yes fallback| FX
D --|Yes fallback| WR

[/diagram]

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Cross-refs

## Related canvases.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/settlement"
div class="canvas-card-num"10 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Settlement & Chains

div class="canvas-card-desc"Multi-chain executor, CCTP, paymaster, fiat rails — the external side.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/protocol-objects"
div class="canvas-card-num"20 · Protocol

div class="canvas-card-title"Protocol Objects

div class="canvas-card-desc"FundingCell, Settlement Receipt, FXQuote — what flows through these rails.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# CREDIT
https://canvas.sardis.sh/credit
=====================================

Canvas title="23 · Agent Credit — Sardis Canvas" currentId="credit"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 23 · Product/span

## Agent Credit — facility access without Sardis becoming the lender.

p class="hero-lead"
Facility Gate is Sardis's credit control plane for agents. It lets an agent request temporary
purchase authority against a partner-backed facility, but keeps Sardis out of custody, issuing,
balance-sheet lending, and collections. Sardis decides whether authority exists; the sponsor or
partner remains the economic actor.

div class="row row-wrap mt-24" style="gap:12px;"
span class="badge badge-info"New/span
span class="badge"Facility Gate/span
span class="badge"Non-custodial control plane/span
span class="badge"Simulator-first/span
span class="badge badge-muted"No live card issuance by default/span

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Sardis does not hand an agent a general-purpose credit card. The agent submits a facility request;
Sardis loads persisted mandate, facility, policy, risk, evidence, revocation, and liability state;
then appends an immutable decision packet. Execution is gated through a simulator today and a
provider adapter later. Authorization and money movement stay separated.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Boundary

## The product is a credit authority firewall.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
The architecture deliberately avoids the tempting but dangerous framing: "credit card for agents."
A card is only one possible execution rail. The durable primitive is delegated facility authority.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"What Sardis controls

div class="card-description"
Identity binding, mandate scope, policy evaluation, risk scoring, evidence capture, human step-up,
authorization, revocation, exception handling, and audit reconstruction. These are control-plane
functions, not custody functions.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"What Sardis does not become

div class="card-description"
Sardis is not the lender, issuer of record, stored-value holder, collections system, or repayment
obligor. Liquidity and settlement belong to the sponsor, customer-controlled account, or partner
facility provider.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Control plane

## How a facility request actually moves.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

flowchart TB
A["Agent
request"] -- FG["Sardis Facility Gate"]
FG -- M["Persisted mandate
+ facility versions"]
FG -- P["Policy, risk,
evidence, revocation"]
P -- D{"Decision"}

D --|"approved"| L1["Append approved
decision packet"]
L1 -- X["Execution adapter"]
X -- S["Simulator now"]
X -- R["Partner rail later"]

D --|"step-up"| L2["Append step-up
decision"]
L2 -- O["Approval queue"]
O -- P2["Recheck revocation
+ facility state"]
P2 -- L3["Append final
decision"]

D --|"denied"| L4["Append denial
with reason codes"]
L1 -- AU["Audit link"]
L3 -- AU
L4 -- AU
AU -- A

[/diagram]

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Non-custodial line

## Where custody stays out of Sardis.

div class="boundary"
div class="boundary-item"
div class="boundary-label"Agent

div class="boundary-title"Delegated actor

div class="boundary-body"Can request authority and attach evidence. Cannot create a facility, expand a limit, or become the repayment obligor.

div class="boundary-item"
div class="boundary-label"Sponsor

div class="boundary-title"Economic principal

div class="boundary-body"Owns the mandate and liability. The audit packet names the repayment obligor and loss owner explicitly.

div class="boundary-item"
div class="boundary-label"Sardis

div class="boundary-title"Authority control plane

div class="boundary-body"Evaluates, gates, records, revokes, and reconstructs decisions. It does not hold funds or issue credit.

div class="boundary-item"
div class="boundary-label"Facility

div class="boundary-title"Partner-backed liquidity

div class="boundary-body"Represents an external or sponsor-backed line. Sardis stores state and version refs, not the balance-sheet obligation.

div class="boundary-item"
div class="boundary-label"Adapter

div class="boundary-title"Execution gate

div class="boundary-body"Simulator today; real provider later. No credential is created unless an authorization event already exists.

div class="boundary-item"
div class="boundary-label"Partner

div class="boundary-title"Rail / issuing / settlement

div class="boundary-body"Moves money or provides credit under its own contract. Webhooks mirror external state back into append-only events.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Decision packet

## Every approval is reconstructable.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:760px;"
The audit surface is not a screenshot of current state. It is a versioned packet that says exactly
what Sardis knew and which authority it relied on when the decision happened.

div class="packet"
div class="packet-row"
div
div class="packet-title"Mandate version

div class="packet-desc"Explicit facility draw authority, agent binding, merchant/category scope, draw caps, approval threshold.

div class="packet-row"
div
div class="packet-title"Facility version

div class="packet-desc"Provider, sponsor, status, limit payload, allowed categories, blocked merchants, repayment mirror if present.

div class="packet-row"
div
div class="packet-title"Policy and risk versions

div class="packet-desc"Deterministic policy snapshot, risk model version, feature snapshot, reason codes. No hidden model magic.

div class="packet-row"
div
div class="packet-title"Evidence considered

div class="packet-desc"Only evidence attached before decision time is considered. Later evidence requires a new decision path.

div class="packet-row"
div
div class="packet-title"Liability assignment

div class="packet-desc"Repayment obligor, sponsor, loss owner, and facility provider are first-class fields, not buried in metadata.

div class="packet-row"
div
div class="packet-title"Event hash chain

div class="packet-desc"Requests, approvals, executions, exceptions, revocations, webhooks, and exports sit on append-only events.

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Pilot shape

## What is live in the repo now.

table class="data"
thead
trthSurface
thStatus
thWhy it matters

/thead
tbody
trtdFeature gate
tdspan class="badge badge-success"pilot-ready/span
tdGlobal enablement plus codeSARDIS_FACILITY_GATE_ORG_ALLOWLIST/code prevents broad accidental exposure.

trtdStrict authority
tdspan class="badge badge-success"pilot-ready/span
tdAuthorization loads persisted mandate, facility, and policy records instead of trusting request snapshots.

trtdSimulator adapter
tdspan class="badge badge-success"default/span
tdExercises execution semantics without live provider money movement.

trtdReal provider
tdspan class="badge badge-muted"disabled/span
tdProvider adapter work stays behind contract tests, sandbox credentials, and capability acceptance.

trtdPilot readiness
tdspan class="badge badge-success"scripted/span
tdcodefacility_gate_pilot_readiness.py/code checks flags, allowlist, alert artifacts, and tabletop evidence.

trtdLive card issuance
tdspan class="badge badge-warning"not yet/span
tdIntentionally out of scope until revocation, audit, adapter, webhook, and partner semantics are accepted.

/tbody
/table

section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Related canvases

## Read next.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/policy-engine"
div class="canvas-card-num"08 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Policy Engine

div class="canvas-card-desc"The deterministic enforcement layer Facility Gate composes with.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/dual-rail"
div class="canvas-card-num"22 · Protocol

div class="canvas-card-title"Dual-Rail Architecture

div class="canvas-card-desc"Where facility-backed authority fits into the broader rail strategy.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# SARDIS CONNECT
https://canvas.sardis.sh/sardis-connect
=====================================

Canvas title="20 · Sardis Connect — Sardis Canvas" currentId="sardis-connect"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 20 · Extended/span

## Sardis Connect

p class="hero-lead"
Make any API agent-ready in three lines of code. The merchant ships USD,
the agent pays in stablecoins, Sardis handles every crypto primitive in between.
This canvas explains what Connect is, how it differs from Pay with Sardis, and how
the full onboarding flow works end-to-end.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
strongPay with Sardis/strong is a customer-facing checkout (buyer clicks "Pay with Sardis" on a merchant site).
strongSardis Connect/strong is the merchant-facing inverse — a drop-in SDK that turns any HTTP API into an
agent-payable service: discovery, payment, verification, and USD settlement. Merchant integrates in
code5 minutes/code, never touches a wallet, receives USD in their connected bank account.

!-- Who uses it --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Audience

## Who actually ships Sardis Connect.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Connect is a B2B2Agent wedge. The merchant is any company that already runs an HTTP API and wants
AI agents as a new revenue channel — without learning what a gas fee is.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"AI API providers

div class="card-description"
Text-generation, embeddings, analysis, transcription, image, speech APIs. Today they gatekeep with
API keys + human-signed Stripe subscriptions. With Connect, every endpoint gets a price and an agent
can pay per call — no signup, no CAPTCHA, no credit card form.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Data and scraping APIs

div class="card-description"
The long tail of priced HTTP APIs (proxies, weather, FX, company data). The per-request monetization
loop is the native Connect shape: agent discovers via code/.well-known/sardis.json/code, pays
$0.001 per call, uses it.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"SaaS platforms exposing agent features

div class="card-description"
A traditional SaaS (CRM, analytics, ticketing) adds Connect to bill agents for programmatic access.
Human users stay on the existing subscription plan; agents get a per-call plan without any pricing-page
gymnastics.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Services in the OSP ecosystem

div class="card-description"
A provider implementing Open Service Protocol (OSP) can declare codesardis_wallet/code in
codeaccepted_payment_methods/code. Connect is the drop-in library that makes that declaration
real — payment, verification, settlement. See Canvas 21 · OSP Integration.

!-- Integration flow --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The Flow

## Merchant onboarding, end to end.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Four moving parts: the merchant app, the Connect SDK, the Sardis API (merchant checkout),
and the merchant's connected bank rail. The merchant touches three of the four; Sardis orchestrates the fourth.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant M as Merchant app (FastAPI)
participant SC as sardis-connect SDK
participant AG as AI Agent
participant SA as Sardis API
participant ST as Bank rail

Note over M,SC: 1. One-time setup
M-SA: POST /merchants/register
SA--M: merchant_id + mch_live_xxx key
M-ST: Express onboarding (hosted)
ST--M: acct_xxx, charges_enabled=true
M-SC: SardisConnect(api_key, merchant_id)
M-SC: sardis.price("/api/generate", "0.05")
M-M: app.include_router(sardis.router)

Note over AG,M: 2. Agent runtime
AG-M: GET /.well-known/sardis.json
M--AG: manifest {endpoints, prices, accepts}
AG-M: POST /sardis/pay {endpoint, mandate_id}
M-SA: POST /merchant-checkout/sessions
SA--M: session_id + client_secret
M--AG: PaySession
AG-SA: pay(session_id) via SDK + mandate
SA-SA: policy + KYC + mandate checks
SA-SA: USDC settlement on Base/Tempo

Note over SA,ST: 3. Settlement
SA-ST: Connect Transfer (USD)
ST--M: auto-payout to bank
AG-M: POST /api/generate (X-Sardis-Session)
M-SC: paywall verifies session
SC-SA: GET /sessions/{id}
SA--SC: status=paid
M--AG: 200 OK (API response)

[/diagram]

!-- Python quickstart --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Python SDK

## Three lines in FastAPI.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Straight from codepackages/sardis-connect/README.md/code. The router exposes three endpoints:
agent discovery, payment session creation, and payment verification.

div class="code-block"
precodefrom fastapi import FastAPI
from sardis_connect import SardisConnect

app = FastAPI()

# 1. Initialize with your merchant credentials
sardis = SardisConnect(
api_key="mch_live_xxx",
merchant_id="merch_xxx",
service_name="My AI API",
)

# 2. Define priced endpoints (or sardis.meter() for per-unit)
sardis.price("/api/generate", amount="0.05", description="Generate text")
sardis.price("/api/analyze", amount="0.10", description="Analyze data")

# 3. Mount the router
app.include_router(sardis.router)
/code

table class="data mt-16"
thead
trthEndpoint
thMethod
thPurpose

/thead
tbody
trtdcode/.well-known/sardis.json/code
tdGET
tdAgent discovery manifest — name, accepted rails, endpoints, prices.

trtdcode/sardis/pay/code
tdPOST
tdCreate a codemerchant-checkout/code session; returns codesession_id/code + codeclient_secret/code.

trtdcode/sardis/verify/code
tdPOST
tdLook up session status; returns code{ verified, amount, payer_id }/code.

trtdcode/sardis/webhooks/code
tdPOST
tdSigned (HMAC-SHA256) webhook receiver for payment events.

trtdcode/sardis/usage/code
tdPOST
tdReport metered usage (tokens/units) for per-unit pricing — see codeSardisConnect.meter()/code.

/tbody
/table
p class="text-muted mt-16" style="max-width:720px;"
Protect any endpoint with the codeSardisPaywall/code FastAPI dependency — the client passes
codeX-Sardis-Session: mcs_…/code and the dependency verifies status against the Sardis API before the
handler runs. Source: codepackages/sardis-connect/src/sardis_connect/middleware.py/code.

!-- JS quickstart --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Node SDK

## Express / Node.js, same three lines.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Published as code@sardis/connect/code. Source in
codepackages/sardis-connect-js/src/middleware.ts/code.

div class="code-block"
precodeimport express from "express";
import { sardisConnect } from "@sardis/connect";

const app = express();

const sardis = sardisConnect({
apiKey: "mch_live_xxx",
merchantId: "merch_xxx",
serviceName: "My AI API",
baseUrl: "https://api.example.com",
});

sardis.price("/api/generate", "0.05", "Generate text");
sardis.price("/api/analyze", "0.10", "Analyze data");

app.use(sardis.middleware());
/code

p class="text-muted mt-16" style="max-width:720px;"
The middleware hands off to codenext()/code for anything that isn't a Sardis route, so it composes
cleanly with existing Express apps. The manifest declares codeaccepts: ["sardis", "x402", "mpp"]/code
— agents can choose whichever rail they prefer; the server-side flow is identical.

!-- E2E demo walkthrough --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Reference Demo

## The E2E script, step by step.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
codedemos/sardis_connect_e2e_demo.py/code walks the full lifecycle in ~320 lines.
Each step maps to one subsystem.

div class="step-list"
div class="step"
div class="step-num"1

div
div class="step-title"Merchant registration

div class="step-desc"
codeMerchant(name, settlement_preference="stripe_connect", mcc_code="5734")/code. Produces a
codemerchant_id/code and an API key in the codemch_live_…/code namespace. Backed by
codesardis-core/merchant.py/code and the codemerchants/code router.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"2

div
div class="step-title"Merchant onboarding

div class="step-desc"
Hosted Stripe Express flow (KYB + bank details). On return: codecharges_enabled/code,
codepayouts_enabled/code, codeonboarding_state=complete/code. The merchant record is
stamped with codestripe_account_id/code; this is where USD will land.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"3

div
div class="step-title"Agent discovery

div class="step-desc"
Agent does codeGET /.well-known/sardis.json/code. The manifest is built by
codeSardisConnect._get_manifest()/code from the in-process endpoint registry plus
codeaccepts: ["sardis", "x402", "mpp"]/code.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"4

div
div class="step-title"Spending mandate check

div class="step-desc"
Before calling code/sardis/pay/code, the agent's codeSpendingMandate/code is evaluated:
per-tx, daily, monthly caps, codemerchant_scope/code allowlist, codeallowed_rails/code.
Denials include a machine-readable reason code. See Canvas 08 · Policy Engine.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"5

div
div class="step-title"Natural-language policy

div class="step-desc"
The demo parses phrases like "Maximum $50 per day on AI API calls" via
codeRegexPolicyParser/code in codesardis-core/nl_policy_parser.py/code. The parsed
structure becomes a signed mandate.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"6

div
div class="step-title"Multi-protocol execution

div class="step-desc"
Same agent, same mandate, three rails: direct USDC (Base/Tempo), x402
(HTTP 402 on micropayments), and MPP (Stripe session). Connect is rail-agnostic because the
manifest advertises all three and the Sardis orchestrator selects the best fit.

div class="step"
div class="step-num"7

div
div class="step-title"Settlement to USD

div class="step-desc"
Sardis settlement engine fires a transfer to codeacct_…/code; Stripe's
auto-payout pushes USD to the merchant's bank. Reconciliation enqueued in the Postgres
reconciliation queue (see Canvas 10 · Settlement & Chains).

!-- Merchant payout rail --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Settlement Provider

## Why the merchant never touches crypto.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Why a bank-connected merchant account

div class="card-description"
The merchant already trusts Stripe for card payments. Connect's Express account model
amortizes KYB/KYC across their existing compliance stack, so Sardis does not rebuild it.
The tradeoff is Stripe's fee on the USD leg — Sardis eats none of that margin on the crypto
leg (codegasless/code via Circle Paymaster, USDC native).

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Alternative rails

div class="card-description"
EU merchants can opt into Striga (SEPA + vIBANs). Multi-rail merchants can use Bridge
(ACH/SEPA/PIX). Lightspark is wired for Lightning. The codesettlement_preference/code field on
the codeMerchant/code record is the toggle — abstracted behind
codesardis-core/settlement/code.

!-- Pricing / settlement model --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Economics

## Pricing + settlement model.

table class="data"
thead
trthMechanism
thHow it works
thWhen to use

/thead
tbody
trtdFixed price
tdcodesardis.price(path, amount)/code — agent pays the posted price per call.
tdPredictable endpoints: generation, embeddings, search.

trtdMetered (per-unit)
tdcodesardis.meter(path, per_unit, unit)/code — agent calls, merchant reports codeunits/code via code/sardis/usage/code, Sardis computes charge.
tdLLM completions (tokens), vision (pixels), data egress (bytes).

trtdPlatform fee
tdSardis takes a flat percentage on the USDC leg. Stored as codeplatform_fee_amount/code on each session. Breakdown exposed to both sides.
tdHow Sardis monetizes. Transparent on every receipt.

trtdMerchant payout
tdTransfer to merchant bank → auto-payout; configurable schedule (daily/weekly).
tdUS default. Swap to Striga/Bridge for EU/multi-rail.

/tbody
/table

!-- Comparison vs Pay with Sardis --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Two Products, One Rail

## Sardis Connect vs. Pay with Sardis.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Both sit on the same codemerchant-checkout/code session primitive, but the audience and ergonomics
diverge. Don't conflate them in sales conversations.

table class="data"
thead
trth
thSardis Connect
thPay with Sardis (Checkout)

/thead
tbody
trtdWho calls it
tdAn AI agent, programmatically
tdA human buyer, in a browser

trtdUI
tdNone — JSON over HTTP
tdProduct-private hosted checkout client backed by public session APIs

trtdDiscovery
tdcode/.well-known/sardis.json/code on the merchant domain
tdMerchant-issued checkout link / embedded button

trtdPayer source
tdAgent's Sardis wallet + signed mandate
tdExternal wallet (WalletConnect, Coinbase, MetaMask) or Sardis codewal_…/code

trtdAuth
tdMerchant codemch_live_…/code key + session codeclient_secret/code
tdSession codeclient_secret/code; merchant endpoints use coderequire_principal/code

trtdSettlement
tdBank rail → USD
tdSame settlement engine; Stripe/Striga/Bridge

trtdTypical ticket
td$0.001 – $10 per call
td$5 – $500 per order

trtdSDK
tdcodesardis-connect/code (py) + code@sardis/connect/code (js)
tdHosted client package lives in the private product repo

trtdCanvas
tdThis page
tdCanvas 11 · Checkout & Merchants

/tbody
/table

!-- Key files --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Key files.

table class="data"
thead
trthFile
thRole

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect/src/sardis_connect/middleware.py/code
tdThe FastAPI router — manifest, pay, verify, webhook, usage, paywall dependency.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect/src/sardis_connect/models.py/code
tdcodePricedEndpoint/code, codePricingModel/code, codeServiceManifest/code, codeUsageRecord/code.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect/README.md/code
tdThe quickstart; source of the Python example above.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect-js/src/middleware.ts/code
tdNode/Express middleware; same three routes, hand-rolled with codefetch/code to avoid SDK bloat.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-connect-js/src/types.ts/code
tdTS types shared with the Python models by contract.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/merchants.py/code
tdMerchant registration + key issuance. Upstream of every Connect flow.

trtdcodepackages/api/src/sardis_server/routes/commerce/service_directory.py/code
tdRegistry side: where discovered services can be listed globally.

trtdcodedemos/sardis_connect_e2e_demo.py/code
tdThe seven-step reference walkthrough.

/tbody
/table
