=====================================
# OSP INTEGRATION
https://canvas.sardis.sh/osp-integration
=====================================

Canvas title="21 · OSP Integration — Sardis Canvas" currentId="osp-integration"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 21 · Extended/span

## OSP Integration

p class="hero-lead"
Open Service Protocol (OSP) is an open standard — separate from Sardis — for AI agents to
discover, provision, rotate, and deprovision developer services. Sardis is a reference payment rail
inside OSP, declared as codesardis_wallet/code in any provider's codeaccepted_payment_methods/code.
This canvas shows the bridge: what OSP is, why Sardis is one of its rails, and the code that wires them.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
strongOSP is to service provisioning what MCP is to tool access./strong A provider publishes
code/.well-known/osp.json/code; an agent calls codePOST /osp/v1/provision/code; credentials come back
Ed25519-encrypted. OSP is payment-rail agnostic — Sardis plugs in via
code@sardis/osp-integration/code, providing the wallet, escrow, ledger, and an MCP server that exposes
nine OSP tools to Claude / Cursor / other MCP clients.

!-- Why OSP exists --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Motivation

## Why OSP exists.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Every managed service today has its own proprietary onboarding flow. Agents can't fill signup forms,
can't solve CAPTCHAs, can't verify emails. The alternatives are invite-only ecosystems
(Stripe Projects, March 2026) or custom-per-provider glue. OSP is the open-standard third path.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Problem 1

div class="pillar-title"Manual signup is the wrong primitive

div class="pillar-desc"
Browsers, CAPTCHAs, email verification — interaction patterns agents cannot automate without
brittle scraping. Every new service is bespoke integration work.

div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Problem 2

div class="pillar-title"Platform lock-in beats open coordination

div class="pillar-desc"
Stripe Projects is invite-only, Stripe-rail-only. OSP is Apache 2.0; any provider can implement it,
any agent can consume it, any rail can settle it.

div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Problem 3

div class="pillar-title"Reusable credentials don't fit agent threat models

div class="pillar-desc"
OSP's core thesis: "Reusable credentials are the wrong primitive. Every service interaction
should be a first-class protocol operation — discoverable, provisionable, rotatable, auditable."

div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Problem 4

div class="pillar-title"No standard place to settle a bill

div class="pillar-desc"
Agents can reason about a service spec but have no universal way to pay for it. OSP declares
codeaccepted_payment_methods/code; Sardis is one of them alongside x402, MPP, Stripe SPT, free.

!-- OSP flow --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Protocol Flow

## Discover → provision → rotate → deprovision.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Four operations define the OSP lifecycle. Every provider exposes them under the same URL scheme;
every agent speaks the same JSON. Spec lives at code~/osp/spec/osp-v1.0.md/code (v1.1 draft).

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent
participant P as Provider (e.g. Supabase)
participant S as Sardis (payment rail)

A-P: GET /.well-known/osp.json
P--A: manifest { offerings, tiers, accepted_payment_methods }
A-A: Pick offering + tier + payment_method="sardis_wallet"

Note over A,S: Escrow leg (optional but recommended)
A-S: EscrowManager.hold(amount, release_condition)
S--A: escrow_id + signed proof

A-P: POST /osp/v1/provision { offering_id, tier_id, agent_public_key, payment_proof: escrow_id }
P-S: verify escrow_id
S--P: held=true, amount ok
P-P: allocate resource_id
P--A: { resource_id, credentials (Ed25519-encrypted) }

Note over A,S: Settlement leg
P-S: release(escrow_id) on successful provision
S-S: ledger append (credit provider, debit agent mandate)

opt Rotation
A-P: POST /osp/v1/rotate/{resource_id}
P--A: new encrypted credentials
end

opt Deprovision
A-P: POST /osp/v1/deprovision/{resource_id}
P--A: receipt
end

[/diagram]

!-- Where Sardis fits --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The Bridge

## Where Sardis fits in an OSP flow.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
OSP's codeprovision-request.schema.json/code enumerates seven payment methods:
codefree/code, codesardis_wallet/code, codestripe_spt/code, codex402/code,
codempp/code, codeinvoice/code, codeexternal/code. Sardis is the first-class crypto-native rail.

div class="code-block"
precode// Provider manifest — /.well-known/osp.json
{
"provider_id": "supabase.com",
"offerings": [{
"offering_id": "supabase/postgres",
"tiers": [
{ "tier_id": "free", "price": { "amount": "0", "currency": "USD" } },
{ "tier_id": "pro", "price": { "amount": "25", "currency": "USD", "interval": "P1M" } }
]
}],
"accepted_payment_methods": ["free", "sardis_wallet", "stripe_spt", "x402"]
}
/code

p class="text-muted mt-16" style="max-width:720px;"
When the agent sends codepayment_method: "sardis_wallet"/code, the codepayment_proof/code is a Sardis
escrow ID or charge intent. The provider verifies it out-of-band with the Sardis API
(code/api/v2/escrow/{id}/code) before allocating the resource. On successful provisioning the escrow
releases; on failure the hold is refunded.

!-- Sardis integration folder --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"The Package

## code@sardis/osp-integration/code — three modules.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Lives at code~/osp/sardis-integration//code (peer to the OSP core, shipped as its own npm
package). Three modules, each a clean seam.

div class="grid grid-3"
div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Module · payment/

div class="pillar-title"Wallet · escrow · ledger · charge

div class="pillar-desc"
codeSardisWalletClient/code, codeEscrowManager/code, codeLedger/code, and the typed
shapes for codeSpendingMandate/code, codeEscrowHold/code, codeChargeIntent/code,
codeLedgerEntry/code. The minimum surface a provider needs to accept codesardis_wallet/code.

div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Module · mcp/

div class="pillar-title"Nine OSP tools for MCP clients

div class="pillar-desc"
codesardis-mcp-server.ts/code exposes: codediscover_services/code,
codeprovision_service/code, codedeprovision/code, coderotate_credentials/code,
codecheck_status/code, codeget_usage/code, codeestimate_cost/code,
codelist_skills/code, codeenv_generate/code. Makes Claude/Cursor first-class OSP consumers.

div class="pillar"
div class="pillar-label"Module · cli/

div class="pillar-title"Sardis CLI extension

div class="pillar-desc"
codecommands.ts/code + codebridge.ts/code. Adds codesardis osp discover/code,
codesardis osp provision/code, etc. — same bridge as the MCP server, command-line shape. The
payment layer is shared.

!-- Ed25519 --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Shared Cryptography

## Ed25519 everywhere.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Both OSP and Sardis settled on Ed25519 for identity + envelope encryption. This is not coincidence —
it means a single agent keypair can simultaneously sign a Sardis mandate, prove identity to an OSP
provider, and decrypt the returned credential bundle.

table class="data"
thead
trthUse case
thPrimitive
thWhere it lives

/thead
tbody
trtdAgent identity (KYA/TAP)
tdEd25519 signature over canonical JSON
tdSardis codesardis-protocol/code (TAP verifier)

trtdSpending mandate signature
tdEd25519 signature over mandate hash
tdSardis codespending_mandate.py/code

trtdOSP provision request signature
tdEd25519 signature over canonical request
tdOSP codeosp-crypto/code crate

trtdOSP credential envelope
tdx25519 key agreement → XChaCha20-Poly1305
tdOSP codecredential-bundle.schema.json/code

trtdOn-chain ledger anchor
tdSecp256k1 tx sig, Merkle root payload
tdSardis codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code

/tbody
/table

!-- Portfolio framing --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Strategic Framing

## Two open standards, one author, one platform thesis.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Sardis and OSP are deliberately separate projects. Sardis is a product (fintech rail, VC-fundable).
OSP is a standard (Apache 2.0, zero revenue target). They reinforce each other because every OSP
provider that accepts codesardis_wallet/code puts Sardis in front of another agent cohort — and
every Sardis agent that wants to provision a DB, a logger, a feature flag service gets a clean OSP
path to do so.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Sardis — the product layer

div class="card-description"
Non-custodial wallet, MPC signing, spending mandates, KYC/AML, on-chain anchor, 15+ framework
SDKs. Revenue = platform fee + enterprise compliance tier. Live on Base + Tempo mainnet.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"OSP — the standards layer

div class="card-description"
Protocol spec (9,600+ lines, v1.1 draft). Apache 2.0. SDKs in Rust, TypeScript, Python, Go.
Rebranded public umbrella: strongPact/strong. Maximum adoption, zero revenue ambition — the
moat is in being the default spec that Sardis (and others) implement.

!-- Current status --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Status · April 2026

## Where each side actually is.

table class="data"
thead
trthSurface
thState
thLocation

/thead
tbody
trtdOSP spec
tdv1.1 draft · ~9,600 lines · active development
tdcode~/osp/spec/osp-v1.0.md/code

trtdOSP JSON schemas
td10 schemas (draft 2020-12) + 14 example manifests
tdcode~/osp/schemas//code

trtdOSP core (Rust)
tdCrates for crypto, manifest, vault, CLI (18 cmds), registry, provider, conformance, SDK
tdcode~/osp/osp-core/crates//code

trtdTypeScript SDK
tdv0.2.0 · 139 tests
tdcode~/osp/reference-implementation/typescript//code

trtdPython SDK
tdv0.2.0 · 171 tests
tdcode~/osp/reference-implementation/python//code

trtdGo SDK
tdActive
tdcode~/osp/osp-sdk-go//code

trtdSardis ↔ OSP bridge
tdcode@sardis/osp-integration/code — payment + MCP + CLI modules
tdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src//code

trtdSardis MPC-server-for-OSP
td9 tools · MCP-compatible (Claude Desktop, Cursor)
tdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src/mcp/sardis-mcp-server.ts/code

trtdWebsite
tdLive — a href="https://osp.sh" target="_blank" rel="noopener"osp.sh/a
tdcode~/osp/website//code

/tbody
/table

!-- Key files --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Key files.

table class="data"
thead
trthFile
thWhat's in it

/thead
tbody
trtdcode~/osp/README.md/code
tdPitch, protocol summary, v1.1 feature list, repo layout.

trtdcode~/osp/spec/osp-v1.0.md/code
tdFull normative spec (v1.1 draft).

trtdcode~/osp/schemas/service-manifest.schema.json/code
tdThe code/.well-known/osp.json/code shape.

trtdcode~/osp/schemas/provision-request.schema.json/code
tdDefines codepayment_method/code enum including codesardis_wallet/code.

trtdcode~/osp/schemas/credential-bundle.schema.json/code
tdEd25519-encrypted credential envelope format.

trtdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src/index.ts/code
tdBarrel export: payment + mcp + cli modules.

trtdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src/payment/index.ts/code
tdcodeSardisWalletClient/code, codeEscrowManager/code, codeLedger/code.

trtdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src/payment/types.ts/code
tdcodeSpendingMandate/code, codeEscrowHold/code, codeChargeIntent/code, codeLedgerEntry/code.

trtdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src/mcp/sardis-mcp-server.ts/code
tdNine MCP tools: discover / provision / deprovision / rotate / check / usage / estimate / skills / env.

trtdcode~/osp/sardis-integration/src/cli/commands.ts/code
tdSardis CLI subcommands for OSP lifecycle.

trtdcode~/osp/reference-implementation//code
tdTS + Python reference SDKs (v0.2.0).

/tbody
/table

!-- Cross-refs --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Go deeper

## Follow-ups.

div class="grid grid-2"
a class="canvas-card" href="/sardis-connect"
div class="canvas-card-num"20 · Extended

div class="canvas-card-title"Sardis Connect

div class="canvas-card-desc"The inverse wedge: turn any HTTP API into a codesardis_wallet/code-accepting endpoint in three lines.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/protocols"
div class="canvas-card-num"13 · Product

div class="canvas-card-title"Protocol Gateway

div class="canvas-card-desc"AP2, TAP, x402, A2A — the other protocols Sardis verifies. OSP sits alongside, focused on provisioning rather than payment.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/policy-engine"
div class="canvas-card-num"08 · Flows

div class="canvas-card-title"Policy Engine

div class="canvas-card-desc"The mandate engine that backs every codesardis_wallet/code payment_proof in an OSP provision request.

/a
a class="canvas-card" href="/sdks"
div class="canvas-card-num"14 · Product

div class="canvas-card-title"SDKs & CLIs

div class="canvas-card-desc"Where codesardis-mcp-server/code lives on the Sardis side — the OSP MCP bridge reuses the same transport.

/a

/Canvas


=====================================
# ZK PRIVACY
https://canvas.sardis.sh/zk-privacy
=====================================

Canvas title="22 · ZK Privacy Stack — Sardis Canvas" currentId="zk-privacy"
Fragment slot="head"
style is:global set:html={styles}/style
/Fragment

section class="hero"
span class="eyebrow"Canvas 22 · Extended/span

## ZK Privacy Stack

p class="hero-lead"
Prove a payment satisfied its policy, mandate, funding, and identity requirements —
without revealing the amounts, the limits, the merchant, or the agent. Sardis ships two
Noir circuit packages (codesardis-zk-policy/code, codesardis-zkp/code), a deterministic
Python simulator for CI, and three adjacent runtime layers — codesardis-ucp/code,
codesardis-guardrails/code, and the codePreExecutionPipeline/code — that decide when
a clear-text decision needs to turn into a proof.

section class="section" style="padding-top:0; border-top:0;"
div class="callout callout-info"
span class="callout-title"TL;DR/span
Enterprises can't publish raw spending logs to auditors — the logs are the product.
ZK lets the same row say "this payment was within all 11 policy rules" without showing
per-transaction amounts, merchant identities, or the limits that define the rules.
Sardis uses strongNoir + Barretenberg/strong (Aztec) for the circuits, because the
toolchain is audited, the proving backend is fast enough for per-transaction use, and
verification is cheap enough to run on-chain via codeSardisLedgerAnchor/code.

!-- Status --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Status

## What ships vs. what's experimental.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
The ZK stack is real code — two Noir projects, working circuits, a Python simulator used in CI.
It is not on the default hot path. Today it runs as an emalternative/em policy check for
tenants that explicitly opt in; the clear-text codePreExecutionPipeline/code remains the
production default.

table class="data"
thead
trthComponent
thMaturity
thDeployed
thGate

/thead
tbody
trtdcodesardis-zk-policy/code (Noir circuit + simulator)
tdFeature-complete for 5 / 11 policy checks
tdNo (opt-in)
tdRequires codenargo/code installed in the runtime image.

trtdcodesardis-zkp/code (mandate / identity / funding circuits)
tdCircuits compile, dev-mode mock proofs only
tdNo
tdcodeproduction=True/code on codeZKProver/code — refuses to mock-verify otherwise.

trtdcodesardis-ucp/code (Universal Commerce Protocol)
tdShipped
tdYes
tdPublic surface; feeds checkout + AP2 adapters.

trtdcodesardis-guardrails/code (circuit breaker, kill switch, limiter, ML fraud)
tdShipped
tdYes
tdAlways on. Not ZK — classical runtime safety layer.

/tbody
/table

!-- Why Noir --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Backend Choice

## Why Noir (Aztec) — and not Circom / Halo2 / RISC0.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Rust-like syntax, small surface

div class="card-description"
Circuit authors are Python and Rust engineers, not cryptographers. Noir reads like Rust —
codeassert/code, codefor/code, codeif/code, codeu64/code — which keeps the
policy circuit reviewable alongside the clear-text Python implementation in
codepolicy_circuit_sim.py/code.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"ACIR → ACVM → Barretenberg

div class="card-description"
Noir compiles to strongACIR/strong (intermediate representation), executed by the
strongACVM/strong witness solver, and proven by strongBarretenberg/strong (UltraPlonk
+ Honk). That layering means we can swap proving backends later without rewriting circuits.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Audited toolchain

div class="card-description"
Aztec audits its compiler and prover. The alternative — hand-rolled Circom — requires writing
templates + solvers + verifiers that all need independent review. For a fintech, using an
audited toolchain is cheaper than auditing custom R1CS.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Cheap on-chain verification

div class="card-description"
Barretenberg emits Solidity verifier contracts. One ZK policy proof verifies in ~300K gas on
Base L2 — low enough to anchor alongside the Merkle batch from codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code
without blowing the per-batch cost model.

!-- sardis-zk-policy --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Package · sardis-zk-policy

## Policy circuit — 5 of 11 checks in zero knowledge.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
A single Noir binary at codepackages/sardis-zk-policy/src/main.nr/code. Private inputs
carry the transaction + policy parameters; a single public input pair
(codeevidence_hash_high/code, codeevidence_hash_low/code) links the proof to the
off-chain policy decision log.

div class="circuit-card"
div class="ct-title"main.nr — policy compliance proof

div class="ct-path"packages/sardis-zk-policy/src/main.nr · Nargo.toml · Prover.toml · Verifier.toml

div class="ct-desc"
Proves all five checks in one circuit: per-tx cap, daily cap, MCC block-list membership,
time-window (including wrap-around), and per-merchant cap. Asserts codeevidence_hash/code
is non-zero so the off-chain row is addressable.

div class="io-grid"
div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Private inputs (witness)

div class="io-list"
amount: u64

daily_total: u64

merchant_total: u64

mcc_hash: u64

hour: u8

max_per_transaction: u64

daily_limit: u64

per_merchant_limit: u64

window_start: u8

window_end: u8

blocked_mcc_hashes: [u64; 8]

div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Public inputs

div class="io-list"
evidence_hash_high: pub u128

evidence_hash_low: pub u128

div class="circuit-card"
div class="ct-title"policy_circuit_sim.py — bit-exact Python mirror

div class="ct-path"packages/sardis-zk-policy/python/policy_circuit_sim.py · types.py

div class="ct-desc"
A Python function that runs the same five checks in the same order, producing a
codeCircuitResult(passed, failed_check)/code. Used in CI for fast regression tests; the
companion suite codetests/test_noir_equivalence.py/code is designed to pin Noir output
to the Python output once codenargo prove/code is wired into CI.

div class="io-grid"
div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"API

div class="io-list"
simulate_circuit(private, public)

prepare_circuit_inputs(...)

mcc_to_hash(mcc_code)

evidence_hash_to_pair(hex)

div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Guarantees

div class="io-list"
Same assert order as main.nr

Same wrap-around time logic

Same MCC zero-slot handling

Deterministic, no I/O

!-- sardis-zkp --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Package · sardis-zkp

## Reusable circuit library — mandate, identity, funding.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
Three Noir circuits under codepackages/sardis-zkp/circuits//code, orchestrated by a Python
codeZKProver/code in codesrc/lib.py/code. Each circuit targets one question; together
they cover the questions Sardis actually needs to answer in ZK at the mandate layer.

div class="circuit-card"
div class="ct-title"mandate_compliance.nr — payment within mandate bounds

div class="ct-path"packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/mandate_compliance.nr

div class="ct-desc"
Proves codeamount ≤ per_tx_limit/code AND codedaily_spent + amount ≤ daily_limit/code,
and that the private inputs hash to the public codemandate_commitment/code and
codepayment_commitment/code (both Poseidon on BN254). Merchant inclusion via 8-deep
Merkle proof; replay protection via codemandate_nonce/code.

div class="io-grid"
div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Public

div class="io-list"
mandate_commitment: Field

payment_commitment: Field

div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Private

div class="io-list"
amount, per_tx_limit,

daily_limit, daily_spent,

merchant_id_hash,

allowed_merchant_root,

merchant_proof[8],

mandate_nonce

div class="circuit-card"
div class="ct-title"identity_proof.nr — KYA level without agent identity

div class="ct-path"packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/identity_proof.nr

div class="ct-desc"
Proves the agent holds an attestation whose codekya_level ≥ required_kya_level/code,
unexpired, and not future-dated. Public side sees only the commitment + the threshold;
the agent's id hash and the issuer remain private.

div class="io-grid"
div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Public

div class="io-list"
identity_commitment: Field

required_kya_level: Field

div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Private

div class="io-list"
agent_id_hash, kya_level,

attestation_timestamp,

attestation_expiry,

current_timestamp,

issuer_id_hash,

attestation_nonce

div class="circuit-card"
div class="ct-title"funding_sufficiency.nr — cells cover amount

div class="ct-path"packages/sardis-zkp/circuits/funding_sufficiency.nr

div class="ct-desc"
Proves codesum(cell_values[0..16]) ≥ payment_amount/code without revealing any individual
cell value. Each cell is bounded to 2sup53/sup to prevent field-overflow attacks.
Commitments are Poseidon batches so the off-chain funding-cell set can be reconciled.

div class="io-grid"
div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Public

div class="io-list"
payment_amount: Field

cells_commitment: Field

div class="io-block"
div class="io-label"Private

div class="io-list"
cell_values[16], cell_ids[16],

cell_count, currency_hash,

nonce

!-- Privacy tiers --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Privacy Tiers

## Transparent, hybrid, full ZK.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
Defined in codesardis_zkp.PrivacyTier/code. Tenants select a tier per payment object
(mandate, payment, wallet) — the orchestrator routes to the corresponding verifier path.

table class="data"
thead
trthTier
thWhat's visible
thWhat's hidden
thWhen to use

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeTRANSPARENT/code
tdAll fields (amount, merchant, limits, counterparty)
tdNothing
tdDefault. Consumer agents, developer sandboxes, public tokens.

trtdcodeHYBRID/code
tdMerchant, chain, timestamp, decision outcome
tdAmount, limits
tdB2B payments where merchant identity is non-sensitive but commercial terms are.

trtdcodeFULL_ZK/code
tdProof only — commitments + verification result
tdAmount, merchant, limits, agent identity, funding cells
tdEnterprise treasury flows, regulated payouts, custodial agent fleets.

/tbody
/table

!-- Which policies go ZK --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Coverage Matrix

## Which policy checks translate to ZK — and which don't.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
The codeSpendingPolicy/code engine has ~11 check types. Five are implemented in
codemain.nr/code today. The rest either need oracle data, a running graph, or third-party
attestations that can't be proven in-circuit without leaking the external state.

table class="data"
thead
trthPolicy check
thZK today
thZK feasible
thWhy / why not

/thead
tbody
trtdPer-transaction cap
tdYes
td—
tdPure numeric comparison.

trtdDaily aggregate cap
tdYes
td—
tdRunning total carried as private input.

trtdPer-merchant cap
tdYes
td—
tdKeyed by codemerchant_id_hash/code; same shape as daily.

trtdMCC block-list
tdYes (8 slots)
td—
tdSet-membership via linear scan; extend to Merkle for larger lists.

trtdTime window (with wrap)
tdYes
td—
tdHour as u8; handled in both linear and wrap case.

trtdAllowed-merchant Merkle root
tdPartial (in codesardis-zkp/code mandate)
tdYes
tdNeed to wire merchant registry → Merkle root into prover input.

trtdKYC/AML status
tdNo
tdYes, via attested credential
tdRequires a KYC-provider-signed attestation usable inside circuit.

trtdSanctions / OFAC screen
tdNo
tdPartial
tdElliptic API is external; proof would cover attestation, not live screen.

trtdAnomaly score threshold
tdNo
tdPartial
tdScore is an ML output; circuit could assert a signed score, not compute it.

trtdRate limit (token bucket)
tdNo
tdYes, with state commitment
tdNeeds a commitment to bucket state; opens replay questions.

trtdKill switch / emergency stop
tdNo
tdNo
tdKill switch is operator intent, not a math fact. Stays clear-text.

/tbody
/table

!-- Prover flow Mermaid --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Flow

## Prover → verifier, end to end.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
A ZK-tier payment takes the same orchestrator spine as a transparent one — it just forks at
the policy check into a proof-generation path instead of a direct assert.

div class="diagram"

[diagram]

sequenceDiagram
participant A as Agent
participant O as PaymentOrchestrator
participant P as PreExecutionPipeline
participant ZP as ZKProver (sardis-zkp)
participant N as Noir / nargo
participant L as Ledger + Anchor
participant V as On-chain Verifier

A-O: execute(intent, privacy_tier=FULL_ZK)
O-P: run classical gates (rate limit, kill switch)
P-ZP: prove_mandate_compliance(amount, limits)
ZP-N: write Prover.toml + nargo prove
N--ZP: proof bytes + public inputs
ZP-P: ZKProof(commitments, bytes)
P-P: hash evidence row → evidence_hash
P-ZP: prove_policy(evidence_hash, witness)
ZP--P: policy proof bytes
P--O: approved (ZK)
O-L: append row + attach proofs
L-V: submit Merkle root + ZK proof batch
V--L: on-chain receipt

[/diagram]

!-- Example --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Worked Example

## "Prove spending was under $500 today — without per-tx amounts."

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:720px;"
Concrete path through codesardis-zk-policy/code for a single transaction. All amounts
scaled to integer minor-units (1 USDC = 1,000,000).

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"Prover side (private)

dl class="defs"
dtamount
dd47_500000 (47.50 USDC)

dtdaily_total
dd392_000000 (already spent today)

dtmax_per_transaction
dd250_000000

dtdaily_limit
dd500_000000

dthour / window
dd14 / [09, 18]

dtmcc_hash
ddcodesha256("5812")[:8]/code

dtblocked_mcc_hashes
dd[gambling, crypto_mx, ...]

/dl

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Public (what verifiers see)

dl class="defs"
dtevidence_hash
ddcodesha256(decision_row)/code

dt→ high / low
ddtwo u128 values

dtVerifier checks
ddBarretenberg verifier accepts the proof against the compiled circuit and the public inputs.

dtAuditor sees
dd"Policy row codepol_0x…/code passed all 5 in-circuit checks." No amounts. No merchant. No limits.

/dl

!-- Adjacent: UCP --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Adjacent · sardis-ucp

## Universal Commerce Protocol — the object model ZK wraps.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
UCP is not ZK, but it defines the commerce primitives (codeUCPCartMandate/code,
codeUCPPaymentMandate/code, codeUCPOrder/code, codeUCPCheckoutCapability/code) that
ZK proofs reference as commitment inputs. When codemandate_compliance.nr/code takes a
codepayment_commitment/code, the preimage is a UCP mandate object.

table class="data"
thead
trthUCP primitive
thFile
thZK touch point

/thead
tbody
trtdcodeUCPPaymentMandate/code / codeUCPCartMandate/code
tdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/models/mandates.py/code
tdCommitment preimage for codemandate_compliance/code.

trtdcodeUCPCheckoutCapability/code
tdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/capabilities/checkout.py/code
tdSession lifecycle — where privacy tier is chosen.

trtdcodeUCPOrder/code / codeUCPFulfillment/code
tdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/models/orders.py/code
tdPost-settlement state; fulfillment events anchor next to ZK receipts.

trtdcodeap2.py/code adapter
tdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/adapters/ap2.py/code
tdTranslates AP2 Intent → Cart → Payment into UCP, so AP2 flows can opt into ZK.

trtdTransports (REST, MCP)
tdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/transports//code
tdDeclare privacy tier on the wire.

/tbody
/table

!-- Adjacent: Guardrails --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Adjacent · sardis-guardrails

## Classical runtime safety — always in front of the proof.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
Guardrails are not ZK; they run first. A proof that a payment satisfied a policy is worthless
if the agent is on the kill list or the circuit breaker is tripped. Guardrails answer
"should we even be computing a proof right now?"

table class="data"
thead
trthModule
thRole
thWhen it fires

/thead
tbody
trtdcodecircuit_breaker.py/code
tdAutomatic failure isolation per agent
tdN consecutive failures → reject without prover work.

trtdcodekill_switch.py/code
tdGlobal / per-org / per-agent hard stop
tdOperator or codeFRAUD/code/codeANOMALY/code auto-trip.

trtdcoderate_limiter.py/code
tdToken-bucket + sliding window
tdToo many tx or too much amount per minute/hour.

trtdcodeinput_validator.py/code
tdAddress / amount / chain / token validation
tdBefore any downstream work, including proving.

trtdcodebehavioral_monitor.py/code + codeanomaly_engine.py/code + codeml_fraud.py/code
tdStatistical / ML anomaly scoring
tdGenerates signed score that a ZK circuit can consume.

trtdcodegraph_fraud.py/code
tdGraph-level counterparty analysis
tdRuns over counterparty network; feeds FIDES trust graph.

trtdcodeagent_threat_detector.py/code · codefingerprint.py/code · codeft3_taxonomy.py/code
tdAgent-level threat classification
tdFeeds KYA attestations used by codeidentity_proof.nr/code.

trtdcodezen_engine.py/code
tdRules DSL executor
tdDeclarative merchant / policy rules applied pre-orchestrator.

/tbody
/table

!-- Integration --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Integration

## Where ZK hooks into the spine.

p class="text-muted" style="max-width:740px;"
The codePreExecutionPipeline/code runs all transactions through the same ordered gate
(see canvas 01 · Architecture). The ZK path is a fork eminside/em the policy gate, not a
parallel pipeline — everything else (mandate check, AP2 verify, rate limit, kill switch) is
shared with the transparent path.

div class="grid grid-2"
div class="card"
div class="card-title"What the orchestrator does differently

div class="card-description"
On codeprivacy_tier=FULL_ZK/code, the policy check returns a codeZKProof/code instead
of a boolean. The orchestrator attaches the proof to the ledger row, and the Merkle batch
submission includes proof bytes for on-chain verification.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"What stays the same

div class="card-description"
Kill switch, rate limit, input validation, MPC signing path, CCTP settlement, reconciliation
queue. ZK changes what is emseen/em; it doesn't change what is emdone/em.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"Dev vs. prod mode

div class="card-description"
codeZKProver(production=False)/code emits JSON placeholder proof bytes and emrefuses/em
to verify. codeproduction=True/code shells out to codenargo prove/code /
codenargo verify/code in the circuit directory. Mock verify was removed on purpose to stop
anyone from mistaking dev mode for security.

div class="card"
div class="card-title"On-chain anchor

div class="card-description"
Verifier contracts generated by Barretenberg are deployed alongside
codeSardisLedgerAnchor.sol/code (canvas 06). A batch root + proof tuple posted there is
independently verifiable by any third party without reading Sardis logs.

!-- Key files --
section class="section"
div class="section-header"
div class="eyebrow"Source of Truth

## Key files.

table class="data"
thead
trthFile
thPurpose

/thead
tbody
trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/src/main.nr/code
tdThe 5-check policy circuit. Binary target (codetype = "bin"/code in codeNargo.toml/code).

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/Prover.toml/code
tdTemplate for prover-side inputs; populated at runtime.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/Verifier.toml/code
tdPublic inputs shape — codeevidence_hash_high/code, codeevidence_hash_low/code.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/python/policy_circuit_sim.py/code
tdPython mirror of the circuit. CI uses this for regression.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/python/types.py/code
tdcodePolicyCircuitInputs/code, codePolicyCircuitPublicInputs/code, hash helpers.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/tests/test_circuit_sim.py/code
tdCorrectness tests for the Python simulator.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zk-policy/tests/test_noir_equivalence.py/code
tdPin Noir output to Python simulator output (when codenargo/code is on CI).

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zkp/circuits/mandate_compliance.nr/code
tdMandate per-tx + daily bounds + commitment check.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zkp/circuits/identity_proof.nr/code
tdKYA-level proof without revealing agent id.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zkp/circuits/funding_sufficiency.nr/code
tdSum of 16 cells ≥ payment amount.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-zkp/src/lib.py/code
tdcodeZKProver/code + codePrivacyTier/code + codeCircuitType/code + dev-mode guard.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/models/mandates.py/code
tdUCP mandate objects — commitment preimages.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-ucp/src/sardis_ucp/adapters/ap2.py/code
tdAP2 ↔ UCP bridge.

trtdcodepackages/sardis-guardrails/src/sardis_guardrails/*.py/code
td15 runtime-safety modules that gate emwhether/em a proof is worth computing.

/tbody
/table
